aiReviewsSummary.text: Customers report mixed experiences with the fruit fly culture's lifespan, with some saying it lasts a long time while others received dead flies. The flies' activity level and flight ability are also mixed, with some customers reporting good results while others note minimal activity and that the flies aren't truly flightless. Moreover, the container size receives negative feedback, with customers mentioning there's barely any medium in the container. Additionally, customers find the product not worth the money.

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: They may not fly, but they fly!
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: These flies are very active. The mantis' really like them, but they jump far and fast, so they are hard to catch and contain. I put them in the freezer for about 3 minutes before I give them to the mantis'. That works pretty well. Also, there is a ton of larva in the base material, so I fed some of those to a sick mantis. That worked pretty well until he could eat the flies again.SECOND BATCH: Excellent condition, nice and fat flies for my growing mantis.

title: RESCUE! Fruit Fly Trap – Reusable, Includes Liquid Bait Attractant
inStockText: Only 4 left in stock - order soon.
breadCrumbs: Patio, Lawn & Garden > Pest Control > Traps
features: Fast Acting - This discreet indoor trap uses a food-based lure that attracts, catches, and eliminates fruit flies that congregate and breed near fruit, vegetables, and food scraps.
Long Lasting - Includes two 15-day supplies of food-based fruit fly attractant that is safe to use around food. Fruit flies find the color and scent of our attractant irresistible!

Easy to Use – No mixing or home remedies required. Simply add the liquid attractant to the trap and it starts catching fruit flies within a day! A smart No-Tip design prevents spills in your kitchen or food storage areas.
Reusable Trap - Once caught, fruit flies drown in the liquid attractant. When the trap is full, it can be emptied, cleaned, and stored for future use. Attractant refills are available to purchase.
Made in the USA – At RESCUE!, our goal is to design and manufacture the safest and most effective pest control solutions available. We proudly manufacture our products in the USA!

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: These are absolutely the best fruit fly traps that I have found.  During the summer, we have tons of produce and always end up with  fruit flies.  I bought this trap and within a few hours, there were at least 20 flies in there.  Every few days I empty it and refill with either the included attractant or apple cider vinegar.  Both work well to attract them.  I also add a drop of Dawn to help break the surface tension, otherwise sometimes they will float around for a long time. However, they don't escape! It's great because it does not tip over and it's super easy to open, rinse, and refill.  We have 3 of them now and they have really helped control the fruit flies in our kitchen during harvest time !
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on July 17, 2021

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I am writing because this is just too exciting. It works great! When I opened it after a few days I saw one little black spot...I was disappointed. Then, I looked again, a little closer...and I saw that, in fact all the little flies were at at bottom of the container, hard to see....maybe 40-50 of them! No wonder I did not see them around the kitchen anymore! Had I devised my own, cheap method? maybe, as this is simply a piece of plastic and some vinegar, worth pennies. Even an open container with apple vinegar will do, I am sure, or anything that is fermenting, like juice or wine. But this one I bought and it works :-) so I will keep it :-)
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on September 24, 2023

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: The issue I have with these is 1st off the smell is awful, be sure not to spill a drop when poring because it’s hard to make it go away. Also the container does not secure, you are simply resting the top on the bottom full if liquid. If you knock into it, it will topple & spill. Not great for a house with kids/pets.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on September 21, 2023

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: Worked well for me with Apple Cider Vinegar
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I was not sure if this would work and used apple cider vinegar in the cup instead of the provided solution. It took a while (3-4 days) before it started to catch the fruit flies. Once it started, it caught a lot of flies and I am pleased with its performance. It looks much better than a cup with cling wrap and punched holes in it.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on October 19, 2024

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: First, I can only report on my own personal experience here. Second, I feel i was 'fooled' by all the "positive" reviews & ordered it. Got this today and put it right to use with High Hopes. While easy to set up, unfortunately it's basically useless. The fruit flies fly all around it but very few actually go into it. After several hours only 3-4 were actually in there. Meanwhile I killed nearly 50+ with a spray bottle filled with dish soap & water. (It kills them almost instantaneously). Also you can make your own "trap" for Free. Just take a small jar, put it some fruit scraps (& or honey) mixed with some dish soap, cover with wax paper (or saran wrap) secured with a rubber band & punch several holes in the wax paper to give the flies access. Presto! A trap that will work just as well for FREE. I'll be asking Amazon for a refund on this expensive useless trap. Really wished it had worked as once infected with  these guys it's really hard to eliminate them completely.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on September 21, 2022

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: Used old 16oz bottles and apple cider vinegar, home made traps were more effective.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on September 9, 2025

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: Bought a different brand, the cute red apple one, that I found difficult to empty and refill.  When time came to try something else, I tried these.  They are not only attractive, but the blend that is used to attract the fruit flies is the real deal.  I bought 2 more  to set up in another part of the house.  Its 'attractive to look at, infinitely easier to clean and refill than other products.  Very happy with my purchase

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: Highly Effective and Safe Fruit Fly Solution
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I purchased this Fruit Fly Trap Refill Liquid (4 Pack) because I was tired of dealing with fruit flies around my kitchen, especially near the sink and trash can. I was honestly impressed by how well it works.From the first day, I noticed that it attracts all types of fruit flies and gnats, not just a few. The fruit-scented bait draws them in quickly, and once they enter the trap, they can’t escape. It works especially well when placed near breeding areas, making it a reliable and continuous solution instead of just a temporary fix.One of the things I appreciate most is that it’s safe to use near food, kids, and pets. There are no harsh chemicals or strong odors, which is very important to me when using something in the kitchen or bathroom. I’ve also used it near indoor plants with no issues at all.Another plus is its wide compatibility. You don’t need a special trap—just pour it into a small bowl, ramekin, or open container, and it works perfectly. It’s truly a pour-and-go solution with no mess or cleanup.The value is excellent. Each bottle lasts up to 8 weeks, so the 4-pack provides long-lasting protection. I’ve used it in the kitchen, bathroom, and near trash areas, and it performs consistently in every space.Overall, this product is effective, non-toxic, easy to use, and a great value. If you’re struggling with fruit flies indoors and want a safe solution that actually works, I highly recommend it. I would definitely buy it again.

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: Powerful stuff that controls fly issues.
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: This stuff works! It attracts flys immediately upon setting into appropriate fly trap jars. I’ve purchased it a few times already and will keep it on hand. productPageReviews.reviewTitle: Effective, Efficient, and Easy to use
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: Effective, that's the only word I can think of when it comes to this product.It's an easy-to-use product; there's not allot of difficulty on how to use this product.  Dump into the correct container, add water, check day to day and add water/empty as neeed.The durability of the product is decent, every time I think it's wearing out, I just added water, and it was back to working.The ability to simply dump the product out and refill is a plus; there's no need to be thorough on cleansing the container this goes into.Size; I found that using only half the bag goes a long way - using to fill two containers versus just one as the directions state.The con's:The smell is bad, and I found that both in powder form and liquid it's unpleasant.  As a powder, it has a strong mothball like smell that stains the skin with the odor as such.  Once wet, the smell is putrid - somewhere along the lines of an outhouse meets raw decomposing matter.  Although this has it's benefits, the importance of keeping this away from your windows, patio, or anywhere you want to sit is highly recommended.Disposing of the remains is somewhat important, if the vector load is low (few flies) I'll allow the water to dry/evaporate out, if not, I wait for the trash truck to come and throw it in the trash right before it is picked up.Ultimately this product is very effective in attracting flies, and as such I will continue using.

title: Flightless (Drosophila hydei) Fruit Fly Culture - *Newly inoculated*
inStockText: Only 6 left in stock - order soon.
breadCrumbs: Pet Supplies > Reptiles & Amphibians > Food

items.title: Indications
items.text: Reptile feeder insects, Amphibian feeder insects, Fish feeder insects

items.title: Directions
items.text: Tap container so that flies fall to the bottom of the container, then sprinkle flies into your pets' enclosure.

description: Flightless D. hydei fruit fly culture (24oz container) D. hydei fruit flies are ideal feeder insects for small or young animals. They are about twice as large as D. melanogaster. Unlike crickets or other typical feeder insects, fruit flies cannot bite or harm your pet. They are easy to maintain and reproduce quickly (D. hydei reproduce slower than D. melanogaster). The life cycle of D. hydei is complete in about 4 weeks while that of D. melanogaster is complete in under two weeks. Life cycle: Fruit flies undergo complete metamorphosis. They begin life as eggs which, within 2-4 days of being laid, will hatch into larvae called maggots. Maggots feed on and live within the media provided at the bottom of the container. They remain in the media until ready to pupate. At this point, the larvae will crawl to the sides of the container and on the wood shaving substrate to pupate. They will remain in their pupal stage for about a week. Pupae will change from a orange-brown color to transparent (you can see the fly inside!) When they are ready to eclose (emerge as an adult from a pupae). Newly emerged adult flies are sensitive and shouldn’t be handled for about an hour. In a couple of days, they will be ready to mate and lay eggs. Temperature/humidity: The life cycle progression of fruit flies, like other insects, is dependent on the temperature at which they are kept. While room temperature is ideal for maintaining flies, they can tolerate colder temperatures. If necessary, they can be refrigerated (NOT FROZEN) for about a week without inflicting any harm to the flies at any stage of life. Refrigerating the flies will cause them to be very sluggish or immobile until temperatures warm up. As a result, temporary refrigeration can be used to separate/transfer flies elsewhere. As long as there is adequate air exchange, and the media is properly hydrated, humidity isn’t a crucial factor. Media: The media provided is a potato-based proprietary modification of a commercially available type of fruit fly media. I supplement media with beetroot powder as this provides extra nutrients, like carotenoids, which have been shown to extend the lives of fruit flies as well as gives your pets (who eat the flies) better nutrition. Media should remain hydrated at all times. It should be smooth (like thick mashed potatoes), but never runny or sticky. Otherwise, adult flies will become trapped in the media and suffocate. If media is too dry, cracks may appear. Media that is not properly hydrated will delay the production of flies. To rehydrate, simply add water (slowly and little by little) until media appears smooth.
features: D. hydei fruit flies are ideal feeder insects for small or young animals. They are about twice as large as their smaller counterparts in the genus - D. melanogaster. Unlike crickets or other typical feeder insects, fruit flies cannot bite or harm your pet.
Enhanced medium (the food for larvae) to support fruit fly growth and increase nutrient density.
Culture will produce thousands of flies within 3-4 weeks (if maintained at room temperature).
Compact 24oz container to save you storage space without taking away from amount of medium at bottom of container.

No harmful substances. All contents of container are safe for your pets and for children to handle: wood wool, potato-based medium supplemented with powdered nutrients (giving its red color), fruit fly eggs, larvae and adults.
Newly started/prepared - culture will be innoculated with flies upon your order.

Ideal amount of media added! Not too much (where flies never get to the media and it becomes overcrowded and rancid), and not too little (where flies are left searching for food)! You'd think that more media equals more flies... Not exactly. There's only a certain amount of surface area within containers where larvae can properly pupate and emerge as flies. If ther is too much media (and if it doesn't spoil by then), larvae will pupate in layers over one another and suffocate those that have pupated prior.

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: About 6 weeks ago I returned to a vegetable juice fast for weight loss and health reasons. lacking a garbage disposal in my sink, the leftover pulp was accumulating in the produce shopping bags. Of course, "beaming in" came the gnats/fruit flies (or whatever they are). I had used these type traps before with some success. Despite the poor reviews I needed something. 1 month later, each trap has dozens of dearly departed invaders. Unfortunately, the trap covers are not secure - pick one up improperly and be prepared to clean up the mess. Yes - the fluid can be homemade with apple cider vinegar and a drop or 2 of dish soap, although they each come with one refill.NOTE! To the negative reviewers, I have an observation: The attraction of the trap must be greater to the buggies than the "issue" that caused them to pop up in the first place, or they will choose that over the traps! Whether we like it or not, we must deal more effectively with the "issue." Of course, once that is completely cleared up, the invaders will shortly depart anyway -- trap or no trap.

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I made the mistake of buying 12 of these traps, and it was a complete waste of money.  The gnats / fruit flies / no-see-ums simply are not interested AT ALL.  For years I had been using Bye Bye Fruit Fly in open cups or cans, and that works great, but is unsightly and prone to messy accidental spills.  So these traps seemed like they’d do the same thing, and look nicer, and not spill.  But the flies just refuse to go into these traps.  Something about the design of the holes, I don’t know if insufficient scent gets out, or if the flies can’t find their way in, but I’m still struggling with them flying all over.  And I’m in a studio apartment, and have had 12 of these traps set up all around for two weeks now.  They’re not catching ANYTHING.

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I purchased this Fruit Fly Trap Refill Liquid (4 Pack) because I was tired of dealing with fruit flies around my kitchen, especially near the sink and trash can. I was honestly impressed by how well it works.From the first day, I noticed that it attracts all types of fruit flies and gnats, not just a few. The fruit-scented bait draws them in quickly, and once they enter the trap, they can’t escape. It works especially well when placed near breeding areas, making it a reliable and continuous solution instead of just a temporary fix.One of the things I appreciate most is that it’s safe to use near food, kids, and pets. There are no harsh chemicals or strong odors, which is very important to me when using something in the kitchen or bathroom. I’ve also used it near indoor plants with no issues at all.Another plus is its wide compatibility. You don’t need a special trap—just pour it into a small bowl, ramekin, or open container, and it works perfectly. It’s truly a pour-and-go solution with no mess or cleanup.The value is excellent. Each bottle lasts up to 8 weeks, so the 4-pack provides long-lasting protection. I’ve used it in the kitchen, bathroom, and near trash areas, and it performs consistently in every space.Overall, this product is effective, non-toxic, easy to use, and a great value. If you’re struggling with fruit flies indoors and want a safe solution that actually works, I highly recommend it. I would definitely buy it again.

aPlusContent.title: Product description
aPlusContent.rawText:
Product description
NOT just plain ol vinegar. This special blend of vinegars and other ingredients was formulated specifically to catch fruit flies effectively. 
Just pour the liquid in a 2-3 oz. container and set on your countertop, and you'll be catching flies in no time. 
The ingredients in our formula are food safe ingredients, which means you can use this lure safely in your kitchen, bar, or restaurant. Note: it is not intended for consumption. 
2-3 oz of liquid should last you close to 45 days (depends on temperature, humidity etc). We recommend disposing and refilling the liquid every 3 weeks for maximum effectiveness. 
FRUIT FLY - YES 
small red eyes
tan body
small size (1/8" long)
Did you buy fruit recently? These little guys fly pretty slow, and like decaying fruit and other fermenting things. 
FUNGUS GNAT - NO 
small black eyes
black slender body
small size (1/8")
Found mostly in plants and drains/dark dank areas. 
DRAIN FLY - NO 
moth-like body
dark dank environments and drains
small size (1/8")
Feeds and breeds in dark dank areas, mainly drains. looks like a small moth. 
HOUSE FLY - NO 
red eyes
dark body
large sized ( >1/4")
Much larger sized than fruit flies and has a dark body.

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I didn't have a huge Fruit Fly problem but enough to drive me crazy when I tried to cook or eat or, for any reason, have food out.  A few flies would invariably show up and I was constantly fanning them away.  When I realized it was just getting worse I took an hour or more to read all the reviews of the Fruit Fly solutions available on Amazon.  I decided on this one based on ease of use, reviews and non toxic/smell qualities.  You already know how these things usually go.  They promise the miracle and rarely deliver!  Not so with this product!!  I received it and immediately set up one in my kitchen and one in my tv room.  In ONE day I noticed a difference......fewer flies.  In two days I noticed one lone fly crawling across the lid of this solution.  He was the last fruit fly I saw.  (I think he was the CEO of the Fruit Fly Corporation checking out this trap and immediately sent out a memo to all worker flies to flee the scene). I have not seen one since.  I am amazed and thrilled that a product actually does what it promised.  Like I said my problem wasn't HUGE but I would highly recommend this to anyone with this problem.  It doesn't have an odor and is so easy to use.  Good luck.  These little guys can drive you to drink!
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on August 11, 2024

title: Dr. Killigan's Reusable Fruit Fly Traps for Indoors - Washable with 2 Refills, 80+ Day Supply, Highly Effective for Home and Kitchen
breadCrumbs: Patio, Lawn & Garden > Pest Control > Traps
features: Reusable Trap with Long-Lasting Attractant: Each fruit fly catcher includes two 2 oz. bottles of Dr. Killigan’s fruit fly lure, providing up to 60 days of fruit fly bait. As a reusable trap with the option for 16 oz. Sweet Surrender liquid refills sold separately, this durable multi-use solution eliminates the need for wasteful single-use traps.

Safe Around People, Pets and Plants: When used as directed, these fruit fly traps for indoors are completely safe, giving you peace of mind knowing they're low-risk to use around children, cats, dogs and other loved ones.

Best-In-Class Fruit Fly Lure: The proprietary attractant derives from a specialized fermentation process with grapes from California. For fruit flies, it's to die for. With no harsh chemicals, artificial fragrances, dyes or colors, this formula has consistently outperformed other products and DIY solutions in hundreds of side-by-side tests.

Lures & Kills Fruit Flies, Fast: With a patent-pending design, these fruit fly traps use our powerful, plant-based formula to quickly attract and kill fruit flies with catch rates up to 10x greater than competing products. NOT INTENDED FOR GNATS OR DRAIN FLIES.

Protects Your Kitchen, Pantry & Home: This versatile solution is effective in any room where fruit flies gather. Unscrew the lid, pour in 2 oz. of the specialized attractant, replace the lid and let the trap take care of the rest. Draw in fruit flies, take them out, and safeguard your peace and sanity.
WORKS ACROSS THE HOME – Place traps near fruit bowls, sinks, compost bins, trash cans, or anywhere fruit flies are seen. Ideal for kitchens, pantries, and food prep areas.
SUITABLE FOR BUSY HOUSEHOLDS – Ideal for anyone seeking a durable, fuss-free solution to indoor fruit fly problems—no extra gadgets, no complicated steps.
FOR HOMES THAT VALUE CLEAN DESIGN – Blends seamlessly into household settings with a form that favors simplicity, cleanliness, and ease of use.

aPlusContent.title: Product description
aPlusContent.rawText:
Product description
The video showcases the product in use.The video guides you through product setup.The video compares multiple products.The video shows the product being unpacked.
Previous page
Next page
1 Sourced from nature 2 Powered By Plants 3 What is "Minimum Risk"? 
What attracts fruit flies? 
Fruit flies are attracted to ripe, fermenting or decaying fruit and vegetables, as well as fermented beverages like wine and beer. They're drawn by the odor of yeasts and acetic acid, produced during fermentation. 
How does the Fruit Fly Trap catch fruit flies? 
The trap's patented vent lines allow just the right amount of aroma to escape. These lines serve as pathways directing fruit flies to their demise. Sweet Surrender's lower surface tension keeps flies from landing on top of the liquid's surface. 
How long does Sweet Surrender last? 
Once opened, Sweet Surrender will last up to 6 weeks. Unopened and stored as indicated in a cool, dark place, it lasts for 2 years from the manufacture date, listed on the container. The Fruit Fly Trap can be reused indefinitely. 
What is the best location for the Fruit Fly Trap? 
Since they are attracted to light and the smell of mature fruit, this tends to be on a windowsill or near fruit on a countertop. This may be a matter of trial and error, until you find the sweet spot (pun intended). 
Will the fruit fly trap attract gnats and drain flies? 
Sweet Surrender is designed to attract fruit flies, NOT drain flies or gnats. Fruit flies have rounded bodies and are brown in color. Gnats resemble mosquitoes with longer bodies and legs and are black; drain flies have fuzzy bodies similar to moths. 
DR. KILLIGAN'S PROMISE 
Greetings, friend. I'm Dr. Killigan, inviting you to join the more than 1.3 million households who have partnered with me in the quest for safe and effective pest control. My vision is to restore peace of mind, personal dignity and freedom from pests to every person who finds unwelcome bugs in their home.

items.title: Directions
items.text: For best results, apply product at time of lowest drain use, typically at the end of the day. Initially add 8 oz to each drain. Try to coat the surface areas inside the drain pipe. productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I had a sudden gnat infestation coming from my bathroom and kitchen drains in my studio apartment after five years of living here. I ordered three bottles and used the first two within the first two weeks. After just one day, I immediately saw gnats evacuating the drains. It was a bit messy for about a week as they tried to find a new home, but I kept up the treatment and used the third bottle to cover all drains. Now, going into week three, I haven’t seen a single gnat. This product works extremely well, is affordable, and the amount in each bottle is more than enough to solve the problem. I highly recommend it.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on February 5, 2026

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: What I did to eliminate my drain fly infestation and product review
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I'd been dealing with a drain fly infestation for months and was at my wits end. Was killing 20-40 each day by hand or more. Tried all kinds of things to get rid of them - bug spray, lining my windows with bug spray, baking soda and vinegar down the drains, bug killer down the drains, sticky paper, and several other methods you've probably read about online. I even used one of those hand-held electric racket zappers to swipe at them which actually made hunting them down kind of fun. ZAP!The product: I used this pleasant smelling goo a few times as instructed and it seems to have taken care of much of the problem. After applying it to my shower drain and bathroom sink, the leftover gel around the drains had a bunch of drain fly carcasses in it the next day. I did the same thing over a period of about a week and more carcasses would appear each time. I have effectively eliminated a drain fly infestation which was driving me mad. Very cool!Keep in mind there were other tactics I employed because I wanted to be thorough and make sure these pests were GONE. So here are some suggestions which I hadn't seen listed on any of the "how to kill drain flies" sites. Adapt and use these in addition to using the gel and your problem will be no more.1) It seems drain flies LOVE bathroom towels, especially ones left up to dry. I would pick up my towel and three or four would immediately fly off of it. So I removed the towel from the bathroom when done with it and place it in my dryer where it was inaccessible. I think they use these to breed or nest. Yuck. You can replace the towels again once the flies have been eliminated.2) Remove bath mat, especially if made of cloth or wool. I think they breed there, too.3) Remove any cloth shower curtains (which can get very damp). Basically, remove anything that gets wet and collects human skin cells or byproducts.4) Flush out your jacuzzi if you have one. Jacuzzis have a lot of pipes which collect a lot of gunk just like your drain does. When I cleaned it out, a ton of unsightly organic effluvium came out, including a ton of fly carcasses. Blech! There are videos on Youtube showing how to do this.5) Put sticky paper on the window sills. This caught a TON of stray drain flies and other pests stuck between the window and outside.6) This is a bit more hardcore, but you may want to try buying a bug zapper and covering the windows in your living space and leave it on over night. Drain flies are attracted to light and will try to escape through your windows. When you block them out and provide only one source of light, they will be attracted to it. This seemed to help me a little bit. It captured and killed some of them but not as many as I'd hoped.7) Vacuum the heck out of your place. I'm not an expert but it seems they like our "leftovers", as in skin cells, dust and other elements produced by indirect human waste. If anything, you'll have a nice clean environment to live in.8) De-clutter any area where they seem to be thriving. This could help expose breeding points. The bathroom was my target as this was where most of them were congregating. I made it uninhabitable for them by stripping it bare.9) If you have a spider, she is your best friend. I had one who was systematically capturing and eating any stray drain flies. My own personal serial killer for hire. Spider caught more of these pests than my bug zapper. I must have counted around 40 carcasses! Do not kill any spiders. They feast on these critters. Keep them around! They are literally a secret weapon which costs you nothing, except maybe a few creepy crawly dreams.I think most of the problem was addressed by the drain gel, probably 90% of the issue. Could be more or less for you. But the chances of eliminating the problem will increase if you follow the steps above.Good luck with it!
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on August 1, 2019

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: Great product. Does what it says, faster than expected. Rid my drain fly problem in 2 days. Which is good, cause it kindof stinks.  Recommend you treat your drains right before bedtime. That way it has a chance to work  some before running water down the drains again.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on August 2, 2025

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: The first time I use this product it worked or so I thought. Then I was struggling for a while, although it never got as bad as some posts. I couldn’t understand why it was not working for me well, and then on my last purchase when I started pouring, I stopped and thought maybe I should shake it up. after shaking it’s pretty well for a few seconds, I poured it, and it looked pearly, white and non-translucent. before it had been more translucent. this time it worked. I just ordered my next bottle to stay ahead of it.I did see that some people mentioned boiling water and putting it down the drain. I started to do that earlier on so that they sink was pretty cleaned out of the stuff. I don’t know if that helps but it makes sense to me so I’m going to keep doing that part.I make sure to put all food scraps away, which when you have kids is a daily thing. If I noticed there’s a plate that was forgotten or something like that, and maybe the dishwasher is running, I have stuck it in the fridge to care for in the morning. I know that that’s not ideal but I wanted to share because not everybody can run to the trash and sometimes we are just exhausted and it does not take much for those little guys.

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I purchased this product thinking I had fruit flies but Learned they were actually drain flies. I had never experienced this before and they look very similar. Save yourself some money and know what type of flies you have. Home remedy for drain fly is 1 cup apple cider vinegar, 1/2 cup salt, 1/2 cup baking soda. Let mixture stand for 24 hours then bring to a slight rolling boil and pour in drain. Worked like magic. This  product may work for fruit flies but for me it was a waste . Certainly not the products fault

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: Normally, I love this brand but I'm really upset about this product that's supposed to take care of fruit flies and sewer flies.  Instructions state that leaving it in the drain overnight is ideal so that's what I did.  They also say that for best results, use it several times so I repeated the process the next night. Both times, I wiped away the excess with paper towel since rinsing them wasn't an option.  So, not only has it eaten the finish off of my drain (or whatever the correct term is), this morning there were several sewer flies.  I am not a happy girl.So, this is an update.  I contacted the Green Gobbler customer service folks copying the review above and since they had no way for me to attach the pictures below, I described what had happened.  I received back a boilerplate email describing that some drains are tougher to unclog than others.  Really?  I write about a product designed to rid your drains of fruit and sewer flies and they respond with info about unclogging my drains?  I responded telling them that I was disappointed that they hadn't even taken the time to read my comment.  Pretty poor and what's worse is that they did not respond to then at all to me.  I'm just disgusted as I have used their drain cleaner several times and it's done a great job but this has soured me on the company.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on July 2, 2025

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I recently tried the Fruit Fly Gel Drain Treatment to deal with an annoying fruit fly problem, and here’s my take:Pros:1. Effective Solution: This gel works wonders on fruit flies and drain flies. It clings to the pipes and eliminates the flies by targeting their breeding ground. Within a few days, I noticed a significant reduction in the number of flies.2. Easy to Use: The application is straightforward. You just pour the gel into the drain, and it does the rest. It's hassle-free and doesn’t require any additional tools.3. Safe Formula: Made with citronella oil, it’s non-toxic and safe to use around the house, which is a big plus for homes with kids and pets.4. Pleasant Scent: Unlike other chemical treatments, this gel has a refreshing citrus scent, which helps keep the drains smelling fresh while it works.Cons:1. Needs Regular Application: For best results, you need to apply it regularly, especially if the infestation is severe. This means it’s not a one-time fix.2. Price: While effective, it’s a bit on the pricier side compared to some DIY solutions. However, the effectiveness justifies the cost in my opinion.3. Sticky Residue: If not handled carefully, the gel can leave a sticky residue around the drain area. It’s essential to pour it directly into the drain to avoid this.Overall Experience:The treatment  has been a reliable solution for my fruit fly problem. It’s easy to use and effective, with the added benefit of a pleasant scent. While it requires regular application and careful handling to avoid residue, the results are worth it.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on May 29, 2024

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I use this one time and it works great

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: Gets rid of fruit flies fast!
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: These work great.  This purchase is about my 4th of this product.  Over the Christmas period I made the mistake of leaving a bowl of apples in our dining room.  January 2nd I went back to trying to eat healthy and realized I had made a fruit fly infestation.  We were out over the Holiday's so I hadn't noticed them.  I tried a homemade fruit fly trap which didn't work.  Ordered these that I'd used before and after about two days all the flies had gone.  They do have a strong smell of apple cider vinegar but it's not unpleasant.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on January 14, 2026

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: The Best Natural, Pet-Safe & Effective Fruit Fly Trap
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I’ve been getting fruit flies in my bathroom that seem to come up from the drain for awhile now and I’ve made my own diy fruit fly traps and consistently poured white vinegar down the shower and sink drains, and it would get rid of a lot but not all of them and then eventually there would be a lot all over again. I started looking for a natural cat-safe and effective fruit fly trap and I decided on this one, and I’m glad I did. It’s been about a week and finally I have zero fruit flies! This is very effective and it’s very affordable too and lasts for about 30 days. I love how it has a lid, so that when I’m cleaning the bathroom cleaner, I can close the lid and put it on the floor while I clean. I even accidentally knocked it over while cleaning with the lid on, and nothing leaked out at all. It doesn’t have a strong vinegar smell after the first few days, and even before then it isn’t so strong, it just smells like red wine vinegar. It’s very easy to use, you just open the lid and remove the seal and place it where you want it, and that’s it. Will definitely be repurchasing to prevent any future fruit flies, $7 for 30 days is a really good deal.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on October 15, 2025

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: So disappointed! No longer recommend. Seller never responded.
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: FRUIT FLY SEASON IS RAPIDLY APPROACHING AND I'M DETERMINED TO PREVENT LAST SUMMER'S INFESTATION. Update : I contacted the company multiple ways and multiple times.Amazon and the company totally ignored my legitimate complaint.  Obviously they don't care about their customers dissatisfaction. It's not about the money - it's about blatant misrepresentation and unethical behavior displayed by both companies. I couldn't even get Amazon to include the "frequently returned" box on the item's page.I bought  three bottles of auntie's product on Amazon 6 days ago to replace my DIY liquid attractant of appl cider vinegar +brown sugar +dish detergent.It's been 5 days and can confirm the other negative reviews that the product CAN'T contain the same liquid attractant - the fruit flies feed on the liquid and are not trapped - they can fly away!!!I unintentionally performed a side by side experiment using this WORTHLESS product against my attractant. Both liquids were placed next to separate hanging cylinders of bright yellow sticky fly paper.I actually watched the flies fly from the  auntie's product into my liquid. Unlike the auntie product, any flies that were not trapped in my DIY liquid were trapped in the fly paper.After 5 days I had to replace the fly paper adjacent to DIY liquid. in contrast, the 3 bottles of Auntie containing who knows what had A TOTAL OF LESS THAN 20 IN THE BOTTLES AND ABOUT 10 TO 20 TRAPPED BY THE FLY PAPER.I am so angry about wasting money on products from Amazon with good reviews. This product has overall rating of 3.8. But that rating is CUMULATIVE - The much older reviews are generally positive compared to more current negative reviews. I conclude this is due to a change in formulation that neither amazon or the seller made clear.I was so pleased that Good Housekeeping had recommended the best way to eliminates fruit fly infestations in a recent article.  The GH panel incorrectly included this product as the only commercially effective liquid attractant since it contained malic acid as part of the formulation. TheFRUIT FLY SEASON article made me remember that I had used Aunties successfully a few summers ago and loved their esthetics and effectiveness.I already emailed a request for them to provide evidence of its effectiveness using a recent bottle.I am requesting a refund from this seller based on my results and will update the status with both responses.Meanwhile if you are battling with fruit flies, infestations made worse by the higher temperature (I live in Connecticut) in the Northeast, read below.First understand that you must use the following steps throughout the fruit fly lifecycle (over several weeks so that the original fruit flies are trapped as well as the their progeny hatched from the hundreds of larvae deposited on whatever they fed on. {I am a biochemist and patent attorney so I needed to know what was going on}.Keep in mind for DIY solutions or other traps that you must attract and rid them from escaping. Otherwise you'll have dead flies near and flying around the attractant.A single fruit fly will deposit hundreds of larvae on fruit and vegetables and existing food sources.Follow these steps :1. Clean all surfaces daily with your typical all purpose cleaner (I use Fantastic with bleach) wherever you find these annoying bugs. For us this is primarily the kitchen in addition to the bathroom and TV room.REMEMBER THEY ARE ATTRACTED TO LIGHT AND 1 FLY WILL DEPOSIT EGGS THAT MATURE IN about 5 to7 DAYS.2. Unfortunately surface cleaning every 1-2 days is necessary to eliminate flying flies you see and larva that will hatch that you can't.3. Immediately thoroughly wash all fruit and vegetables (existing or new) and refrigerate.4. Eliminate All food debris from kitchen counters, microwave, garbage cans, toaster oven,  pet water and food bowls, etc. and keep everything covered or washed like used knives, glassware and plates.5. If you think this is drastic like I did, then you will see an infestation that feels as if those little things won...6. in the article, Good housekeeping recommended this product as using malic acid which is found in apple cider vinegar.  It was part of discussion on using a DIY solution see ingredients above. Any solution must also contain a detergent which causes the flies eating on the attractant  liguid to sink in the solution. They can't fly away and begin a new cycle of depositing eggs. At the beginning of the infestation I used the ingredients above along with standing/hanging yellow sticky fly paper I also bought from Amazon.I am very frustrated that I didn't do the research and work upfront instead of wasting money trying to find the non-existent magic bullet all summer!! As much as I love tasty vine ripened tomatoes and other summer fruit and vegetables, it's just not worth it. I think the high temperatures in July throughout the North East as well as nationaly/globally contributed to the problem experienced by me and almost everyone I know.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on August 9, 2023

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: This product works!  I placed it on my counter and within hours many of these annoying fruit flies disappeared. title: Double-Sided Sticky Trap Covers for Fruit Fly Traps – 25 Pack Adhesive Cards for Fruit Fly – Clear, Mess-Free, Indoor Use
breadCrumbs: Patio, Lawn & Garden > Pest Control > Traps
description: Turn any cup into an effective fruit fly trap! These double-sided sticky cards are designed to seal the top of a cup filled with wine or apple cider vinegar, attracting and trapping fruit flies without any mess or chemicals. Simple, smart, and super effective for kitchens, pantries, and dining areas.
features: Quick Cup-to-Trap Setup – Simply peel off the release paper from both side and place the sticky card over the top of any cup filled with apple cider vinegar, wine, or your preferred attractant. Flies are naturally drawn in and get captured instantly.
High-Strength Double-Sided Adhesive – Both sides of the card are coated with extra-sticky glue, allowing it to trap insects from above and inside the cup for increased effectiveness.
Low-Profile, Mess-Free Design – The clear, discreet card blends seamlessly into kitchen spaces, avoiding spills, splashes, or the need to poke holes or handle liquid bait.
Indoor-Safe & Odor-Free – Made without chemicals or fragrances, making it suitable for use around food prep areas, children, and pets.
Great Value Pack – Each box includes 25 cup-top sticky cards, providing long-lasting coverage for multiple rooms or continuous seasonal use.

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: I like the idea but it didn't work that well for me.
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: These are interesting. I like the idea. I have tried the vinegar in a cup with plastic wrap method before with some success, and this seems like a more robust variation of that. I set this up in my kitchen after seeing a couple of fruit flies, but they went away on their own without getting trapped. Perhaps if I had a bigger infestation of fruit flies, it would have done better in that test.I *do* have a fungus gnat problem with my plants. Despite the best efforts of my carnivorous plants, they have set up home in the soil of my roses. I set up one of these as instructed for fruit flies, and also cut one up into strips to stick into the soil like a traditional fungus gnat trap. They caught a few but were outperformed by the yellow traps, which were also easier to see and handle.

title: Fruit Fly Traps for Indoors (1 Device+10 Refills) Plug in Fly Trap for Gnats, Fruit Flies, Flying Insects Sleek Design Indoor Safe
breadCrumbs: Patio, Lawn & Garden > Pest Control > Traps
features: Upgrade Fruit Fly Traps: LFSYS plug-in fly trap now feature 10 high-quality lamp bulbs with dual wavelengths of 365nm and 395nm. This advanced lighting technology allows the capture rate of various flying insects, including fungus gnats, fruit flies, and moths.
Double-Sided Transparent Adhesive Boards: Our boards are designed with a dual-sided adhesive and complete transparency, providing extensive coverage compared to traditional traps.

Easy installation: Simply remove the protective film from the adhesive board, insert it into the device, and plug it into a socket. Then you could sit and wait for its magic. ●For optimal results, we recommend using the fruit fly trap in a dark room without other light sources to capture more flying insects effectively.

Low Maintenance: Our UV Light Fly Trap is easy to install and requires minimal upkeep. Check the trap once a day, and when it catches insects, effortlessly replace the sticky card with a new one. Enjoy effective fruit fly trapping with minimal effort.

Quiet and Odor-Free Operation: The LFSYS Cube UV light fly trap employs adhesive boards to silently trap flying insects without any odors. Unlike conventional zappers, it operates safely and without noise, providing long-lasting protection. Enjoy a restful sleep without the disturbance of mosquito buzzes.

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: This fruit fly trap works really well and blends in nicely indoors. The design is discreet and doesn’t look like a typical bug trap, which I really appreciate. The sticky panels are very effective and catch gnats and fruit flies quickly.The UV light is gentle—not overly bright—so it doesn’t disturb the room or become annoying at night. I also like that it comes with multiple devices and plenty of refill panels, which makes it a good value.Overall, it’s effective, subtle, and perfect for indoor use. I’d definitely recommend it if you’re dealing with gnats or fruit flies.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on December 28, 2025

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: This is very simple but effective. I was literally surprised at how many little fruit flies this trapped, besides I didn’t realize we had that many flying around. I’m very pleased with this. It’s perfect size, appearance, durability is just what we were looking for. There’s no smell, just a sticky paper placed in a box that has a purple light and the nasty, irritating insects get drawn in and are stuck thus die. The price point is perfect for a budget friendly house. In the photo I just put this up 1 day ago because the other one was full of fruit flies.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on November 26, 2025

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I’ve been struggling to get rid of plant/dirt gnats for months. Unfortunately, I bought a bag of contaminated, indoor potting soil and didn’t realize until it was too late that it was infested with gnats and their eggs. I have been battling to get rid of them for months now. They don’t respond to the sugar vinegar solutions since they’re not fruit flies I got this hoping the black light effect would attract more of them quickly to help get rid of them fast faster. It is odorless. It doesn’t make any noise. It’s super small and emits less light than a nightlight. The removable replaceable clear, sticky sheets are sticky on both sides. If you have a curious pet or small child you may want to put it up where they won’t get to it because it will definitely stick to their face or hands. I would hate to see this get stuck to an animals whiskers so bear that in mind if you have small curious, cats or dogs.The picture I attached is one of the sticky sheets that I removed when I was replacing it so it’s not in the device with the light but as you can see it captured so many gnats. It works great at night in a dark room so that that light can truly attract the gnats. This one was in the trap for about 2 1/2 weeks. As you can see, it’s working wonderfully.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on January 28, 2026

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: It works great for catching small gnats. No issues there. For flies, it seems to work best when the room is completely dark, but it still gets the job done. I was surprised that it even caught a few mosquitoes! The sticky refills are extremely adhesive, and swapping them out is quick and easy. The light is gentle and not at all bothersome when it’s on. Overall, very effective and easy to use.

For the price, it's easy to set up and use, and really seems to be effective!

productPageReviewsFromOtherCountries.reviewTitle: No ayudo a resolver plaga de moscas de la fruta :/
productPageReviewsFromOtherCountries.reviewDescription: Lo compré para lidiar con una plaga de moscas de la fruta. Seguí todas las instrucciones al pie de la letra y le dí hasta semanas de oportunidad para ver si servía de algo, ya que las instrucciones decían que podía "tardar hasta una semana en funcionar" o algo por el estilo, pero jamás atrapó una sola mosca. A pesar de que tenía esperanzas de que funcionara, me dejó muy decepcionado. Ví que el producto tenía varias reseñas positivas, no sé si a mí me tocó un dispositivo defectuoso o qué. En fin, tendremos que buscar otras opciones para lidiar con la plaga. Personalmente, si te interesa comprarlo específicamente para moscas de la fruta, no lo recomiendo. Quizá para otras especies de mosca e insectos voladores sí funciona, pero no me consta.

items.title: Safety Information
items.text: Always follow use instructions. Keep out of reach of children and pets. Do not ingest. In case of accidental ingestion, seek medical attention immediately. Avoid contact with eyes and skin. If contact occurs, rinse thoroughly with water. Store in a cool, dry place away from heat sources and open flames. Do not use near food or food preparation areas. Dispose of the product in accordance with local, state, and federal regulations.

items.title: Directions
items.text: Always follow use instructions. Ideal for use all around your home.

items.title: Legal Disclaimer
items.text: Statements regarding dietary supplements have not been evaluated by the FDA and are not intended to diagnose, treat, cure, or prevent any disease or health condition.

description: Save to use around kids and pets* and it contains no added fragrances, dyes, parabens or sulfates. This Raid Essentials fly trap catches up to 100% of fruit flies (on day one of use). To use, simply shake well before opening, place near the source of fruit flies, twist off the lid and remove the cap. The discreet fruit fly trap can be placed on countertops or tables. This Raid fly trap is part of a complete line of products�including Raid Flying Insect Killer � to target specific insects inside and outside the home. Discover family-friendly protection backed by 60 years of Raid experience.* when used as directed
features: Introducing Raid Essentials Fruit Fly Tap that is safe* to use around kids and pets (*When used as directed)
Raid Essentials is made with essential oils, and is specifically designed with plant-based ingredients to kill bugs
Deals with fruit flies, not tested on gnats or drain flies.
Ideal for use all around your home (always follow use instructions)
Lasts up to 21 days

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I had a gnat size fly that was driving me nuts. So I bought this Raid product. I love it. I shook it like the directions say then I opened the cap and took off the inside plastic. I located a spot in the middle of my plants near the window. The first day I could see the gnats on the rim and accidentally touched the container and they all flew away. The next day I had (no kidding) 15 gnats floating in the container. GROSS!!  I thought I only had one. Then I moved it to my kitchen and there were 2 more that found the container. They dove right in like the rest. I love it. Obviously they didn’t realize how toxic this liquid was. It was so easy to open and it did its trick. It is like 1/2 cup container so small, easy to use, better than plain apple cider vinegar.  It isn’t noticeable and worked better than I ever imagined. As a matter of fact I put it on 21 day repeat subscription in case. But after 6 days, I think I got them all. It does have the scent of apple cider vinegar but you have to be close to it to smell it. I also emptied it down the drain ( that is the directions) and there is sludge in the bottom. I think it disintegrates the bugs as well. .  I couldn’t be pleased more.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on September 3, 2024

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: Mid Tier - wasn’t blown away but also not terrible
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: It worked, I suppose? I still have some fruit flies buzzing around, and even in a clean, closed room with three of these, they weren’t completely effective. I really didn’t care for the fact that it was just a wide-open jar. Most products like this have a topper with holes for the flies to go through, but this was just open with nothing on top. Did it catch some of my flies? Yes, it caught a bunch. Did it solve my problem? Not completely. Did it work any better than the little red Apple product? No, and I think the product really suffers from not having a cover on it. Usually, those help to trap whatever flies have scented the bait but haven’t yet drowned. Most of them can’t fly back out once they’ve flown in. This doesn’t have that. It was a bit of a flop for me, but it’s still useful.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on September 9, 2025

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: Was skeptical, but turned out to be the best option out of all. Shake Well!
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I've bought a variety of different bug removal products and this is the one I had the least confidence in. Reviews didn't seem bad but the negative outweighed the positive in my head and I nervously made the purchase. Let me tell you, I was wrong 100x over. The first week I set it up I noticed it attracting them in droves, sitting around the rim but not going in. I was scared here but I carried on. Not too long later I peered in when passing by and it had caught TONS. I have had it open a bit past the 21 days it recommended, and while the strong smell has vanished, it still seems to attract them even now.It is a bit gross with seeing clumps of the little dead things at the bottom, so I wouldn't recommend keeping it out for long. I don't receive guests so it was no inconvenience for me to keep this in the problem areas indefinitely. Next to my sink for the drain buggers, and right on my dining table.I'm keeping it open for a few days more and will definitely be buying another when I'm next able! Maybe two, but that might be a bit much haha!
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on July 14, 2024

It's amazing seeing how many gnats/fruit flies in just one day one of these jars can accumulate.I got 2 jars and my issue is gone! I highly recommend them if you're having any type of infestation if fruit flies or that's. It's a liquid, so not sticky. I think it's been over 2 months and both jars still have liquid left so they lasted quite a while!The smell is a little off-putting, like vinegar but the annoyance of fruit flies is worse. The smell does die down as the liquid decreases over time. The appearance is fine, at some point the jars just blend in with everything else so not unsightly. I'd highly recommend these!! I'm getting them again, just in case...

aPlusContent.title: Product description
aPlusContent.rawText:
Product description
How do I know if the flies are living in my drain? 
If you don't have any food lying around and the flies are the most concentrated in your kitchen and or bathroom, then chances are they are living and breeding in your drains. 
How much do I need? 
Generally speaking... 
- 32 oz - treats one drain 
- 64 oz - treats 2 drains 
- 1 gallon - treats 4 drains 
- 2 gallons - treats 8 drains 
How long does it take to work? 
You'll see a reduction in flies after 2 to 3 days. They should be all gone after 5 to 7 days. Complete elimination in sever cases can take up to 2 weeks. 
Is this safe to use if I am on a septic system> 
Yes, this is septic safe. 
Will the flies come back? 
If the biofilm returns, they can come back. We recommend treating your drain every two weeks as a preventative measure. 
EcoStrong's Drain & Septic Solutions

I did all the drains in my house once a week if any activity in between just pour some around that drain. I will definitely buy again

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: Fly stickers ALONE were worth the purchase !!
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I got these and put the fly stickers on all my drains.  I had been using "Green Gobbler for Fruit & Drain Flies" for about 3-4 days in my drains with NO noticeable difference in the fly population in my house!  I put these stickers on my drains and the next morning I got up and there were NO flies in my house!  But guess what? There weren't any stuck to the fly stickers either.  It's been three days with my drains plugged.  A plug in the bathroom tub, stopper pulled up in the bathroom sink, one fly sticker still stuck to one drain in my kitchen double sink and I just keep the drain stopper in the other sink so I still have a sink in the kitchen to use. Still not a single fly on the sticker, but no flies in the house either.  I wanted to see just how long I'd be 'fly free' doing this.  -So far, 3 days with NO flies AND I still haven't put the EcoStrong drain treatment in the drains! So, the drains ARE the problem.  At first I didn't think so.  I never saw a fly come out of the drains -ever! I live in a duplex and my sinks, tub and probably toilet all drain to a center drain pipe in the common wall that I and my next door neighbor share.  I'm sure she's been inundated with flies.  Those little buggers are smart!  Not one was stuck to the fly stickers but the fact that I had NO flies the next day after putting them on before going to bed and then got up to a fly free house tells me that's where they're coming from.  If I even go get a fly swatter and sit back down at my desk in front of my living room window the flys would vanish.  They KNEW I had that swatter.  They would hid behind my desk calendar or along the edge of my laptop on my desk. -anywhere they could find that I couldn't get at them, so not surprised there was none on the fly stickers.  They knew better.  They just went down the other side of the drain to my neighbors house!  If I didn't get anything but the stickers with this purchase it would have been worth the $36 to me to find this out!  I spent $250 on fly zappers, fly trap bags and other crap and NONE of it worked! -I even took clear packing tape and sealed all my windows in my house shut thinking that's where they were coming in, under the weatherstripping and was getting ready to replace all my window's weatherstripping!  Thank you EcoStrong for the stickers!! I'm gonna use the drain treatment to treat my drains. -As soon as I get another sink stopper to seal the other side of my kitchen sink so I can take the sticker off!  -And I'm gonna buy a gallon and some stickers for my next door neighbor.  With our drains being in common we're gonna have to tackle this together to get rid of these buggers.  But if she won't be diligent and do her side, I'm just gonna keep treating my side and keep my drains closed unless I'm right there running water into them. It's the only solution I can figure out, but at least, thank God! I HAVE a solution that works now!UPDATE:  I used the Eco Strong Drain treatment in all four of my house drains, kitchen sinks, bathroom sink and tub for 8 straight days before I went to bed. I put a strip of blue painters tape across my kitchen sink so I wouldn't accidently forget I treated the drains and use the sink.  I pulled up the bathroom sink stopper & put a small mixing bowl in the bathroom sink so I could wash my hands and run water from that faucet into the mixing bowl, then dumped the bowl into the toilet next to the sink and set it back in the sink for reuse.  That  little system worked pretty well for the 8 day treatment so that it wasn't much hassle not being able to use the sinks for about 10-12 hours during the night.  After 8 days I'm pretty much fly free. I bought a gallon with the stickers for my next door neighbor and gave it to her to use, since our sinks connect to the same drain pipe.  I live in a duplex and we have a common wall so our kitchens and bathrooms back up to each other and use a common drain pipe to the septic tank.  I'm not sure how faithful and diligent she's been on her side but she told me a couple days ago she only has a few flys now instead of the hoards of flys we were both dealing with so I think this stuff works.  I'm going to continue once a week treatments through fly season and keep my sink and tub stoppers in place when I'm not running water into them.  Can't think of anything else to do and the treatments drastically reduced the flys.  Wish it was a 'zero fly zone' but those little buggers are smart.  I'm looking for and closing any remaining entrances they may be getting in from. Fixing to put up a split door screen on my front door.  I think some are getting in that way.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on May 18, 2024

title: Wingless (Drosophila melanogaster) Fruit Fly Culture - *Newly inoculated*
inStockText: Only 5 left in stock - order soon.
breadCrumbs: Pet Supplies > Reptiles & Amphibians > Food

items.title: Indications
items.text: small animals, young animals, feeder insects for various pets

items.title: Directions
items.text: Tap container so that flies fall to the bottom of the container, then sprinkle flies into your pets' enclosure.

description: Wingless D. melanogaster fruit fly culture (24oz container). Wingless fruit flies are ideal feeder insects for small or young animals. Unlike crickets or other typical feeder insects, fruit flies cannot bite or harm your pet. They are incredibly easy to maintain and reproduce quickly. Under ideal conditions and proper maintenance, a single 24oz culture can produce a couple thousand flies! Adult flies are great feeders for: Fish, baby Lizards, small frogs, praying mantis nymphs, spiderlings, and many more! Size: Healthy adult fruit flies are typically just under 1/8 of an inch long (refer to the last two photos of this listing) Life cycle: Fruit flies undergo complete metamorphosis. They begin life as eggs which, within 24 hours of being laid, will hatch into larvae called maggots. Maggots feed on and live within the media provided at the bottom of the container. They remain in the media until ready to pupate. At this point, the larvae will crawl to the sides of the container and on the wood shaving substrate to pupate. They will remain in their pupal stage for about 5 days. Pupae will change from a light brown/ golden color to transparent (you can see the fly inside!) When they are ready to eclose (emerge as an adult from a pupae). Newly emerged adult flies are sensitive and shouldn’t be handled for about an hour. In a couple of days, they will be ready to mate and lay eggs. Temperature/humidity: The life cycle progression of fruit flies, like other insects, is dependent on the temperature at which they are kept. While room temperature is ideal for maintaining flies, they can tolerate colder temperatures. If necessary, they can be refrigerated (NOT FROZEN) for about a week without inflicting any harm to the flies at any stage of life. Refrigerating the flies will cause them to be very sluggish or immobile until temperatures warm up. As a result, temporary refrigeration can be used to separate/transfer flies elsewhere. As long as there is adequate air exchange, and the media is properly hydrated, humidity isn’t a crucial factor. Media: The media provided is a potato-based proprietary modification of a commercially available type of fruit fly media. I supplement media with beetroot powder as this provides extra nutrients, like carotenoids, which have been shown to extend the lives of fruit flies as well as gives your pets (who eat the flies) better nutrition. Media should remain hydrated at all times. It should be smooth (like thick mashed potatoes), but never runny or sticky. Otherwise, adult flies will become trapped in the media and suffocate. If media is too dry, cracks may appear. Media that is not properly hydrated will delay the production of flies. To rehydrate, simply add water (slowly and little by little) until media appears smooth.
features: Enhanced medium (the food for larvae) to support fruit fly growth and increase nutrient density.
Culture will produce thousands of flies within 2 weeks (if maintained at room temperature).
Compact 24oz container to save you storage space without taking away from amount of medium at bottom of container.

No harmful substances. All contents of container are safe for your pets and for children to handle: wood wool, potato-based medium supplemented with powdered nutrients (giving its red color), fruit fly eggs, larvae and adults.
Newly started/prepared - culture will be innoculated with flies upon your order.

D. melanogaster fruit flies are ideal feeder insects for small or young animals. They are about half the size of their larger counterpart in the genus - D. hydei. Unlike crickets or other typical feeder insects, fruit flies cannot bite or harm your pet.

Treats Up to 200 Cubic Feet in Enclosed Spaces – Each strip covers 5’x5’x8’ areas. Use in homes, garages, bathrooms, laundry rooms, closets, basements, kitchens, storage areas, or pantries. Works better than indoor fly traps or bug lights.

aPlusContent.title: Product description
aPlusContent.rawText:
Product description
Fruit Fly BarPro is the most effective fruit fly control and prevention product on the market. Time controlled vapor technology eliminates of flies, cockroaches, spider mites, gnats, mosquitoes, spiders, moths, silverfish, earwigs and beetles for up to 4 months. The specially formulated plastic strips are activated once exposed to air and slowly diffused for up to 120 days, providing 24/7 around the clock elimination and protection. 
Ditch the bug sprays, liquids, and traps that give off chemical-laden odors and leave behind a sticky residue to clean up. The Fruit Fly BarPro works as a solid unit, which means no-mess ease of use anywhere in the drains, mop sinks, and garbage areas of your home or business. Each strip will deliver powerful, effective treatment to a 200 cubic foot (5’ x 5’ x 8’) breeding, feeding, or nesting site. Simply open the foil pouch, place directly above the site, and allow the vapors to sink into every crevice and crack. Completely safe when used properly. 
Investing in a Fruit Fly BarPro means long-lasting results that you can depend on. Each strip eliminates the current infestation and prevents future infestations from any nests or eggs that were left behind. 
Kills More Than Just Fruit Flies! 
Fruit Fly BarPro kills Flies, Cockroaches, Spider Mites, Gnats, Mosquitoes, Spiders, Moths, Silverfish, Earwigs, and Beetles. Not only does it kill them immediately - it also prevents future infestations! 
All insects must eat in order to survive. Flies will lay their eggs in their food. By identifying the food source of the targeted insect we can aggressively eliminate both adult flies and early stage larvae at the source. 
Clean & Odorless with Time-Release Technology! 
Time controlled vapor technology eliminates of flies, cockroaches, spider mites, gnats, mosquitoes, spiders, moths, silverfish, earwigs and beetles FOR UP TO 120 DAYS. 
Our specially formulated plastic strips are activated once exposed to air. Our “no-mess” time released vapors are slowly diffused for up to 4 months providing 24/7 protection. 
Unlike bug sprays, liquids or sticky traps, our vapors are clean. They leave NO RESIDUE on any surface. No mess, no clean-up and NO ODORS! 
4 Months of Protection from Each Strip! 
Our time controlled vapors are released for up to 4 months making Fruit Fly BarPro effective for up to 120 days….continuously! Date and replace the strips every 3/4 months for year round protection. 
Hang in areas occupied by people less than 16 hours per day.
Before occupying a treated space, remove the vapor strip and allow lingering vapors to quickly dissipate.
Do not hang above prepared/exposed foods.
See More

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: It actually works, when nothing else would
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: This product works, extremely well. I never, ever write reviews for anything, but in this case I feel like I need to.My house has a bad problem with fruit flies during the summer months. And, this year, it's bad. Not only that, but now regular house flies joined in, and I had a ton of them flying around too. It was HORRIBLE.Bug spray helped, but it only helped in the moment. I could spray them all I wanted, and sure, it would kill them right then and there. But, when I sprayed hotspots, they'd all get startled and fly away. I'd kill lots, but plenty would also escape. And, after that, they wouldn't come back to that area for a while. But, in time, they would.I tried more than just bug spray, too. I tried many things to get rid of this problem. I tried using those apple shaped things, the ones you put the liquid inside that attracts and kills them. The first time I used it, it worked somewhat well. But, after a few days, and after enough of them were dead inside of the liquid, they stopped working. I kept getting new ones, but every time I used a brand new one, it seemed to be less and less effective.I tried the vinegar and dish soap thing, too. Pretty much the same outcome as the apple shaped things, except even worse results. Less attraction, less killing.I tried the nuclear option one time as well: bug bombing my home last summer. I purchased a few of those bug bomb canisters. I had to cover tons of surfaces in my home so the chemicals didn't leak into it, and then I had to cage up my cat and leave my home for most of the day, coming back myself once to open up windows to air the place out for a few hours. This absolutely did the job and killed 99.9% of them. However, a few things I dislike about this method. One, having to vacate my home for most of the day. Two, having to cover up all the surfaces of my home and be extra cautious when dealing with the entire situation. Three, the fact that it released a spray/vapor everywhere, hence why everything needed to be covered up. And four, but the worst part: They came back. It must not have killed them ALL, because in a few weeks, the problem was back. Unfortunate.If only there was a product that was efficient, kept working for a long period of time, didn't require me to leave my home or be extremely cautious when using it, didn't scare them away so they would go to the same places just to die, and was quick and easy to setup.Oh wait, there is. And this is it.This bug issue is a big problem. Well, it WAS a big problem. I say "was" because, thanks to this product, it isn't anymore.I had three main hotspots in my home: one in the kitchen by the sink, one in my living room in front of the couch on my coffee table, and one in my gaming room on my shelves.I ordered the five pack.The first two, I hung in my kitchen. I have a large kitchen, so I hung one at each end, one of which directly above the center of the hotspot there.The third and fourth went into my living room. This room is also quite large, hence why I used two. One, I hung from the light fixture that lights the room. The second, I hung right next to the coffee table a few feet above it.The fifth and final strip, I hung off of a nail coming out of the top shelf in the gaming room.Please note that the only reason I used two in any room was because these were decently big rooms. Be safe, don't stuff two or three of these things into your small bedroom.After the first 24 hours, I absolutely noticed a reduction in their numbers.After 72 hours (three days), however, they're gone. 99.9% of them. There's still the extremely occasional fly here and there, but at this point it's rare. And it's becoming more and more rare as time ticks on. Considering these last up to 4 months, I can rest assured the problem is gone. Like I said, my home only has this issue in the hot months, which these will be more than enough to cover.Normally, when I turned on my faucet, I'd see a bunch of fruit flies get startled and swarm around. I knew these things were the real deal the first time I turned on the faucet and saw 0 movement. Every time since then, I've looked as hard as I could for any movement when I turned on the faucet, and nothing. This is where the problem was the absolute worst, and now there's literally none of them there.This product is not a REPELLENT. And that's a GREAT thing. Why? Well, if you're using this product, chances are, the bugs are already inside of your home. Using a repellent would not fix the problem. They aren't going to open the front door and leave your house because of a repelling chemical, they're just going to find somewhere else in your home to migrate to.With this product, the flies don't even know there's a chemical there. They think everything is normal, they behave totally normally. And that's what we want. The fruit flies still love my drain. But, if they go there, they die. Quickly, too. They won't just scatter and go somewhere else, they'll simply die. Bug spray will kill them too, but it will also make that area unattractive to them, so they move, and eventually return to that same area once the spray has worn off. So you spray again, and again. No more, not with this product. Hang it where you see the most of them, and that's it.I've had the product for a week now. I can absolutely attest to everything this product claims to do, except for how long it claims to last, which is 120 days (4 months). I haven't had the product long enough to confirm that claim for myself firsthand, but considering every other claim the product made I've seen to be true firsthand, I feel pretty confident on trusting this final claim.I can also report that I've had no ill effects to my own health. Nor has my cat, and I've watched him like a hawk. Interestingly, the same chemical used in this is used in flea collars for cats as well, which is right under their nose and mouth, so I'm not to worried about this product and the small amount it releases into the air in a time-released, controlled fashion. Like an air freshener.So yeah, I'd highly recommend this product. I'm just one of MANY reviewers who has claimed that they tried everything but THIS is the product that worked. And it's true.Note: I would try your best not to expose the strips to heat. Exposing the strips to heat can make the chemical release faster and in larger quantities, which is not good. I'd try to keep it out of direct, hot sunlight, and away from any other potential heat source.Cheers and good luck!

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I own a large flock of parrots and I have plenty of dogs too. Even when I try to keep up with cleaning if I get a gnat problem, it can really spiral out of hand. My sister introduced me to this product and at first I was very wary due to it being a time released vapor and I did not know how my birds would react, but I am happy to say one fly bar in the bird room was all I needed. My flock is okay And very happy to not be overwhelmed by gnats in their cages. There’s nothing odd with them behaviorally and I especially checked the ones that are closest to the bar and they are fine. This stuff literally works overnight. Within hours there is a decrease in gnats and in days they are completely eradicated. There are no new gnats breeding, even on droppings, garbage cans, and any flies that make it into my home don’t make it to the next morning. I am so satisfied with this product that I raved about it to my other parrot owner friends and they too are loving it! You can look up the MDMS for peace of mind, but as long as you do not have large quantities of this fly bar in an enclosed area, your birds will be okay. I have seen similar products since from more well known brands but they do not boast mess free vapor while this does so even though these are pricier I will still stock up for my fiddos! Don’t overthink it, I tested it for you. Just grab it!
