productPageReviews.reviewTitle: DO NOT use in areas where people will be for an extended period of time""
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: "You could mention easy to use, value for money or longevity" -- Amazon's suggestion for this product reviewPer the back of the product package: "If this product is used within homes, use only in closets, wardrobes and cupboards."More generally, it can be used in "garbage rooms, lavatories, floor drains (to sewer), entries and vestibules, offices, locker rooms, machine rooms, boiler rooms, utility equipment, garages, mop closets and product storage (after canning and bottling).""This product may be used in non-food/feed areas... DO NOT use in areas where people will be for an extended period of time."Seems like it's probably not a good idea to use this product in the home. What if I go for broke and put it next to a window that I like to keep open at least sometimes? "Place... away from windows.... [as] drafts, weather and other conditions may affect performance."The above quotes are from the back of the package. As for the novella we are given to read on this product's Amazon page, buried near the end is this handy little warning: this product should not be used in areas where people spend more than 15 hours per day. You mean, areas like my freaking HOME?? That's a deceptively important warning that I totally failed to notice before buying this product.The active ingredient in this product is an  insecticide called dichlorvos. According to Wikipedia, dichlorvos is a neurotoxin that also damages insect DNA. That seems to be how it kills insects, but it has been shown to affect higher-order animals, as well, including humans. "Acute and prolonged exposure may lead to death, genotoxic, neurological, reproductive, carcinogenic, immunological, hepatic, renal, respiratory, metabolic, dermal and other systemic effects. Its toxicity is due to the ability of the compound to inhibit acetyl cholinesterase at cholinergic junction of the nervous system." ("Dichlorvos toxicity: A public health perspective", Henshaw Uchechi Okoroiwu and Iwara Arikpo Iwara).Prevalent in urban waterways, each tenfold increase in organophosphate metabolites (like dichlorvos) is associated with a 50-70% increase in the odds of ADHD in children. (Wikipedia)The DHHS says it may reasonably be anticipated to be a carcinogen. The EPA has revised the acceptable use parameters for dichlorvos down multiple times, and the EU has banned it outright.The Washington State Department of Health issued a warning about the chemical called "Pest Strips with Dichlorvos." They wrote, "Organophosphate pest strips (strips that contain dichlorvos) are legal, but we are concerned they are making people sick, especially if they are not used according to their directions. We are working... to improve warnings... and make the restrictions about occupied areas more clear on the product label. The strips should not be used in general living or sleeping areas."The label also says the pest strips can be used in vacation homes, cabins, and mobile homes - but only if “unoccupied for more than four months immediately following placement of pest strip."" (end quote)I have two young children in my home. Thank goodness that I am assiduous about reading the instructions that manufacturers provide on their products, or I would have exposed my family to a carcinogenic neurotoxin that was never supposed to be in home in the first place. And I'm not the only one. Reading the Q&A's, it's evident that many people who have bought this product are completely oblivious to its risks, meaning they did NOT read the manufacturer's instructions. For example, somebody (apparently trying to piece together how a product with so many restrictions could possibly be meant for home use) asked if it would be effective throughout the home if placed in a closet. One after another, other customers responded, "Why would you do that? I put it here and there and everywhere in my home! It's great!"I can't blame them. We've gotten used to a corporate world that looks out for our safety -- if only at the behest of the federal government. Like others, I bought this product assuming it was safe for my home simply because it's on Amazon. I assumed, without even thinking about it, that if a commercial/industrial-use product with only a handful of safe residential applications were to be sold on Amazon, this would be made very clear in the product title alone, not to mention the subsequent text and images. Other sellers are careful not to mislead and disappoint buyers about much less serious things.We should all know better than to totally cede all responsibility for our own health and welfare to regulators and businesses. I hope somebody reads this and buys something else.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on August 19, 2022

features: Upgrade Fruit Fly Trap: The new LFSYS standalone fruit fly trap features a rechargeable battery that powers the UV lights for up to 12 hours at a full charge. Its cordless design allows flexible placement anywhere, while the advanced 360° UV lighting efficiently attracts flying insects, including fruit flies, fungus gnats, and moths.

Max Catch: The LFSYS fruit fly trap features 10 high-quality lamp bulbs with dual wavelengths of 365nm and 395nm, and uses double-sided transparent adhesive boards for extensive coverage, effectively capturing a variety of flying insects, including fruit flies, fungus gnats, and moths.

Easy Installation: Simply remove the protective film from the adhesive board, insert it into the trap, and activate the device. For best results, use the trap at night in a dark room. Flying insects are naturally more attracted to UV light when other light sources are minimized, so turning off nearby lamps or screens ensures the trap captures insects more efficiently and quickly.

Quiet and Odor-Free Operation: The LFSYS UV light fruit fly trap employs adhesive boards to silently trap flying insects without any odors. Unlike conventional zappers, it operates safely and without noise, providing long-lasting protection. Enjoy a restful sleep without the disturbance of mosquito buzzes.

Easy Setup and Maintenance: Insert the adhesive board into the trap and activate the device. Check the trap regularly, and when the board becomes full, replace it quickly and effortlessly to maintain optimal performance. Allow the trap to operate for several nights; once it begins catching fruit flies, its effectiveness increases as more insects are attracted to the area.

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I’ve been fighting off fruit flies in my kitchen for weeks, and this Fruit Fly Trap has made a huge difference. It’s small, stylish, and blends right in with my countertop decor (I got the Dutch White color). The 360° attraction design really works — I started seeing results within the first day!It’s super easy to set up and clean. The sticky paper catches a lot more flies than I expected, and I love that it comes with 9 refills so I don’t have to reorder right away. It’s quiet and uses gentle light, so it doesn’t bother me when it’s on at night.This is by far the most effective and least messy fruit fly trap I’ve tried. Highly recommend it if you’re tired of dealing with gnats and fruit flies around produce or houseplants!

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: It's around the season when we get fruit flies and my house has it bad. They were all over the kitchen, the living room, the bathroom, and even my room. We have this homemade solution that we keep in each room and it mostly works, except that at a certain point, they stop eating the poison.I got this since I wanted to try something else out and I am glad I did since it is a HUGE improvement. There were a lot of fruit flies at my desk. I set up the device and it worked super quickly. They flew into it and my room is now clear of fruit flies.The really amazing thing about it is that it's portable. When I go to the kitchen or the bathroom, I just bring it with me and more of them fly into it.I love that there are three different light settings that I can change based on the situation. I can leave it on overnight with the dim light on since it's not bright enough to disturb my sleep. The medium setting is great for most situations. The bright setting is very strong and good enough to act as a flashlight. It's great for drowning out other lights to get all the flies interested.Not only all that, but it uses a type C charger. We have a lot of those in the house so I can just plug it in wherever. It also lasts a pretty long time even when not charging. Overall, so totally worth it.Also, it comes with a bunch of sticky pad refills, but I haven't even had to change my initial two pads yet. These seem to last a long time.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on October 14, 2025

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: It is working OK.  I noticed that most of the gnats were trapped at the bottom of the sticky sheets.  I think one could cut the shifts in half, so only half as tall, to get more out of the sheets.  I peeled back each side half-way, then inserted the sheets into the base.  This prevented the need to touch the sheets as much as otherwise.  The price vs the build is not that great, however, if you need to rely on the internal battery then maybe that helps justify the cost.

features: Catch Flying Insects 24/7 - This flying insect trap uses blue and UV light to attract and catch house flies, fruit flies and gnats. Its continuous attraction helps it catch insects day and night.
Mess-Free Disposal - This indoor fly trap and gnat catcher uses a powerful adhesive backing you will never have to touch. The adhesive pad can discreetly catch bugs, and discreetly faces the wall.

Easy-to-Use - To use, remove the sheet from its adhesive backing, insert the disposable cartridge and plug the indoor bug trap into locations where bugs frequent. When full, lift the cartridge out of the insect catcher, and toss it in the garbage.

Versatile Placement - You can place this plug in bug trap in your kitchen, bathroom, garage, bedroom, dining room, enclosed patio or in any socket near plants. These insect traps can be used indoors for easy, year-round protection.
Includes 2 Plug-In Devices and 2 Cartridges - Each of these plug in fly traps come with one cartridge. Shop our full collection to stock up on refills to ensure you always have one on hand.

aPlusContent.title: Product description
aPlusContent.rawText:
Product description
What is the Zevo Flying Insect Trap? 
The Zevo Flying Insect Trap features a durable plug-in base that uses blue and UV light to attract insects to a disposable refill cartridge. Insects get stuck on an adhesive pad in the cartridge, which is easy to remove and dispose of. 
What type of flying insects does it catch? 
The Zevo Flying Insect Trap is designed to catch fruit flies, gnats, house flies, drain flies, fungus gnats, & non-biting midges. 
Can I use the Zevo Flying Insect Trap in spaces with kids, pets and/or near food? 
Yes! Zevo's U.S. device base meets third-party electrical safety standards. It is a durable, plug-in base requiring 120V/60Hz power and a standard North American plug. As with other electrical devices, keep away from children. 
How often should I change the cartridge? 
Replace the refill cartridge when it is full of insects. We recommend checking it every 2 weeks or as needed. 
Is there anywhere you should NOT keep your trap plugged in? 
Do not use the Zevo Flying Insect Trap outdoors where it might get wet. Avoid placing it near TV or computer screens, and keep it away from furniture, curtains, or corners that could block insects. If you can't see Zevo, insects can't see it either.

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I have tried: Zevo Flying Insect Traps (RECOMMEND this product)Husaco Store 2 Flying Insect Traps & 2 Cartridges - Compatible with ZEVOM364A RefillKatchy Flying Insect Trap IndoorTerro Fruit Fly Traps - (RECOMMEND)I tried several products for my recent gnat/fruit fly/super tiny demons from hell infestation. I really wanted to like some, but basically overall the ZEVO was superior. Thankfully I bought that one initially, but did not realize how severe my infestation got (as I was away from home for abit). Zevo pretty immediately and quickly toned down the flying creatures, and I noticed quickly the trap filling up with more and more. A good week in it was basically all covered up.I ended up getting a total of 4 Zevo traps, in certain areas I knew would fly to at night. Night is very good at getting them. I think it helps having no other lights on  either. I will mention I also got Terro fly trap, and they are just lil tiny apple looking containers that you fill with some nasty vinegar mixture. I fill it up just a small amount, because honestly that is enough and I can keep refilling it. One I had to dump because of so many dead bodies and I refilled it up.I put one of those in my fridge just incase. And some near the other traps to try to attract them faster to the Zevo's light of death.The Katchy and offbrand zevo aka Husaco store traps, just basically didn't trap as many.... Like say a week went by and I could count the flies it was a handful. They just weren't as amazing/fast for me.I had such a drastic reduction with 2 Zevo's and now that I got 4 going, I think they might finally be eradicated. (With the Terro assists. I liked putting them by the sinks.---EDIT ---So. I was wrong. I noticed bugs before I went on another weekend trip and it was declining to a point I thought they were gone and with me being gone they were just able to breed more. This product still worked, But I needed more products for the war against the gnats.To start off. I have pets. And there is water bowl sources, litter boxes. etc.  I didn't seem to notice any issues or problems with the litter, thankfully did not need to make any changes there, maybe because I take care of it often enough.Products Used for the battle (Ill write recommended if I loved it) Over a course of 3 days I saw a drastic reduction and 4-5 days I'm having trouble finding any:More refill cartridges for this plug in -> Mistakenly thought the war ended, but these got full and I needed more.Dawn Platinum Powerwash Dish Spray, Dish Soap, Apple Scent, 16oz <- Recommended - good for dishes anyway. I sprayed inside the toilet after every flush. If they tried to breed in it, this stuff traps them and they cant fly or get out and they drown. This how you home make liquid traps, vinegar helps lure them to it. To be honest they didn't seem to care for the toilet, (which is kept shut). But I did this as precaution.Zevo Brand can spray - I stopped using because I needed alot, and it worked great kills on contact but gets used so fast, there is hardly enough in the can.  (Recommended Mighty spray below)  - also this is really expensive...Mighty Mint Peppermint Oil Insect Repellent Spray – Natural Indoor & Outdoor Bug Control for Spiders, Ants, Roaches, and More – Plant-Based, Extra-Strong Formula - 16 oz -> RECOMMENDED -> This has ALOT of spray. And it doesn't seem to kill IMMEDIATELY but wait 2-3 seconds and yeah, they stop moving. Unless you get a good clean direct close up shot. Was also nice, to spray them on the wall, they would immediately fall off.  PLUS you get to one on one gnats and take 'em down right where you see them.  I'd go around to windows and doors, and find them there. Pew, pew. Bit satisfying. And smell isn't so bad, but can be strong. In the heat of battle you might be overwhelmed. I'd take a break from the area, get a fan etc. Smell will dissipate eventually/gradually.Sticky traps, the yellow that has a tack and hangs from wall -40 Pack Fruit Fly Traps for Indoor, Fly Traps Outdoor, Sticky Fly Trap Strips Tape Killer Catcher Paper Ribbon, Flying Insect Trap for Bug, Gnat, Insect, Pest, Mosquito -> RECOMMENDED -> Good passive attack. These are useful in variety of spots. Gnats tend to scale the walls and go up to the ceiling where these bad boys come in handy.  I had one just in the wall not ceiling but high up so pets cant get to it. Was collecting quiet a few.TERRO Drain Insect Killer - T590 Drain Fly Killer Spray - Foam Treatment for Fruit Flies, House Flies, and CockroachesAlso Green Gobbler drain killer. -> (recommend)  I used the gobbler first, frankly I think both work and wil do the job. But this is a plus that it foams right up (not for long mind you) but  I did notice gnats gravitate towards it and immediately die.  What you need to do is use this stuff and then PLUG the holes. see below + more info.Aluvor Bathtub Stopper 2pcs Sink Stopper Tub Plug Bathroom Shower Drain Plug Silicone Bathtub Drain Plug Tub Drain Stopper Washbasin Stopper - RECOMMENDED - Now this is why I think I got away with one can of the foam. I didn't really need to reapply, but I did as precaution.  For sink, plug the tiny hole with tape, its a backup hole for overflow, most sinks should have it if not you are good. Then use the tiny one for the bathroom sink. And the other one for the tub. I closed off the sink and mostly used the bathroom sink as my main source for washing/cleaning things. I did notice a fly or two come out during this but they seem dazed and was easily able to hand kill them. I'd be ready to spray them down. After I was done and wasn't going to use a drain for awhile I'd foam it up again. So depending on this factor you might want more foam. (I didn't run out of the can yet, so can't say for sure). Either way if it is sealed up you have time to buy more if needed.No product found for kitchen sink drain blocker/stopper -> I had bought one but it didnt actually block. I ended up using a plastic throwaway cup and weighed it down with a can for good measure, pretty sure they don't have ant strength to come and go. And the other sink for disposal I had a sink stopper already which blocked that side completely. I sprayed the foam in both and tried to avoid using the sink.Wondercide - Fruit Fly Traps for Indoors - Fruit Fly Killer - Pet and People Safe - Made in USA & Plant Based - 5.4 oz - 2 Pack -> PLUS has a cover lid on top, pets might knock it over but can't step into it. I would see gnats crawling on top of it quite often. Just checked and imo these did as well as the raid one below. Double for less money I thought it was. And has a cover on it with holes for the bugs to go in.Raid Essentials Fruit Fly Trap for Indoors, Made with Essential Oils, Child and Pet Safe, 5.4 oz -> This worked it was catching ALOT. However my cat, stepped his back foot in it somehow, and it wasn't knocked over. I have no IDEA how this happened but I had to wash his leg off. This DID work well. You could see a layer of gnats forming on the top quickly.  - The expensiveness of this mixed with not being great around pets I can't recommend.I DO recommend those terro fruit fly traps that looks like red apples. Those are effective always have been. Thats my standard go to when I notice these usually from bananas. also not to expensive.Trash can with a LID -> I had ONE trash can with a LID so it was closed. I would use to throw stuff away in and took that outside often so they couldn't sneak in and have a party.  Hefty actually has one with a GOOD seal. However, I was unable to obtain the item as my local store had it in stock but it was damaged and had a hole on the side of the can.... So I settled for a regular lid that wasn't fully sealed but had a button to open it and I'd just close it after.  (Hefty Touch Lid  is the name). It was helpful for the cause. I think most have a trash can with a lid but if not it is highly RECOMMENDED.-- WHAT you need to check --Water sources, bowls etc. Pet bowls of water. pet FOUNTAINS. Need to be cleaned at least 1x every 24 hour period. Personally if I saw them sitting on anything I'd clean that immediately. I never saw them on the pet  fountain but when I  checked I saw so much in the filter it was gross. I didn't use the fountain at all until after I won the war. (Just too gross and I wouldn't feel comfortable using the same filter, so I opted to not use it at all).Look around for exploded water jugs you may have kept for emergencies. Those explode and leak all the time. Found out the hard way.After taking care of the drains and constant cleaning pet water sources, these guys disappeared. Finally.Bananas are no longer welcome.

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: Amazing and works even with ants
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: These work better than you’d think! So they not only attract flies but they make no sound and last really long. Down side is it even caught some geckos. Even ant are drawn to it and end up dying with it being by my dogs dog food. Super easy to use. Light weight. The shape allows for flat plugs to still fit next to it. Sticky enough to hold several bugs even with dog hair too. I live in Hawaii and there are a ton of bugs and ants are the biggest issue. Photos are after two weeks and still sticky.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on February 3, 2026

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: Well it doesn't get rid of the flu that is buzzing around your head, but usually overnight it will catch a fly or two.I've had this for a few weeks now and I'm impressed. We thankfully don't get tons of flies all of time, but then there are days when there are too many, especially on very hot days. I guess flies like air-conditioning! Lol!Anyway a fly swatter can only get slow flies and this gets more, but it takes a dark kitchen (very little to no lights) and then a the pesky thing not bothering you anymore! At 1st I didn't think it was working, but then I checked each one and was happily surprised. About a week to a week and a half, I checked them both. Here are my findings.- The unit is by our partly glass back door and bordering the kitchen and the den. It only had 2 flies. It is plugged in under our bay window and is about 5 feet from a lamp that stays on all night.  Vevo says light will possibly deter flies from the catcher. You see, this uses an ultra violet light with a cover, so if you have other light sources then the fly may go to those lights.- The one in the kitchen, on an upper plug above a counter, that is almost in a corner and is not too far from the covered kitchen trash can. (I have a big dog that I don't want to get into the trash.) This unit had 6 flies! So the darker area and nearer to the trashcan (fly restaurant), lured more flies to it and removed the little pests from around me and my family.This is much better than fly tape that is nasty and looks disgusting. And I don't have to touch the sticky area or the flies in order to dispose of them. That said, I'm waiting until more flies come to their demise before tossing the liner. And the liners are expensive! Admittedly they are on a frame with a holder, which is what makes this sanitary and way less icky. So to me that makes it worth the price. Now these are not decorative, but they are not bad looking either. The soft light they cast onto the wall is kinda nice. And I'll only use these in the summer and put them away during the winter, so that's cost effective.So what is my verdict?2 Vevo units, together with a fly swatter in my kitchen is all I need during the summer to remove annoying flies from my kitchen. I highly recommend this product! It gets rid of flies while I sleep, so there are less or all gone the next day. That said, when the back door opens to let the dogs in and out (all day long), then more flies arrive and I'm glad these Vevo units are in my house!
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on June 25, 2024

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: My sister recommended zero to me for my issue with gnats. If your home gets a lot of sun or day light during the day, you may think that the zero is not going to work. Do not be discouraged. The zero does its best work during the night time hours. I plugged them in during the day and there were no gnat in the traps. I woke the next morning to my traps being full of gnats. I gave it four stars because during the day the gnats were an annoyance, however after two nights I saw fewer gnats during the day. Very pleased. There is no smell and its easy to set up. I recommend plugging in a open space like a kitchen as I did.

features: 🪰 Eliminate Fruit Flies, Period: Don't tolerate infestations. Our thick, citronella-infused gel immediately kills flies upon contact, giving you an instant, lasting defense against winged nuisances.

🪰 Engineered with Precision, Natural Ingredients: Achieve commercial-grade results without the chemicals. Utilizing highly-concentrated citronella, this gel tackles even the toughest fly populations while keeping your space fresh.

🪰 Universal Compatibility, Targeted Impact: From sinks to disposals, to commercial drains, this gel is the all-in-one answer. Created for universal drain compatibility, no matter the pipe material or construction.

🪰 Versatile: From Homes to Five-Star Establishments: Exceptional for household drains, yet robust enough for commercial settings like restaurants, hotels, and grocery stores. If you have a drain, we have your fly solution.
🪰 Uncompromising Safety Standards: Created for both effectiveness and peace of mind. Our gel is rigorously lab-tested to ensure it's non-toxic, biodegradable, and food-safe.
🪰 100% Satisfaction Money-Back Guarantee: If you're not completely satisfied with our commercial-grade formula, we'll refund your money, no questions asked. Your satisfaction is our priority.

aPlusContent.title: Product description
aPlusContent.rawText:
Product description
For All Household and Commercial Drains 
Ideal for kitchen sinks, bathroom drains, garbage disposals, and floor drains — keeps pipes clear and stops fruit and drain flies at the source. 
Commercial Grade Power 
Engineered to tackle large-volume drain systems yet easy to apply in a standard household kitchen, this thick gel delivers the kind of deep-reach results you’d expect in a commercial setting. Treat sinks, garbage disposals and floor drains with one professional-strength application that fits seamlessly into your home maintenance routine. 
Citronella Infused 
Citronella oil powers the formula, helping to dissolve organic buildup and eliminate the conditions where flies breed. 
Targets both surface fruit flies and hidden drain flies, reaching deep into sinks, disposals, and floor drains. 
Thick gel clings to pipe walls for lasting coverage, working quickly to clear buildup and stop fly activity at the source. 
Professional Grade Protection 
Built for demanding environments, this formula delivers powerful results in restaurants, hotels, and grocery stores — yet it’s easy to use for routine home maintenance, too.

aPlusContent.title: From the manufacturer
aPlusContent.rawText:
From the manufacturer
Green Strike Fly Traps 
Simply Relax and Enjoy a Fly Free Space! 
FAST & EFFECTIVE 
All you need to do is add water to the attractant lure and place the trap in a sunlit area.

items.title: Safety Information

items.title: Directions
items.text: Pour 4 oz of product down the drain every 24 hours until flies disappear. Use weekly as a prevention plan.

items.title: Legal Disclaimer
items.text: Statements regarding dietary supplements have not been evaluated by the FDA and are not intended to diagnose, treat, cure, or prevent any disease or health condition.

features: A Natural Fruit Fly Solution: Our non-toxic gel formula is safe for use around food and anywhere in the home, kitchen, bar, or restaurant.

Drain Flies, Sewer Flies, Gnats, or Fruit Flies, any flies like to be in drain scum found in drains, pipes, or garbage disposals. This natural formula cleans out the scum from the drains, and it is safe for your plumbing.

Home & commercial use: Our natural gel contains additional scum-busting properties, making this the most effective solution. Pour into a drain; this fast-acting treatment breaks up and digests drain scum.
Occasional use of this gel treatment will keep your drains odor and scum free. By preventing the buildup of organic scum, you remove the risk of another problem.

What to Expect: Some users commented that after the first treatment, they saw more flies. This is to be expected and means the product is working. By removing the drain scum, the flies leave. Please don't give up. Continue to treat as directed. You will see a decrease over the next few days or so.

productOverview.value: Removes Clog, Eliminates Drain Flies, Keeps Drains Odor Free

aPlusContent.title: Product description
aPlusContent.rawText:
Product description
How do I know if the flies are living in my drain? 
If you don't have any food lying around and the flies are the most concentrated in your kitchen and or bathroom, then chances are they are living and breeding in your drains. 
How much do I need? 
Generally speaking... 
- 32 oz - treats one drain 
- 64 oz - treats 2 drains 
- 1 gallon - treats 4 drains 
- 2 gallons - treats 8 drains 
How long does it take to work? 
You'll see a reduction in flies after 2 to 3 days. They should be all gone after 5 to 7 days. Complete elimination in sever cases can take up to 2 weeks. 
Is this safe to use if I am on a septic system> 
Yes, this is septic safe. 
Will the flies come back? 
If the biofilm returns, they can come back. We recommend treating your drain every two weeks as a preventative measure. 
EcoStrong's Drain & Septic Solutions

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I moved recently and started to notice gnats flying around. I put out traps and suddenly realized how bad the problem was, as I was also growing veggies indoors. It got to the point where I was trying to shoo them away while I was on zoom calls ( I wfh)! None of the home remedies were working so I decided to try this product since it had good reviews. I wasn’t even aware they could live in your drains but not anymore!

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: It really works! (Updating my rating from 5 to 3)
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: Ok I was weary about all these products and definitely felt overwhelmed. After the reviews and research I landed on this one. And listen, BUY IT! Skip the bleach, skip the drain cleaners at the store, skip the bug sprays. This truly works and I’m so thrilled that I’m getting my moneys worth! I am on day 4 of treating my drains and I see such a drastic change. Before this product I was overwhelmed with drain gnats, killing 6-7 just on every bathroom visit, getting harassed by gnats sitting on the toilet and brushing my teeth. This is day 4 at 2:50pm Eastern time lol I have only seen 1 gnat and it was struggling to move so it was easy to kill it. I’m not being dramatic and I swear I wasn’t paid for this review. This product works and I’m happy I found something that finally does. I spent hundreds on other products just the past two weeks and had I known, I would’ve bought this to start. BUY IT, STOCK IT, TELL EVERYONE! Please don’t change the formula because this is perfect! I will buy another gallon soon, but I still have about half of my gallon left after treating 8 drains for just 3 days so far. I love it thank you all so much, this has truly changed my household. I’m going to recommend this to everyone!Update:Ok so the gnats clearly haven’t went anywhere because I am back to killing multiple per bathroom visit. It’s so overwhelming, I’m going to have to try another product. I followed the directions and did the treatment everyday for 5 straight days, I didn’t do it yesterday and it’s like I never did it at all. It’s overwhelming to say the least.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on September 24, 2024

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I only used it twice but it is really working,therefore if I see the drain flies in the sink I just pour some fruit fly drain  treatment around the drain and this kills  and preventd the drain flies from flying  around the house, because  it kill them before they come out of drain. I did all the drains in my house once a week if any activity in between just pour some around that drain. I will definitely buy again
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on February 13, 2026

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: Fly stickers ALONE were worth the purchase !!
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I got these and put the fly stickers on all my drains.  I had been using "Green Gobbler for Fruit & Drain Flies" for about 3-4 days in my drains with NO noticeable difference in the fly population in my house!  I put these stickers on my drains and the next morning I got up and there were NO flies in my house!  But guess what? There weren't any stuck to the fly stickers either.  It's been three days with my drains plugged.  A plug in the bathroom tub, stopper pulled up in the bathroom sink, one fly sticker still stuck to one drain in my kitchen double sink and I just keep the drain stopper in the other sink so I still have a sink in the kitchen to use. Still not a single fly on the sticker, but no flies in the house either.  I wanted to see just how long I'd be 'fly free' doing this.  -So far, 3 days with NO flies AND I still haven't put the EcoStrong drain treatment in the drains! So, the drains ARE the problem.  At first I didn't think so.  I never saw a fly come out of the drains -ever! I live in a duplex and my sinks, tub and probably toilet all drain to a center drain pipe in the common wall that I and my next door neighbor share.  I'm sure she's been inundated with flies.  Those little buggers are smart!  Not one was stuck to the fly stickers but the fact that I had NO flies the next day after putting them on before going to bed and then got up to a fly free house tells me that's where they're coming from.  If I even go get a fly swatter and sit back down at my desk in front of my living room window the flys would vanish.  They KNEW I had that swatter.  They would hid behind my desk calendar or along the edge of my laptop on my desk. -anywhere they could find that I couldn't get at them, so not surprised there was none on the fly stickers.  They knew better.  They just went down the other side of the drain to my neighbors house!  If I didn't get anything but the stickers with this purchase it would have been worth the $36 to me to find this out!  I spent $250 on fly zappers, fly trap bags and other crap and NONE of it worked! -I even took clear packing tape and sealed all my windows in my house shut thinking that's where they were coming in, under the weatherstripping and was getting ready to replace all my window's weatherstripping!  Thank you EcoStrong for the stickers!! I'm gonna use the drain treatment to treat my drains. -As soon as I get another sink stopper to seal the other side of my kitchen sink so I can take the sticker off!  -And I'm gonna buy a gallon and some stickers for my next door neighbor.  With our drains being in common we're gonna have to tackle this together to get rid of these buggers.  But if she won't be diligent and do her side, I'm just gonna keep treating my side and keep my drains closed unless I'm right there running water into them. It's the only solution I can figure out, but at least, thank God! I HAVE a solution that works now!UPDATE:  I used the Eco Strong Drain treatment in all four of my house drains, kitchen sinks, bathroom sink and tub for 8 straight days before I went to bed. I put a strip of blue painters tape across my kitchen sink so I wouldn't accidently forget I treated the drains and use the sink.  I pulled up the bathroom sink stopper & put a small mixing bowl in the bathroom sink so I could wash my hands and run water from that faucet into the mixing bowl, then dumped the bowl into the toilet next to the sink and set it back in the sink for reuse.  That  little system worked pretty well for the 8 day treatment so that it wasn't much hassle not being able to use the sinks for about 10-12 hours during the night.  After 8 days I'm pretty much fly free. I bought a gallon with the stickers for my next door neighbor and gave it to her to use, since our sinks connect to the same drain pipe.  I live in a duplex and we have a common wall so our kitchens and bathrooms back up to each other and use a common drain pipe to the septic tank.  I'm not sure how faithful and diligent she's been on her side but she told me a couple days ago she only has a few flys now instead of the hoards of flys we were both dealing with so I think this stuff works.  I'm going to continue once a week treatments through fly season and keep my sink and tub stoppers in place when I'm not running water into them.  Can't think of anything else to do and the treatments drastically reduced the flys.  Wish it was a 'zero fly zone' but those little buggers are smart.  I'm looking for and closing any remaining entrances they may be getting in from. Fixing to put up a split door screen on my front door.  I think some are getting in that way.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on May 18, 2024

productPageReviewsFromOtherCountries.reviewDescription: I have had a fruit fly problem for a little while now and have tried several options to fix it. features: Catch Flying Insects 24/7 - This flying insect trap uses blue and UV light to attract and catch house flies, fruit flies and gnats. Its continuous attraction helps it catch insects day and night.
Mess-Free Disposal - This indoor fly trap and gnat catcher uses a powerful adhesive backing you will never have to touch. The adhesive pad can discreetly catch bugs, and discreetly faces the wall.

aPlusContent.title: Product description
aPlusContent.rawText:
Product description
What is the Zevo Flying Insect Trap? 
The Zevo Flying Insect Trap features a durable plug-in base that uses blue and UV light to attract insects to a disposable refill cartridge. Insects get stuck on an adhesive pad in the cartridge, which is easy to remove and dispose of. 
What type of flying insects does it catch? 
The Zevo Flying Insect Trap is designed to catch fruit flies, gnats, house flies, drain flies, fungus gnats, & non-biting midges. 
Can I use the Zevo Flying Insect Trap in spaces with kids, pets and/or near food? 
Yes! Zevo's U.S. device base meets third-party electrical safety standards. It is a durable, plug-in base requiring 120V/60Hz power and a standard North American plug. As with other electrical devices, keep away from children. 
How often should I change the cartridge? 
Replace the refill cartridge when it is full of insects. We recommend checking it every 2 weeks or as needed. 
Is there anywhere you should NOT keep your trap plugged in? 
Do not use the Zevo Flying Insect Trap outdoors where it might get wet. Avoid placing it near TV or computer screens, and keep it away from furniture, curtains, or corners that could block insects. If you can't see Zevo, insects can't see it either.

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: Omg what can I say?! I was so skeptical. This thing WORKS!
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I know how helpful pictures are for believability, so I’ll attach one! Don’t judge me, I had no idea what these were for the first couples of weeks. So yes they overran my house for a bit. But that is no longer! We are now free from the clutches of these nasty little 💩’s.I recently moved to Florida, and never in my life had I experienced so many (what I thought were gnats - I’m still unsure what the hell they actually are 😂). For the life of me I couldn’t figure out where the swarms of these pesky little things were coming from. I am originally from up north, where the only things that resemble them that we get are fruit flies. I couldn’t figure out why they were everywhere when none of my fruit was rotten or even remotely bad. Until I did some googling and found out these little demons are called Drain Flies. They live and breed in your drain, lay eggs and come out in SWARMS. I tried every remedy to kill them off. I cleaned my drains, I poured all kinds of chemicals down there. I googled every which way to kill their eggs, catch them. Tried the whole apple cider vinegar, water and soap trick. Where the apple cider vinegar is supposed to attract them, and the surface tension of the soap is supposed to get them stuck and they sink into the water below….noooope! Nothing worked. I’d still see them flying all over the place in my peripheral vision, and to top it off I now had a nasty little vinegar cup hidden in my corner smelling like dooky. I, like every other rational person, ran to Amazon for a solution. And I found LOTS of them. My first go to was going to be that light and fan thing, where the light attracts them and the fan supposedly sucks them in to their demise. I really didn’t care which one I purchased, as long as it worked. I saw the reviews and pictures of this one and figured I’d give it a go, and if this one didn’t work, I’d try the other one I mentioned.Day 1, the first few hours, it caught absolutely nothing. And I thought “greaaat. This thing isn’t going to catch anything and I’m gong to have to deal with this pests for another few days while I wait on another order”Day 2. I woke up, not expecting to find anything, and lo and behold, there were 10-15 flies on it. My prayers had been answered!Day 3. Not going to lie, I forgot I had it plugged inDay 4. I checked again, and this is what I found. The picture attached. Absolutely gross 🤢. Don’t ask, I have no idea how all these nasty little creatures lived in my sink without my knowledge. I must just be that blissfully unaware. I thought I only had a few…clearly I was wrongI’m on like Day 10 now. I waited awhile because I wanted to make sure it continued to work before posting this review. I haven’t seen another fly / gnat besides in the trap, since Day 4, nearly a week ago. Not one. However, the trap is still lit up, and still as sticky as it was on day 1.There is a faint blue glow, it’s a pretty pleasant. It’s just like a night light. It makes 0 noise whatsoever. It’s SO easy to remove and replace the sticky pad. You pull the stick piece up out of the part that attaches into the wall, and slide a new one in. So simple, yet so effective. If you’ve had these nasty little critters flying around your home for far too long now, try it. You won’t regret it. If it doesn’t work in the first spot you put it, move it around. These things supposedly come out of the drains, yet for some reason it catches far more in my bedroom outside the bathroom. So find what works for you. Also, it turns sideways if need be. So if you have a sideways plug, you can still plug it in and swivel it so it’s face up!I hope this review helps some of you choose this, and rid your life of these nasty little buggers!!
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on October 19, 2024

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: Thought seeing dead bugs would be gross but it was actually strangely satisfying. HIGHLY RECOMMEND!
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I’ll be honest: the idea of seeing all the dead bugs that this would catch really turned me off the idea of using this product.  However, after trying basically everything else out there, I felt I had no choice.It worked AMAZINGLY WELL. Extremely happy to not be fighting fruit flies anymore, especially since I could never find out where they were coming from. This also caught one spider, one fly and even a few mosquitoes.In the end, I have finally replaced the very first sticky trap sheet (6 weeks initially). Even counted more than 50 “trapped” bugs. Surprisingly, though I expected to be horrified to see the bugs, I was actually really happy; happy to see all the dead bugs and especially happy to be rid of the bugs!!I highly recommend and very satisfied!

aiReviewsSummary.text: Customers find the fly swatter effective at killing mosquitoes and fruit flies, and appreciate its ease of use and good value for money. The device attracts even the tiniest pests, with one customer noting it eradicated all flies in their house. The battery life and zapping sound receive mixed feedback - while some say the charge lasts for hours, others report it doesn't last long, and while some love the zapping sound, others find it absurdly loud. The product's lifespan is concerning, with many reporting it stops working within two months.

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I love these guys!  I live in Puerto Rico and have doors and windows wide open all day and night.  While bugs aren’t a huge problem, we do get our share of small annoying critters.  These bad boys catch and zap the ones I can’t get with my hands.  The zapping is loud but they are otherwise silent.  There’s no odor and you can have them be plugged in and always on or use them manually.  My only criticism is that the purple light is dim and only at the bottom of the screen, otherwise they work like a champ!  Easy to use and good quality!
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on January 21, 2026

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: Great for fungus gnats so far.
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I got these for fungus gnats and they work great.  They attract them in auto mode and I can strike them down as necessary in manual mode.  They said they were fully charged out of the box but did not last very long in auto mode when I let it sit for a few hours.  Hopefully the battery just wasn't full and they will last longer than that.  I only left the house for 4 or 5 hours and they were off by the time I returned.My initial review complained about the USB cables but I think I was getting coil whine from the USB hub they were plugged into.  Now if that could be caused by the cables somehow, I don't know but I'm not an expert.  All I know is I plugged these 2 in to charge in a USB hub I've had for years and the thing squeals loudly until I unplug specifically the cables that came with these zappers.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on January 12, 2026

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I have struggled with the common, battery powered yellow rackets that last about a year if lucky.  This one has a rechargeable battery.  It also has a stand and a night-light function that attracts flying insects.  The surface area is large and even the tiniest fruit-flies wont escape.  It is an attractive design with several settings.  Do you have pantry moths?  Flies?  Mosquitos? It makes short work of them with a resounding crack, and you dont have to hit them twice. Fruit flies sound like popcorn!  I cannot comment on its effectiveness on a larger insect. I like the design and it feels good in the hand.  Easy to use and effective.  Stays charged for quite a while. Hope it lasts.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on November 12, 2025

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: When the gnats ‘hit sounds like fireworks going off
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I purchased these about 2 weeks ago. I have gnats and they really are annoying.  These work great for them.  They have a stand that is used to charge it up, but also to have it stand upright after charging.  This is helpful because you do not have hold it all the time.The first night I charge them. I had one by my plants and it sound was like fire crackers going off. That sound made me smile as I knew it was working. Can’t wait to try them camping.The reason for 4 stars instead of five is that I feel like the charge should last longer.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on January 24, 2026

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: Star Wars level light saber. The smell of burining bugs permeates my home office because I leave the door open and a lot of mosquitoes fly in. I leave it in the active mode on the stand and even if it's not on the charger, it lasts several hours and zaps bugs without my effort. (I leave it not plugged in because I broke a charging cable in my eagerness to kill a bug flying by). You just have to pick it up and wave it at the bug and it satisfyingly fries it as it flies by. (Frying a flying fly is so satisfying).  This thing is probably 2.5 times the surface area of an average hand, but according to Austin Powers, maybe 3.5 times that of a carney's hands. It is actually well made. The stand is not a charging stand...that would be nice...and the stand is lightweight so it's a top-heavy arrangement and prone to tip over in anything beyond a Beaufort 4. Highly recommend.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on February 3, 2026

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: Wonderful zapper. Old place had many fruit flies and was hard to get rid of. Got this and it zapped so many in the bathroom and near the patio. The zapping sound is quite loud. The light, if kept on automatic, is quite bright so be aware of that if you keep it in the bedroom. Otherwise it's a great night light for the bathroom or hallway. However, I have to say, at my current place the fruit flies here must've had a brain rewired and became smarter because they've been ignoring the zappers pretty well. The only issue I had was when one of the zapper's metal somehow bent and touched the other side it was making a different buzzing sound and was a bit scary. I didn't know if I should not use it or what. Haven't taken the chance to bend it back to see if that would fix the problem. Anyways, I love that it comes with the racket holder so I can just place it there without having to swing it all the time. Like how stylish it is. It had other family members asking and purchasing themselves some. Got my grandmother one of these too so she didn't have to swing it around. Overall, it's one of the best purchases I've made on Amazon. Hoping it lasts for years to come.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on October 27, 2025

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I saw a video for this device, and it piqued my curiosity.  I bought this one and another brand to compare the features.  I have to say, this one is the winner, hands down.  One of the features that makes it superior was that you do not have to hold down the button to activate the electric. Unlike a fly swatter, you do not swat at bugs, but just gently wave it around when a bug is flying near you.  The bugs get bopped and zapped. By not needing to hold the button you don't have to find the button when you need it quickly.  The other benefit is that you can keep the device active on its stand so if any fly into it, it takes care of them. They do not seem to be attracted to it, so it is just by chance that they fly into it.  I set it on the table next to me in case they are lured in by my breath.  I live in the country where mosquitoes and gnats are common this time of year and although I have a regular zapper, this is actually more effective - because not all bugs go for the zapper, but most will go for a human's breath. The other feature that I think makes it superior is that the grid pattern is very small.  Although the one with the wider grid does effectively take down small bugs, like gnats, I think this one gets more. It definitely doesn't eliminate every bug - but it definitely helps. As with other brands, it does have a usb charging feature.  I specifically chose that because replacing batteries did not sound like something I would want to deal with.  It holds a charge for quite a while - enough for me to spend a few hours outside, for several days, before it needs charging.  It takes a few hours to charge.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on June 13, 2025

features: Reusable Design : This fruit fly trap can be rinsed with water and used again without losing effectiveness. A practical solution for multiple uses

Safe and Non-Toxic: This fruit fly catcher trap uses fruit as bait to catch fruit flies, making it a gentle yet effective solution for your home. It's designed to be safe for use around food, kids and pets, making it a worry-free solution for your family

Easy to Use and Monitor: Simply open the cap, add a few pieces of banana/wine/apple cider vinegar and a few drops of dish soap, then place the trap where fruit flies gather. The clear design lets you monitor results at a glance—watch as the flies are lured in by the bright yellow funnel (optimized to attract their vision) and get trapped inside

Sticky-and-Go Discreet Placement: The included low-profile adhesive strips allow for quick, no-mess setup—just peel and stick. Leaves no residue and blends seamlessly into your space. Suitable for discreet placement near trash bins, refrigerators, sinks, dining tables, or other smooth surfaces

Indoor Use: This fruit fly trap is suitable for indoor spaces like kitchens, dining areas, pantries, bedroom, baby house. Its compact design allows for discreet placement on countertops, near trash cans, by sinks, on windowsills, or close to fruit bowls—effectively targeting fruit flies without getting in the way

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: If you use my method this is an excellent fruit fly trap
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: So these work great, especially if you add the sticky fly traps to them as you can see in the photos. What you do is, you take a couple of the sticky fly traps and take scissors and cut them down to size, as you can see in the photos, and then take some Scotch tape and simply tape them to the trap.Because I've noticed that even with a piece of banana and apple cider vinegar in the trap, many fruit flies will simply walk around on the rim of the trap and not actually enter it, it seems many fruit flies are very hesitant to actually go in there.So easy solution, just go buy a pack of sticky fly traps, here on Amazon you can get like 40 of them for 6 bucks. And take a pair of scissors and cut them down to size (you don't cut the sticky part you're cutting the non-sticky part) and simply tape them to the trap, and you'll notice many of the fruit flies will land on it thinking it's a landing strip when in reality it's their doom lol.I just tuck it away there against the wall and turn it diagonally so only the two sticky traps are presenting to the fruit flies when they approach. It kind of forces them to land on it.They think it's a landing strip when in reality It's a trap!!!It can be quite fun, I'll actually watch a fruit fly approach it (cause you know the scent of apple cider vinegar and banana they can't resist that) and hover around a bit, and then land on the sticky trap, and I'll laugh when it happens.I'll probably leave the banana and apple cider vinegar in the trap for maybe a week or two before I clean it out, and the sticky traps can be used for weeks on end before they need to be replaced with new ones. Eventually those sticky traps will completely fill up with fruit flies.You can just pull that yellow part up and out with the sticky traps, and then clean the container, put fresh apple cider vinegar (and just a tiny drop of dish soap) and banana in it, and then stick the yellow part back in. So this is my method.So by using my method, this is an excellent fruit fly trap.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on October 15, 2025

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: These are excellent fruit fly traps, but don't put fruit in them. Put some yeast, some sugar and a drop of dish soap in some warm water, mix it up and pour to about a half-inch deep, and the flies will mob this trap like crazy. Very effective and easy to clean out.

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: They trap a lot of those little buggers. A drop of dish soap and apple cider vinegar to just a little below the bottom of the funnel does it. The ACV draws them in, and the dish soap reduces the surface tension of the vinegar, so they drown when they hit it. I keep the cap nearby so I can sneak up and trap the stragglers that haven't found or are too smart to use the funnel. then it's off to the deep freeze for a bit and repeat the cycle. You don't even need to re-bait every time. (the dead frozen bugs act like decoys for the next batch!)

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I had an absolutely HORRIBLE infestation of fruit flies... and ordered two different (but similar in the concept of the bottle trap) brands.  This one, SUPER NINJA, worked the best.  The design of it's inverted yellow cone is superior to the other brand.  I can NOT understand other reviewers saying this does not work... because it DOES !!  It took a day or two... I think the odor intensified... but then it started to litererally fill up with the fruit flies.  It does NOT work for gnats... but they make that clear.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on December 4, 2025

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: Super Ninja Fruit Fly Traps: bait useless for my fruit flies while Terro brand bait has worked well.
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I tried 3 different brands of liquid fruit fly attractant: in this home for 25 yrs but had never had a fruit fly problem in my life, they must have come in w/fruit which we don't buy a lot of: found out one female lays 500 eggs...This product, Super Ninja Fruit fly traps- I put several of these pre-filled bottles out for days but SUPER NINJA FRUIT FLY TRAPS DID NOT CATCH A SINGLE FRUIT FLY, compared to Terro brand used in the red plastic tomatoes, which worked well. I even tried the Super Ninja bait in the red Terro plastic tomatoe containers as some ads claimed the color + shape of the vessel played a role in attracting bugs but still no luck. The excellent Terro brand even worked well in a clear glass baby food jar.Terro works well, I see several of the fruit flies in the liquid. However, the tomatoe shaped plastic holders can tip easy, its a sticky, stinky liquid for which the company in South America would not tell me if it has a percent of poison in it- many of their products do, + they only give the number of poison control, + insist that the formula is 'proprietary information'. But after trying 3 brands, will stick w/Terro even though it does not sell a large container of liquid (I do not need more plastic tomatoe holders  plus glass baby food jars worked just as well)Have put the Terro liquid bait-filled plastic tomatoes in clear plastic cups (clear so insects could see the red fruit shape), as the tomatoes have many preferations, which is good, but they are light-weight, tip + spill easily.However, the flat rectangular shape of the bottles of the Super Ninja Fruit fly Traps, though the liquid they came with is useless, the bottles w/their flat walls can be attached to any flat surface or wall w/a sticky strip; That had been my intent when I ordered them so I did not have to worry about containers spilling or space to put them down in or my cat getting to one... As the Ninja contents have proved useless, I will empty + rinse them out so I can fill them w/Terro attractant. will report if the insects will use these glass containers.While I had planned to take advantage of the flat walls of the Ninja glass containers + a photo in the Ad may have suggested they could be stuck to a wall, I was surprised that they came w/a pre-attached silicone sticky strip pre-attached for that purpose (one should use a spatula to remove the sticky strip and bottle intact from the wall (they are silicone w/adhesive + residue can be easily removed).There was one other brand of 25 vials which also did not catch a single fruit fly- even when used in Terro's tomatoe shape, which I also do not recommend, 'Qualirey 20 Pack Fruit Fly Trap Refill Liquid'...Do not recommend the Super Ninja Fly Traps because the bait does not work, at least for my fruit flies in Connecticut; but I still need to substitute Terro liquid bait in their their rectangular glass containers a+ see if the insects will then use them before I formally recommend the containers themselves- they have a yellow funnel on top. However, $39.99 plus tax is a high price to pay for the 12 glass containers which I will need to empty out to use with Terro brand liquid- if the bugs will use that container w/Terro bait.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on November 14, 2025

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I have to be honest. When I saw that my wife ordered these with a rating of 3.7 stars I made fun of her. Well, I had to eat my words because they worked immediately. The smell is very strong initially but dissipated in about 20 minutes. I feel like the trap in the kitchen caught maybe 50 fruit flies. We have only used it for 2 days and I feel like I don't really see them anymore. Good product

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: These traps are very effective. Within just a few days there were many, many fruit flies dead in the bottle. Seems like they start losing their effectiveness after three weeks or so but they really do a great job in that time. I am a repeated customer.

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I was hoping that these would be my last ditch answer to a terrible fruit fly infestation that we cannot get a handle on. For just a few fruit flies, I’m sure these would be ideal, but still having to combine with other methods to get rid of so many.

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: The Super Ninja Fruit Fly Traps are just okay. They do catch some fruit flies, so they aren’t completely ineffective, but they don’t attract nearly as many as I expected. Even when I placed them right next to the problem areas, I still saw flies buzzing around that never went near the traps. They’re easy to set up and they do work to a degree, just not as strongly as the product description or other reviews made it seem. Overall, they’re fine in a pinch, but I wouldn’t call them highly effective
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on December 9, 2025

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: does not work in the bottle provided, you need to be smart to put this into a panera juice cup with little left out juice and add this solution to then cling wrap the whole juice cup with the cap. and add few holes around the center. that is it, it works like magic. spoted and captured all of the no see ums. i still have 5 bottles to use in future.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on December 29, 2025

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: This is 100% the best fruit fly trap on the market that I have used. We had a major infestation of fruit flies that we just could not get rid of no matter what we tried. Whether it was DIY Solutions or other fly traps we bought from the local stores. I found these on Amazon and they had good reviews and now I can understand why the reviews were so good. I had the fly traps spread out in several areas about 10 to 15 ft apart. If I had to say anything negative them, it would be there are so many flies in the traps that it must be creating a different odor because we still have a few lingering fruit flies that don't seem to want to go in.  I will be getting more to finish them off. If you have a fruit fly problem get these you will not be disappointed! Make sure you have fruit flies and not gnats because they won't work on gnats apparently.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on January 7, 2026

productPageReviewsFromOtherCountries.reviewTitle: Super Nija Fruit Fly Trap, pack of four bottles - excellent product.
productPageReviewsFromOtherCountries.reviewDescription: Super Nija Fruit Fly Trap, pack of four bottles. These were ordered on Mon 09.02.2026 and were delivered on Tues 10.02.2026.On Tues 10.02.2026 early evening, I opened one bottle and placed the opened bottle on the kitchen worktop. Within 48 hours all of the fruit flies in the kitchen were trapped inside the Super Nija Fruit Fly Trap bottle.A few of the fruit flies were trapped inside the Super Nija Fruit Fly Trap bottle during daylight hours. The vast majority of the fruit flies were trapped inside the Super Nija Fruit Fly Trap bottle overnight when the kitchen was dark.This excellent product is 100% effective.Would I order this product again if needed? Yes, definitely.
productPageReviewsFromOtherCountries.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United Kingdom on February 13, 2026

productPageReviewsFromOtherCountries.reviewDescription: So effective ! Don’t see single one now

productPageReviewsFromOtherCountries.reviewTitle: Effektive und einfache Lösung gegen Fruchtfliegen funktioniert gut
productPageReviewsFromOtherCountries.reviewDescription: Ich war zunächst skeptisch, ob eine so kleine Fliegenfalle wirklich etwas bringen kann. Die Super Ninja Fruchtfliegenfallen haben mich positiv überrascht. Schon nach etwa einem Tag waren erste Fliegen verschwunden und nach zwei bis drei Tagen war meine Küche deutlich sauberer. Besonders gut finde ich, dass der Lockstoff natürlich ist und nicht stark nach Chemie riecht. Das ist gerade dann wichtig, wenn Kinder oder Haustiere im Haushalt leben. Die Fallen lassen sich leicht aufstellen oder mithilfe der Klebefläche anbringen und benötigen kaum Platz. Ein kleiner Nachteil ist, dass die Wirkung bei sehr vielen Fliegen etwas nachlassen kann, weil die Behälter relativ schnell voll werden. Dann muss man die Fallen häufiger austauschen oder mehrere gleichzeitig nutzen. Insgesamt bin ich zufrieden. Als schnelle, sichere und unkomplizierte Lösung gegen Fruchtfliegen im Haushalt ist dieses Set sein Geld wert.

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: Draws a horrendous amount of flies...
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I bought this bait after having great success with the Victor M380 Reusable Fly Trap and the bait that comes with it (a great product for it's purpose as well). I just wanted to see if I could find a cheaper effective refill for the container. This stuff smells just as horrible and draws just as many flies. I live where the weather gets hot in the summer months and the trap fills in a week or two. However, on cooler days it doesn't seem to draw as many. Just be patient. It works when it matters. Having an inexpensive and effective replacement for the bait is great.For those reviewers complaining about the fly larvae, I expect that's normal and any hatched flies will be unable to escape the container, just like every other fly that enters the trap. Another word of advice, wear gloves if you are going to empty and reuse the trap. Soap will not remove the stench this bait leaves behind...
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on June 15, 2022

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: It will attract and kill flies
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: If you have flies you need this stuff. I had a serious fly problem and tried many different things that minimized the problem. After using this product I have NO live flies.  The jar will have about 3 inches of dead flies within days.  This stuff works.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on July 24, 2025

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: Stinks, but in the right way!
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: These are GREAT for reuseable traps! They stink to high heaven, which works very well in attracting flies. Easy to use, but I do suggest wearing gloves, or you'll find yourself washing your hands after handling. The attractant has a good lifespan, I actually had to refill the water several times before it no longer seemed to attract, or the container filled up, which was more common. Will definitely recommend and will be buying again!
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on June 27, 2025

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: Terrible compared to Rescue brand.I must not have looked at reviews, only compared ingredients. This one has about half the Rotten Egg solids than Rescue brand making it very weak. It may be effective in a small space, but outdoors it is doing poorly for me. I barely notice any smell.Two setup for about 4 days now, both would normally be packed with flies. One is completely empty and the other has less than a full layer floating.  The packets are easy to use but I am going to have to crack an egg in each to get the putrid smell going.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on November 7, 2025

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I’m filling entire fly traps to the brim each week. I’ve got 4 quart traps and 2 half gallons. These packets are enough to refill either size.Be aware- it’s rancid af, and takes a couple days of simmering out in the heat to get to its full pungency. Once it’s ripe, it brings them from near and far. Works great to keep the flies away from the chicken coop.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on June 10, 2024

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: Doesn’t attract flies like Rescue brand

title: TERRO Fruit Fly Traps for Indoors (4 Pack) + 180 Days of Lure Supply - T2503SR - Lure and Kill Indoor Fruit Flies Near Fruit, Trash Cans, Countertops - Ready to Use Trap - 180 Day Supply

items.title: Safety Information
items.text: Keep it out of the reach of children.

description: The Terro Fruit Fly Trap offers an effective answer for tackling fruit fly infestations indoors. Designed as an indoor fruitfly trap, this product quickly reduces the fruit fly population within a few days. Equipped with a long-lasting lure, each fly catcher provides up to 45 days of potency, providing over a month of protection. The non-staining, food-based liquid lure used in these anti fruit fly traps effectively attracts adult fruit flies, drawing them into the indoor fly trap where they remain trapped without escape. The apple-shaped design of these indoor Terro fly traps blends in with your apples and fruit, providing a discreet and attractive alternative to more noticeable traditional traps. The traps come with built-in windows, making it easy to monitor catches and check lure levels. For optimal effectiveness, place these traps near known fruit fly breeding locations such as fruit bowls, trash cans, kitchen counters, and sinks. By strategically locating and targeting these areas, homeowners can more effectively reduce the fruit fly population. Traps 7x more fruit flies with no mess based on the number of fruit flies caught in a 24 hour period in a competitive test with the TERRO Fruit Fly Trap vs the Hot Shot Flying Insect Trap. Eliminate the nuisance of swarming fruit flies with these effective fruitfly trap indoor products, keeping your home clean and without pests.
features: FAST-ACTING ELIMINATION: Notice a dramatic decrease in fruit flies within a few days with these indoor fly traps for your home, containing a fast-acting anti fruit fly trap lure

LONG-LASTING PROTECTION: Each ready-to-use, non-staining TERRO fruit fly lure offers up to 45 days of effective trapping, making it a great indoor fruit fly killer. Comes with 180 days worth of lure supply

DISCREET AND ATTRACTIVE DESIGN: The apple-shaped anti fruit fly trap design of this indoor fly trap blends in with the fruits these pests love, keeping unsightly fruit fly populations down without attracting attention
EASY MONITORING: This fruit fly catcher features built-in windows to allow for at-a-glance monitoring of the lure levels and catches
TARGET BREEDING AREAS: These indoor fruit fly traps are most effective when placed near fruit bowls, trash cans, and counters to control fruit fly breeding locations

aPlusContent.title: From the manufacturer
aPlusContent.rawText:
From the manufacturer
TERRO Fruit Fly Traps 
Have you spotted fruit flies swarming around your kitchen or garbage can? It's a familiar problem for most homeowners. With the TERRO Fruit Fly Trap, you can get the best of these annoying household pests in no time! This fast-acting trap uses a non-staining liquid bait to lure adult fruit flies inside. Once the flies enter the trap, they cannot escape to continue breeding and multiplying. The trap also features an attractive apple-shaped design that will look at home in any kitchen. 
Say Goodbye to Fruit Flies 
Attracts & Traps 
The food-based lure attracts adult fruit flies to the trap. Flies that enter the trap through one of the many funnel-shaped holes can't escape to continue breeding. 
Easy to Use 
Just add the lure to the trap and place it near fruit fly breeding areas, such as near fruit bowls, kitchen sinks, dishwashers, garbage cans, and counters. 
Fewer Fruit Flies in Days 
TERRO Fruit Fly Trap works fast! In a matter of days, you should begin to notice a dramatic decrease in the number of fruit flies around your home. 
Attractive Apple Design 
Gone are the days of unsightly traps taking up space inside your home! This apple-shaped trap will look at home in any kitchen, while blending in with the fruit these pests love! 
Convenient Fruit Fly Control 
No Mess 
The non-staining liquid lure stays contained inside the trap to keep your home mess-free. 
Easy Monitoring 
The trap features a built-in window, allowing you to keep an eye on catches and lure levels. 
Long Lasting 
Each lure pack lasts up to 45 days to provide weeks of protection against fruit flies.

The product receives positive feedback for its quality, with one customer noting it's the best gnat and house fly trap. Functionality and catchability receive mixed reviews - while some say it works quickly and catches all flies, others report it doesn't work at all or catch anything. Similarly, value for money and longevity are debated, with some finding it well worth the money and lasting 75 days, while others consider it a waste of money that doesn't last 90 days. The smell aspect is also mixed, with some appreciating that it has no odor while others note it smells like vinegar.
