productPageReviews.reviewDescription: It got a few fruit flies in a day! We use to have fruit fly issue every spring by the fruit tree in the backyard. These traps helped keep them by the backdoor entryway. I have not seen an issue in the kitchen since.The trap looks pretty, is easy to use and clean, durable for reuse, and effective.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on February 2, 2026

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: Someone gave us a bag of ripe fruit and all of a sudden we had fruit flys everywhere! I read a bunch of reviews and ordered these Terro fruit fly traps and another product that contained a blue light with sticky paper. After one week the fruit fly problem was resolved. The Terro trap was full of fruit flys and the other was not. I attached photos of the results. No more fruit flys in the kitchen - amazing result! I did learn a couple of things. I had set up all 4 traps and noticed that one trap got all the action even though it was not near the counter where the flys were most active. I realized that the active trap was the one that I had handled the least, and it wasn’t near the counter that I sprayed and cleaned daily. I think the counter cleaner mist must have gotten on the other devices reducing their efficiency.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on January 24, 2026

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: Mostly effective, and for German roach nymphs
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: These have been somewhat effective. While I have seen less of these pests when I've got this active, I've also seen them literally just hangout on top of the apple. It's like it can see its dead incestuous disgusting family in the goo and it actually takes it as a warning to not proceed. Perhaps I should stop reusing the same trap...Once this stuff runs out, the pests start right back up again. Not as dense as before, but it is noticeable. I'd rather have this than nothing, that's for sure.I wish this came with way more of the liquid. I feel like I have too many apples and not enough liquid. This seller doesn't appear to sell the liquid in bulk, you just have to buy more traps, which is annoying.I also prefer filling it way more than it suggests because if I fill it less, then I have to replace it more frequently. Ergo, providing more of the liquid would be far more useful.This does emit a faint smell, but I've found that it only smells when I'm directly above it.Make sure to tell your guests or family that this thing can spill if you pick it up wrong.I'd ideally rate this maybe 3.5 stars. It works well enough to keep the pests down, but it's not a permanent solution and they don't always fall for the trap.Update: I've changed my mind. Solid 4 stars. I noticed German roach nymphs wading dead in the liquid. I've been keeping these near my sink because that's where the fruit flies hang out. It appears as though the roaches, while trying to get a drink in the sink, find the fruit fly trap and want in. I have a small infestation of roaches in my place, so if they don't fall for one trap, I'm glad they'll fall for another. I don't like having roach traps on my counter due to the chemicals used, so this much-less-poisonous-to-humans trap is a great alternative.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on August 1, 2025

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: My first go to gnat remover! Works great!
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I have tried: Zevo Flying Insect Traps (RECOMMEND)Husaco Store 2 Flying Insect Traps & 2 Cartridges - Compatible with ZEVOM364A RefillKatchy Flying Insect Trap IndoorTerro Fruit Fly Traps - (RECOMMEND -this product)I tried several products for my recent gnat/fruit fly/super tiny demons from hell infestation. I really wanted to like some, but basically overall the ZEVO was superior. Thankfully I bought that one initially, but did not realize how severe my infestation got (as I was away from home for abit). Zevo pretty immediately and quickly toned down the flying creatures, and I noticed quickly the trap filling up with more and more. A good week in it was basically all covered up.I ended up getting a total of 4 Zevo traps, in certain areas I knew would fly to at night. Night is very good at getting them. I think it helps having no other lights on  either. I will mention I also got Terro fly trap, and they are just lil tiny apple looking containers that you fill with some nasty vinegar mixture. I fill it up just a small amount, because honestly that is enough and I can keep refilling it. One I had to dump because of so many dead bodies and I refilled it up.I put one of those in my fridge just incase. And some near the other traps to try to attract them faster to the Zevo's light of death.The Katchy and offbrand zevo aka Husaco store traps, just basically didn't trap as many.... Like say a week went by and I could count the flies it was a handful. They just weren't as amazing/fast for me.I had such a drastic reduction with 2 Zevo's and now that I got 4 going, I think they might finally be eradicated. (With the Terro assists. I liked putting them by the sinks.These Terro were my first go to to remove gnats. Like banana's?  Yeah, these were good and enough for that.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on November 19, 2025

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: Works better than anything else we tried!!!  And we have tried soooo many. These got rid of the gnats within a few days and I haven’t seen any new ones. I highly recommend them. They do give off a slight vinegar smell.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on February 4, 2026

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I was skeptical but hopeful because I had tried this before with little or minimal success.  But I decided to just leave this sitting out in the living room on a window sill forgot about it, until I just checked it and found these little guys inside.Needless to say, I rinsed it out, poured in a new capsule of the Terro special “juice”, and set this back in the same spot to continue its campaign and reign against these pesky gnats!Reading other reviews, I think it just depends on your situation and location and maybe even the type of gnats that you’ve got for these to be effective or not effective.  But for the low cost of this 4-pack, it won’t hurt your wallet badly to at least give these a try, because you never know - they might just work for you as it did for me!👍👍👍

aPlusContent.title: Product description
aPlusContent.rawText:
Product description
The Raid Fruit Fly Trap protects fruit and reduces fruit flies. 
Protect your produce and say goodbye to fruit flies with the Raid Fruit Fly Trap! Just add water and place in the area bugs are bothering you most. Then, attract and trap for up to 30 days. Simple to use and in a delightful shape, the Raid Fruit Fly trap is the perfect solution for taking care of uninvited guests indoors! Flip through these photos for product use steps 1 through 3. 
Step 1 
Unscrew the top of the apple from the base. (Funnel must remain in the top piece of the apple, do not separate.) 
One dissolving lure packet has already been placed in the apple base - Do not open lure packet. 
Store remaining lures in the provided storage bag for later use or after 30 days.
Step 2 
Add approximately 2 teaspoons of warm tap water to the apple base. 
Lure packet will dissolve in water in approximately 30 minutes. 
It is normal for a slight aroma to exist after the trap is set. 
Add 1 or 2 small drops of liquid dish soap to enhance the lure if desired.
Step 3 
Check trap daily and add water when level drops. 
Do not let water touch funnel.

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: Although this product didn’t work for me, in all fairness to Raid, it turns out that what I had wasn’t Fruit Flies. In my case they lived in sink drains and indoor plant soil. features: HYGIENE AND 100% SAFE - Veyofly is extremely safe and hygienic. Flies are attracted to the device by its bright UV light and are trapped in the device by the strong glue plate located inside the device. No high voltage electric sound and no zapping; eliminates flies silently and safely. Safe to use 24/7 and can be used in areas with children and pets. The fly catcher is made with quality components and will last for many years.

NON-TOXIC AND ODOR-FREE - Contains no pesticides, insecticides and is completely odorless. Get rid of all bugs and flying insects without using pesticides or dangerous chemicals Low energy consumption.

EFFORTLESS PLUGN-IN DESIGN- Designed with user convenience at its core, this plugin in bug catcher embraces a plug-and-play ethos, offering unyielding protection across various settings, including kitchens, living rooms, bedrooms, bathrooms, warehouses, and basements. It steadfastly safeguards your home from disease-carrying insects, ensuring a secure and comfortable environment for all

UV LIGHT ATTRACTOR - AN IRRESISTIBLE TEMPTATION FOR FLIES -Featuring a ultraviolet LED lamp, VEYOFLY effectively lures and captures flies and insects, making it suitable for various environments, from homes to commercial and industrial settings, covering areas up to 400 square feet.

aPlusContent.title: Product description
aPlusContent.rawText:
Product description
Previous page
Next page
How often should the glue boards be replaced? 
VEYOFLY Gnat Trap refill boards should be replaced based on catch level: light infestations every 4–6 weeks, moderate every 2–3 weeks, and heavy weekly or when full. Adhesive typically lasts 2–4 weeks; replace when covered or less sticky. 
Is it safe around pets? 
Yes, VEYOFLY Gnat Trap refills are safe around pets. They are non-toxic, odorless, and contain no chemical fumes. The glue works by physical adhesion only. Place traps out of pets’ reach to avoid contact with the sticky surface. 
Does it attract all types of flies? 
VEYOFLY Gnat Trap refills are best for fruit flies and gnats, with strong customer results. They also catch small flying insects, moths, and some mosquitoes. Less effective for large house flies, as the blue light mainly attracts smaller insects. 
Does it make noise? 
No, VEYOFLY Gnat Traps operate completely silently. Customers report no noise or humming. The trap uses a quiet blue LED light to attract insects to adhesive glue boards, with no buzzing, zapping, or mechanical sounds. 
What do customers say? 
Customers find the fly catcher easy to set up and use, and many like that it also functions as a night light. It receives positive feedback for overall quality. Reviews on stickiness—some find it extremely sticky and worth the price.

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: The principle of the Veyofly, utilizing a defined spectrum of light that flying insects are attracted to, assures that this device will reliably capture some flying pests.  Specifically, it seems most effective on small insects like gnats, fruit flies and mosquitos.  Whereas it is less effective attracting the common housefly.Although the Veyofly casts a bright circle (say, a bit larger than a foot in diameter) on the wall of a pleasing soft purple light, still it is no match for the bug attraction power of sunlight streaming indoors from your windows.  Specifically, in daylight, I've observed common house flies buzzing over and banging into the pane of a window that is immediately above where the Veyofly is illuminated.The problem is further compounded by the fact that house flies don't seem to be "night fliers".  For example, study what bugs swarm about over a porch light at night -- it's not black flies, but rather moths, mosquitos and tiny gnats.So, as your house becomes dark the "night flying bugs" will roam about and eventually be attracted to, and be caught, by the Veyofly.  Whereas, house flies that have parked themselves, at dusk, outside of the immediate glow of the Veyofly are much less likely to find the device, since, by my theory, they are not night fliers.However, the above aside, my two Veyoflys have indeed caught a few house flies.  But the kill rate, or effectiveness, seems like it's 10 to 1 in favor of the night fliers.  On balance, it should be noted that I do attempt to keep house flies out of the house in the first place, so my population of these pests may be low relative other bugs.Lastly, it is very important that a category of insects the Veyofly has success with are mosquitos.  For this reason alone it gets five stars.  Also, a big plus is having no stinking chemicals to deal with.  Changing out the Veyofly's "tacky card" every 2 - 3 weeks takes about 10 seconds, so maintenance is minimal.  OEM replacement tack cards on Amazon are a bit pricey, but generics at half the price are available -- no experience with the quality of the latter.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on June 21, 2024

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: Excellent product. Does what it’s supposed to do. Flying insects are down by at least 90%

title: RESCUE! Fruit Fly Trap Bait Refill - 30 Day Supply
breadCrumbs: Patio, Lawn & Garden > Pest Control > Traps
description: Fruit flies are a nuisance. They invade your kitchen and food storage areas, congregating indoors around fruit, vegetables and food scraps. When used with a RESCUE! Fruit Fly Trap (sold separately), this Fly Trap Bait Refill quickly attracts the fruit flies in your kitchen away from your fruit and into the trap. They’re easy to use—simply cut open the tubes and add the liquid to the trap's bottom cup. Replace the top and it's ready to catch fruit flies. Fruit flies that are caught in the trap will drown in the liquid. Each package includes two tubes of fruit fly bait—each tube lasts 15 days.
features: For RESCUE! Fruit Fly Traps – This attractant refill kit includes two tubes of fruit fly bait—each tube lasts 15 days.
Easy to Use – Simply cut open the tubes and add the liquid to your RESCUE! Fruit Fly Trap’s bottom cup. Replace the trap’s top and it’s ready to catch fruit flies!
Attracts Fruit Flies – This attractant lures fruit flies that congregate indoors around fruit, vegetables and food scraps.
Food-Based Ingredients – This fruit fly bait is fast-acting, made with food-based ingredients, and is safe to use around food.
Made in the USA – At RESCUE!, our goal is to design and manufacture the safest and most effective pest control solutions available. We proudly manufacture our products in the USA!

title: RESCUE! Fruit Fly Traps (Set of 2 Traps) – Reusable, Includes Liquid Bait Attractant
breadCrumbs: Patio, Lawn & Garden > Pest Control > Traps

items.title: Safety Information
items.text: Keep out of reach of children and pets. Do not ingest. Dispose of responsibly. Always follow instructions for safe handling and storage.

features: Fast Acting - These discreet indoor traps use a food-based lure that attracts, catches, and eliminates fruit flies that congregate and breed near fruit, vegetables, and food scraps.

Long Lasting – Each box includes 2 traps and four 15-day supplies of food-based fruit fly attractant that is safe to use around food. Fruit flies find the color and scent of our attractant irresistible!

Easy to Use – No mixing or home remedies required. Simply add the liquid attractant to the trap and it starts catching fruit flies within a day! A smart No-Tip design prevents spills in your kitchen or food storage areas.

Reusable Trap - Once caught, fruit flies drown in the liquid attractant. When the traps are full, they can be emptied, cleaned, and stored for future use. Attractant refills are available to purchase.
Made in the USA – At RESCUE!, our goal is to design and manufacture the safest and most effective pest control solutions available. We proudly manufacture our products in the USA!

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: Instantly Trapped Fruit Flies - I had GREAT success using this product!!!
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: Soooo, I came home from a week traveling and found fruit flies hanging around some dishes I didn't clean before leaving town (I know gross right?!?). I was disgusted and researched home DIY solutions as well as researched Amazon and settled on this item because it was cheap and was eligible for one day shipping. I received them early the next day and immediately put them in place and no joke they started trapping flies instantly. Be careful when opening the bait/juice packs because they are VERY full and I spilled some after trying to cut open the edge like a ketchup packet. I ended up cutting off the entire top with scissors and dumping the liquid bait in the trap which was easy to do. The bait seemed like basic apple cider vinegar yet seemed to have something added to it to make it extra potent smelling because it was strong but in a good way and it wasn't hard on my nostrils at all. The bait must have had something slippery added to it too because the flies didn't come out once they went in, this would be similar to how DIY traps suggested adding dish soap to apple cider vinegar. OH I ALMOST FORGOT, I did 3 DIY traps at the same time using small dishes that I poured in red wine and covered them with saran wrap and a rubber band to hold the Saran Wrap on and then I poked small holes in it. I did 3 of them of different sizes and added dish soap in one to see what worked best and they did catch a couple flies after leaving them on the counter overnight but they were no match at all for this real trap. The DIY traps caught maybe 5 total between all 3 traps and this real trap caught 15-20 instantly and solved the problem where the DIY traps would not have solved the problem 100% by themselves. Considering the low cost, purpose built design, and high performance I would definitely recommend these traps to anyone. They did such a good job I didn't even have to use all of the bait so I am prepared if I ever have this happen again since these traps are reusable and I have 2 of the 4 bait packets remaining. Great buy, don't hesitate or waste time trying DIY traps just get these and be done with the problem!!
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on September 19, 2017

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: These work!!! I literally never write reviews, but I feel I have to post my first one for this product. For any of you out there debating which trap would be the best for fruit flies, this is it. We had an ungodly amount of fruit flies in our house for almost a month and did the most suggested home remedies (apple cider vinegar, funnel trap, bleach solution for the drains/garbage disposal, cleaning everything top to bottom) with no success. They were getting so out of hand and I felt disgusting that they weren't going away. After reading reviews for the various options, I decided to try these (since the price was right and there were a couple reviewers that sounded adamant that these were the solution). In less than one week of putting these out, ALL of the fruit flies were gone.To give you a visual (I meant to take a picture before I disposed all the flies that the traps caught) there were over 100 flies in each trap - we had one in the kitchen and one in the bathroom by the sinks. I couldn't believe it. And I know a couple people mentioned that the liquid that these come with smells like just apple cider vinegar, but I had my own apple cider vinegar trap right next to this one and mine caught TWO flies after being out for 2 weeks. These caught HUNDREDS in a couple days!! I'm convinced they have a special chemical (which I know some people would care more about) but it works!!!Long story short, these truly work. Give them a whirl and I don't think you'll be disappointed. I'm honestly shocked at the negative reviews because of my successful experience. I will be buying these again if the flies ever come back, but we haven't had one since we've gotten these. Happy customer! :) :) :)
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on October 15, 2017

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I purchased this Fruit Fly Trap after reading the reviews. Many of the reviews I'd read  stated that the trap worked adequately. So, I thought I'd give it a shot. When it arrived I noticed that there were two small traps and a 60 day supply of two tablespoon full packets of Fruit Fly Attractor solution. (5 small packets) Using them was easy, just pull the cover of trap off its' holder and empty the Fruit fly Attactor Solution, which happened to be plain Apple Cider Vinegar into the holder replace the cover. Simple enough, right? So, I sat my traps in the problem areas of my home and waited two days for it to work and NOTHING HAPPENED!!!!! Not one fruit fly in either trap! Don't waste your money this trap is useless. It only got one star because it was easy to put together.

title: Terro Fruit Fly Traps for Indoors (6 Pack) + 270 Days of Lure Supply
breadCrumbs: Patio, Lawn & Garden > Pest Control > Traps

items.title: Safety Information
items.text: Keep out of reach of children and pets.

description: The Terro Fruit Fly Trap offers an effective answer for tackling fruit fly infestations indoors. Designed as an indoor fruitfly trap, this product quickly reduces the fruit fly population within a few days. Equipped with a long-lasting lure, each fly catcher provides up to 45 days of potency, providing over a month of protection. The non-staining, food-based liquid lure used in these anti fruit fly traps effectively attracts adult fruit flies, drawing them into the indoor fly trap where they remain trapped without escape. The apple-shaped design of these indoor Terro fly traps blends in with your apples and fruit, providing a discreet and attractive alternative to more noticeable traditional traps. The traps come with built-in windows, making it easy to monitor catches and check lure levels. For optimal effectiveness, place these traps near known fruit fly breeding locations such as fruit bowls, trash cans, kitchen counters, and sinks. By strategically locating and targeting these areas, homeowners can more effectively reduce the fruit fly population. Traps 7x more fruit flies with no mess based on the number of fruit flies caught in a 24 hour period in a competitive test with the TERRO Fruit Fly Trap vs the Hot Shot Flying Insect Trap. Eliminate the nuisance of swarming fruit flies with these effective fruitfly trap indoor products, keeping your home clean and without pests.
features: FAST-ACTING ELIMINATION: Notice a dramatic decrease in fruit flies within a few days with these indoor fly traps for your home, made with a fast-acting anti fruit fly trap lure inside

LONG-LASTING PROTECTION: Each ready-to-use, non-staining Terro fruit fly lure offers up to 45 days of effective trapping, making it a great indoor fruit fly killer. Comes with 270 days worth of lure supply
DISCREET AND ATTRACTIVE DESIGN: The apple-shaped anti fruit fly trap design of this indoor fly trap blends in with the fruits these pests love, keeping unsightly fruit fly populations down
EASY MONITORING: Built-in windows in this fruit fly catcher allow for simple monitoring of the lure levels and catches of these fly traps.
TARGET BREEDING AREAS: These indoor fruit fly catchers are most effective when placed near fruit bowls, trash cans, and counters to control fruit fly breeding locations

aPlusContent.title: From the manufacturer
aPlusContent.rawText:
From the manufacturer
TERRO Fruit Fly Traps 
Have you spotted fruit flies swarming around your kitchen or garbage can? It's a familiar problem for most homeowners. With the TERRO Fruit Fly Trap, you can get the best of these annoying household pests in no time! This fast-acting trap uses a non-staining liquid bait to lure adult fruit flies inside. Once the flies enter the trap, they cannot escape to continue breeding and multiplying. The trap also features an attractive apple-shaped design that will look at home in any kitchen. 
Say Goodbye to Fruit Flies 
Attracts & Traps 
The food-based lure attracts adult fruit flies to the trap. Flies that enter the trap through one of the many funnel-shaped holes can't escape to continue breeding. 
Easy to Use 
Just add the lure to the trap and place it near fruit fly breeding areas, such as near fruit bowls, kitchen sinks, dishwashers, garbage cans, and counters. 
Fewer Fruit Flies in Days 
TERRO Fruit Fly Trap works fast! In a matter of days, you should begin to notice a dramatic decrease in the number of fruit flies around your home. 
Attractive Apple Design 
Gone are the days of unsightly traps taking up space inside your home! This apple-shaped trap will look at home in any kitchen, while blending in with the fruit these pests love! 
Convenient Fruit Fly Control 
No Mess 
The non-staining liquid lure stays contained inside the trap to keep your home mess-free. 
Easy Monitoring 
The trap features a built-in window, allowing you to keep an eye on catches and lure levels. 
Long Lasting 
Each lure pack lasts up to 45 days to provide weeks of protection against fruit flies.

features: No Nasty Chemicals, No Nasty Pesticides! Say goodbye to harsh chemicals inside your home with UV indoor fly trap! The No Buzz Zone bug trap only uses a discreet UV light to lure in flying pests so you can be flyfree and worryfree!

Plug and Play Installation: Simply plug the No Buzz Zone bug trapper in any standard outlet and it will start attracting pests and trap them onto its adhesive trap for an instant, no-hassle pest protection!

Extra-Strong Hold Refillable Gnat Traps for Ultimate Protection: Pests are no match to the No Buzz Zone's mosquito killer super strong sticky traps that captures flies and other insects for easy disposal!
Discreet UV Light Pest Control: The No Buzz Zone fruit fly traps for indoors use UV-A and Blue LED lights to effectively attract pest without the need for harsh chemicals.

No Patience For Pests: BugMD bug trapper indoor aims to give you easy, and effectual home pest control so you can relax and be worryfree as BugMD No Buzz Zone protects your home from annoying flying insects!

aiReviewsSummary.text: Customers find the bug trap effective at catching small gnats and appreciate its nice night light with a blue glow. The product receives positive feedback for its quality, ease of use, and compact size. However, opinions are mixed regarding its catching ability, with some saying it catches all little bugs while others report no flies were caught. Similarly, customers disagree on the value for money, with some considering it worth the cost while others find it overpriced. The stickiness of the trap also receives mixed reviews, though one customer notes the adhesion is good on the paper.

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: My Wine is Safe Again—Thanks to This Little Glow Trap Hero
productPageReviews.reviewDescription:
The media could not be loaded.
                
            
                
            
            
        
    
    








  
  
    I live in Louisiana, aka the Bug Olympics. Gnats, fruit flies, mystery flyers that shouldn’t exist — they all think they’re welcome in my house. I also have a pet door, which apparently doubles as an open bar for every flying pest within a 3-mile radius.Every night, it was the same: me, a glass of wine, and a kamikaze gnat treating my face like a landing strip. UNTIL THIS. The BugMD No Buzz Zone is the silent MVP. Just plug it in and boom — a gentle UV glow lures them in, and the extra-sticky trap handles the rest. No nasty chemicals. No buzzing zaps. No drama.I’ve tried several different brands, and this is the version I landed on — now proudly owning two, and probably adding a third soon. It’s compact, discreet, and weirdly satisfying when you go check the pad and realize it’s been working like a tiny, sticky nightclub bouncer for bugs.If you live anywhere south of sanity where bugs reign supreme, do yourself a favor. Join the No Buzz Zone. Your wine (and your face) will thank you. 🍷🦟🚫

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: Quality product, but difficult to locate refills
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: My original review complained that there seemed to be no refills available, either on the Amazon window showing the fly trap or the BugMD site.  Later, I browsed bug devices on Amazon and found refills at a reasonable price, made by Dichroma.  So, to revise, the fly trap works as advertised, but the web pages need to show the refills.  You may have to search for "Dichroma fly trap refills."

title: Outdoor Fly Traps with Natural Fruit Fly Bait Refill,Reusable Fruit Flies Trap Jar for Fruit and Vegetable Garden(Only Catch Fruit Trees and Melon Vines Fly)
breadCrumbs: Patio, Lawn & Garden > Pest Control > Traps
features: QUICKLY CATCH FRUIT FLIES: The fruit flies are lured into the trap bottle by the smell of the trap bait. Where they enter but cannot escape.So you can keep fruit flies away from orchard,patio!

NATURAL INGREDIENTS BAIT: Fruit fly bottle trap use a scent fly attractant, so they don't need to be as stinky as others.You can rest assured that they won't attract more fruit flies than you started with.Fast acting, natural ingredients,Please note the bait can take 2 days to become fully active.

REUSABLE FRUIT FLIES TRAP: Once the trap is full, open the cap,pour a small amount of clean water and pour the fruit flies into a bag and throw in the trash. Perfect fruit flies trap for Courtyard, orchard, vegetable garden.

WIDE APPLICATION: Our fruit flies trap primarily designed for catch fruit flies attracted to fruits on fruit trees and melon plants.It is suitable for cucumber, sponge gourd, tomatoes,papayas, and other melon plants, as well as fruit trees.Such as: apples, oranges, lemons, cherries, guava,star fruits, plums, mangoes, pomelos, citrus,bananas, loquats, jujubes, apricots, custard apples,peaches, lychees, rambutans, passion fruit, and dragon fruit.

EASY TO USE: Unscrew the lid of the fruit fly trap,take 1 bottle of 20ml trap bait and drop 2-3ml onto a cotton pad,then screw on the lid.Hang the trap between the crown and treetop,preferably on the outer side of the orchard and on non-fruit-bearing trees, at a height of approximately 4.9ft. Use 10 traps per acre for optimal effectiveness.(Suggest supplement the trap bait every 20 days)

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: They Do Not Kill Fig Fruit Flies
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I've had this in my Turkish White Fig Tree for over a  month now... I'm still losing figs to the fruit flies, and the container is STILL EMPTY!  So, it hasn't killed anything.  Waste  of my money: It might work on regular fruit flies, but not on these new ones that attack fig trees.  Unlike regular liquid fly traps there is no odor, they are easy to hang, but they are not for me.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on August 28, 2025

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: This works very well. The flies are attracted and go in and don’t go out.  Works perfectly in the house but even better in my garden. I’m very happy to have this
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on April 1, 2024

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: The container is good for catching small insects "one-way" - however, it is not really practical or sensible as intended for "outdoor use only." The attractant doesn't seem to work, maybe it's intended for a very narrow selection of bugs but not the ones in my area. And given the pretty small size, it wouldn't make a big difference in an outdoor garden; you might catch some flies but there could be thousands more that are more attracted to your fruit trees than the trap.  I am more concerned with when I occasionally get fruit flies INSIDE my house from an overripe banana, and will use this in my kitchen with some wine vinegar as bait instead of the provided attractant.Overall I think a $20 price point is too high for something like this - they sell hummingbird feeders at the dollar store that contain similar parts and materials; so unless you value the fly attractant provided (good luck) it is not that precious of an item in terms of size, weight, and raw materials.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on May 13, 2024

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: It is very similar to one that I tried to make myself, that was effective, but this one is a much better design. It says not for kitchen, but that is why I got it, for my kitchen because fruit flies keep getting in at certain times of the year. I make my own bait to go inside, but it also comes with some bait drops.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on March 24, 2024

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I am the resident bug-master in my group of camping friends after an unfortunate encounter with a swarm of horse flies a couple of years ago – from mosquitos to fruit flies to wasps and houseflies, all flying things fear and avoid me like the plague. This unfortunately did not do the trick of meeting my needs. I tried the fly trap as intended, with ½ of the bottle on the felt pad secured to a tree, but after a day did not manage to catch more than a single fruit fly despite the area being laden with them. I tried the solution in another fly trap model I know to be successful (Terro apples) as well as trying a more effective bait in the system. After two days, however, the results were the same, with the proven-effective fly trap being unable to attract any flies and the proven bait trap being unable to retain any caught flies beyond a handful. Sadly disappointed this could not be added to my full-time arsenal of bug-killing mechanisms.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on September 3, 2024

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: This is a uniquely designed fruit fly trap including a small bottle of a chemical meant to attract flies to the trap. Once inside the flies seemingly get trapped and can't get back out and with nothing but this chemical inside, they die.Unfortunately I have not had any bugs trapped by this item. I actually like the design of this trap and I will probably add my own bait, most likely some red wine, which they are attracted to but is poisonous to them. The design is cool and it looks like it should work, I just think these bugs around here haven't given it a chance for some reason.Overall it has a lot of potential, as it does look like something that would be hard for a bug to escape if it were to get inside. I'm not giving up on it yet!
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on May 18, 2024

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: Very simple to use fruit fly trap
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I ordered this not realizing that it was a trap for FRUIT flies as opposed to common black flies.  However, I happen to need a fruit fly trap anyway. As the warm days get warmer and fruit more abundant on trees and elsewhere, the fruit flies definitely swarm where I live. It is risky to leave fruit in a bowl overnight because it could be covered by these tiny pests fairly quickly. I put this trap together. There is enough pheromone for 2 to 3 uses of the trap, but once that is gone, I supposed that if you wanted to continue to use the trap, a bit of fruit would work. This traps are meant to be reusable. I haven't tried reusable fruit fly traps in the past, but from experience, I could never reuse the traps for the common black flies as they can be pretty disgusting when full. I will have to comment on that in the future.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on April 22, 2024

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: Sigh. I had high hopes for this product and I needed it. It essentially did nothing. Instructions were clear and easy to follow. They come printed on the box and included in a separate little booklet as well. This kit also includes liquid bait with enough for a refill. I thought that was excellent and one of the many reasons I selected it. But it doesn't work. So, its a waste. I have it still hanging, hoping daily a fly will get trapped and NOTHING.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on April 1, 2024

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: Quiet Fix for Annoying Kitchen Bugs
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I set this trap out on my counter after dealing with those tiny flies that show up around food scraps. What stood out right away was that there was no strong smell at all. I did not want something that made the room feel weird or chemical, and this stayed pretty much unnoticed. It blended into the space and did its job without drawing attention. After a short while, I could tell it was working because the flies stopped hovering around the sink and fruit bowl. I like that I did not need to mix anything or add extra stuff from the kitchen. It felt simple in a good way, like something made for regular homes, not professionals. The trap itself stayed clean looking and did not feel like a one-time throwaway item.

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: Quiet Fly Control Without Extra Work
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: This one felt different from the start. It didn’t smell strong, didn’t draw attention, and didn’t make the room feel like a temporary fix was sitting out in the open. After placing it, I noticed that the usual fly activity around certain areas dropped off. I wasn’t constantly wiping surfaces or checking corners. It allowed the space to feel cleaner and more comfortable without changing how I use the room. What really worked for me was the hands-off nature of it. I didn’t need reminders or schedules. It just stayed put and handled the issue quietly.

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: Low Effort Way to Handle Fly Issues
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I am not into complicated setups, so I liked that this did not require much thought. I opened it, placed it where flies tend to be, and left it alone. No mixing, no measuring, no guessing. After a while, the difference was clear. The flies were not hovering around food areas anymore, and the room felt cleaner even though nothing else changed. productPageReviewsFromOtherCountries.reviewDescription: Fruit flies were driving me bananas around compost bin, so I tried this reusable trap. You add the bait (liquid or whatever comes with it), set it on the counter, and within a day or two it started catching a ton of them, they go in but can't get out. It's discreet, not gross at all, easy to empty and reuse, and one bait lasts a week without smelling bad. No chemicals or sticky mess everywhere. Cleared up the problem way better than vinegar bowls or DIY traps I tried before. Tip: I tried to keep it away from direct sun so the bait doesn't dry out too quick, looks good so far. Highly recommend if you're battling fruit flies.

Setting it up is really straightforward, you take one of the refill pods and open it, then slide it into the holder portion of the trap. The top piece screws on to secure everything in place, the whole assembly process takes less than a minute. The design is simple and unobtrusive, it's not going to be an eyesore sitting on your kitchen counter. The trap has large entry holes on the sides which should allow fruit flies to easily get inside, the openings are definitely big enough that they won't have trouble finding their way in. One thing that's important to mention is that the refill pods sit open inside the trap once you install them, they're not sealed or contained. This means if the trap gets knocked over or bumped hard enough the liquid will spill out. It's not a leak proof system, so you need to be careful about where you place it. You want to put it somewhere stable where pets won't knock it over and kids won't accidentally bump into it. For us that's not a major concern because we're planning to place it on the kitchen counter next to the sink where fruit flies always seem to gather, and that spot doesn't get much traffic or disturbance.The size is compact, around  8 centimetres tall or so, which means it doesn't take up much counter space but should still be effective. Each refill pod appears to have enough attractant liquid to handle a typical fruit fly infestation based on the volume. Having two refills included is good value, it should last through multiple seasons depending on how severe your fruit fly problems are.

productPageReviewsFromOtherCountries.reviewTitle: Clean, hygienic, and easy to install and use.
productPageReviewsFromOtherCountries.reviewDescription: This fruit fly trap works by first opening the cap of the small bottle on the box and slowly pouring the liquid into the bottom container of the trap, being careful not to pour too quickly to avoid spilling. Then, align the top cap with the liquid and tighten it by turning it slightly; no tools are needed. I usually place it on the kitchen countertop or near the fruit basket and sink, as fruit flies like to fly in those areas. When the bottle runs out, you can replace it with a new one; simply unscrew the old one and fill it with the new one. The product comes with two replacement bottles, enough for quite a while.This device is cleverly designed. The outer shell is made of black plastic, small in size, and doesn't take up much space, so it's inconspicuous wherever you put it. The honeycomb-shaped holes in the middle seem to be designed to make it easy for fruit flies to enter but difficult for them to escape. There's also a small hole at the top to allow the scent to diffuse and attract them. The liquid has a faint fruity scent, but it's not pungent and won't smell unpleasant in the kitchen. Most importantly, unlike some traps that use sticky notes or electric shocks, it looks cleaner and more hygienic, and you don't have to worry about it affecting children or pets.

productPageReviewsFromOtherCountries.reviewTitle: a good product for fly control, safe and hassle-free
productPageReviewsFromOtherCountries.reviewDescription: I have never tired such trap before and took me to read the instructions to learn how to use it. It’s actually quite easy to set up and it comes with baits.We have fruit flies and drain flies at home due to heating system on and always eat fresh fruits home, which is annoying! I found this fruit-based formula attractors fruit lies and drain flies quickly. It pulls flies in very effectively without producing odors, which makes it comfortable to use in kitchen. This is really important because the kids got scared and do not want to eat when they see flies bodies….Another point worth mentioning is the long-lasting bait! I almost feel they can last forever! This is convenient and make me have a peace in mind not to worry about frequent replacement.It’s labeled as non-toxic and odorless, which feels safe to use even with young kids around.Overall, it’s a reliable and convenient solution to get rid of fruit and drain flies effectively and safely.

productPageReviewsFromOtherCountries.reviewTitle: Clean-looking trap, dead-simple setup, does the job quietly
productPageReviewsFromOtherCountries.reviewDescription: Fruit flies are tiny, persistent, and weirdly confident. This trap is the opposite: small, discreet, and quietly effective.You get the holder plus two attractant pods, and setup takes about as long as it takes to lose your patience with fruit flies - slide the pod in, close it up, place it near the usual trouble spot (sink, compost, fruit bowl). No mixing. No mess. No “homemade science fair” smell.The design is what sold me. It doesn’t scream pest control, so it can live on the counter without ruining the vibe. It just sits there and works while you carry on cooking, cleaning, and existing.One note: don’t let it get tipped. The pods sit open inside, so if it’s knocked over, liquid can spill. Put it somewhere stable and out of reach of pets/kids.Overall, it’s a smart, refillable, low-drama solution, and the extra pod makes it feel like a purchase, not a one-and-done.

description: Insects invading your corner of the world? Whatever insect you're fighting, Black Flag products are dedicated to providing the tools you need to regain control. With formulas that act fast and never back down, Black Flag insect controls have been arming homeowners in the battle against invading bugs for more years than any other brand. Houseflies complete their life cycle to adult within just a couple of weeks. They get in through open windows, doors, cracks and crevices, and they stay because they can’t find their way out – and because the sugary foods they love are often abundant on our countertops. They can carry diseases that can then be transmitted to humans when they touch our food. Black Flag Fly Paper is a simple way to control houseflies, mosquitoes and other flying insects. The trap provides long-lasting protection after opening. Once flies land, they never leave. Just unroll the sticky paper using a twirling motion and hang anywhere flying insects are a problem in the home or around the porch, patio or garden. If the trap is cold, bring it to room temperature before using. Hang it away from sunshine. Simply wrap in newspaper and replace when the fly paper is full. This trap can also be used to control gypsy moths. Wrap the tree trunk at a height of approximately 4 ft. As the gypsy moth caterpillar makes its way up the tree to reach the leaves, contact with the sticky surface will prevent gypsy moth caterpillars from crawling further.

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: Wage a War of Attrition on Gnats With These Yellow Baton Things
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: We suffered a prolonged invasion of gnats during the latter half of summer and early autumn this year, presumably because of recurring minor flooding in the basement from using the washer and a pipe drainage issue. They were so numerous and terrible during the worst of it, especially before I surmised the intermittent flooding was attracting them and then took action.Generally, unless you can seal off all avenues of infiltration and remove/destroy all attracting things, or use a repellant tactic such as a product or routine exterminator visits, you will have to wage a war of attrition on those that are there and will come in, so their numbers don't steady build above a few in any given room on any given time- and also to exorcise your abode of these infernal bastards more quickly if a massive invasion occurs. This war should be prosecuted with constant passive methods (as in, you don't have to do more than set it up and leave, as opposed to spraying them with pesticides or isopropyl as you see them, or the nuclear tactic called fumigation). As they are attracted to windows during daytime and are either more active naturally during midday or are stimulated by light (or both), window strips such as the Catchmasters is one indispensible core method, especially within rooms you primarily use that are being significantly invaded.The other entails the use of attractants paired with an ensnarer at locations which either are far away from windows and/or are an area they like to congregate. This device alone sans the use of bait doubles as attractant (the yellow color) and ensnarer (adhesive is on the yellow rod part) and is marketed as both. However, you may add bait to it as well to potentiate the attractant effect. Enduring blitzkriegs from these hated creatures every few days and being driven almost insane by the constant annoyance and having to alter my eating habits, I ordered these and prayed for deliverance from my insectoid nemesis (well, desperately hoped would be a better word- I'm an ardent atheist).These worked so well. On one particularly bloody day, over the 12 hours following the placement of one baton in my room paired with an attractant, the thing had caused terrible carnage in their ranks all by itself. 100-200 were consigned to death, and I found the nearly innumerable black spots peppering the device to be simultaneously disgusting and galvanizing. Very few to none of these things can escape once they land on the yellow surface.I found these to be useful in the kitchen (two hung from the window curtain rod), adjacent dining area, and downstairs where the gnats seemed to largely congregate and be almost always present (we have refrigerators and freezers down there, and they'd always fly in after we opened the door, leaving some number of them for us to find on the shelves or floor of the units every day after being trapped and soon perishing from hypothermia). Downstairs and the dining room were the best places, especially downstairs, as the repellants made IIRC by the same company completely lost effectiveness within a week, and downstairs was likely where they were invading from and where the attracting factor(s) was.I wholeheartedly recommend these along with a highly rated brand of window fly strips, as well as homemade inexpensive devices (plastic cups, dish soap, red wine or apple cider vinegar are the products needed for the standard basic type) that work using the same principles and modes of action that these do (attraction and ensnarement). They can be hung from a rod or whatever, or rested on a surface either longways or straight up (use mounting putty for the latter to secure it, though if it falls over, it won't get adhesive on anything if no other objects are near it).Do be mindful of valuable items like treasured clothing when you deploy and maneuver/work very close to these. The adhesive is soft, thick, and goopy, which means minor contact won't make the device stick to you much as you pull away, but it will come off onto you and potentially ruin or mar items such as clothes (an adhesive dissolver such as Goo-Be-Gone or whatever might resolve it- or maybe ruin it more) which can't be easily washed and is pourous. That said, these are a FAR MORE elegant and useful solution than the more primitive and rudimentary variant of this type known as fly ribbons/paper.Also, I did not find their suggestion of using syrup in the bottom tray to be useful. They are attracted not to sweet things per se (gnats, I mean- fruit flies are different), but to decay and fermentation. You'd be better served using milk or maybe fruit juice, or red wine or vinegar. Something which goes sour or ferments. Syrup doesn't do either, and I perceived no benefit from it.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on December 13, 2019

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I get a lot of fruit flies, especially this summer. While i work on ways to store my produce and hopefully lessen the issue, I've purchased several ways to trap the gnats. I have a few of those plug in led sticky traps, some kid safe and natural insect spray, and have purchased this brand of fly paper 2/3x now. It's inexpensive, catches a good number of gnats, and I've noticed it has helped to control the number of flies i see flying around in general. The paper lasts about a month at max before I like to change it. Once there are a good amount of flies, I notice it stops catching more. The stickiness seems to stay  pretty sticky and useful, but maybe once they see their buddies trapped the get wise? Not sure...it is VERY sticky and takes some getting used to operating as to not get it on your skin while unfurling. I do wish i was a bit easier to stick to my wall, but that's likely a personal issue that others won't have. It comes with a ribbon and tack to affix it to your wall or ceiling and they work well and stay secured. I notice it starts catching gnats within a day or two and then will catch a bunch! When ive gotten some stuck to my finger I rinse with warm/hot water to clean it off, then wash my hands regularly and that seems to work fine. I continue to repurchase these fly papers because they are easy to use, work well and don't produce an off-putting smell like some traps that use vinegar or other chemicals do. They also don't require any power which is nice (i only have so many outlets after all). The only con is that they csn be unsightly, but that's not really a problem for me because they work as intended. They also arrive quickly, within a day or two and are a good price.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on November 1, 2024

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I've had great luck deploying these when there's an infestation of small bugs like fruit flies or gnats. It will catch a tremendous number of them in only a day or two, and it's easy to move around to wherever the current problem area is. You can put something appealing like maple syrup or honey or bits of fruit in the bottom, but you don't have to, and will catch plenty either way.The reason I say it's misleading is that you might buy this for a housefly problem. For whatever reason, regular flies don't like this trap that much. We've got guinea pigs and will sometimes have a fly problem in summer if the cage isn't cleaned frequently enough. We will always catch a few flies but not enough to really knock out the problem. They just don't care and I think it's mostly luck of flies randomly landing there and getting stuck. We've had better luck with the old fashioned fly ribbon for houseflies. Finally, while this stick does work for fruit flies, there are other options like the Zevo lighted sticky traps or even just a bowl of vinegar, water, and dish soap that can be equally effective and cheaper or less messy. This isn't a bad product, you just have to be sure it's what you want.

title: Fruit Fly Bottle Top Trap – Reusable Non-Toxic Indoor/Outdoor Catcher (Yellow, 2 pcs)
breadCrumbs: Patio, Lawn & Garden > Pest Control > Traps
description: Pesky fruit flies driving you crazy? These pests are a common nuisance in household, commercial, and outdoor settings. The Fly-on-In trap captures fruit flies in a quick, effective, and inconspicuous manner. The flexible silicon trapping funnel inserts into most standard glass wine, beer, soda or water bottles (may also fit select plastic containers).  Instructions:  1. Leave a small amount of your drink in a bottle (not included) or fill with your bait of choice. Recommended attractants: banana, wine, apple cider vinegar.  2. Insert the cork trap into the top of the bottle and place wherever the pests are hanging out.  3. Recycle the bottle… don’t forget to remove the cork trap.  4. Reuse again and again. This simple device allows hygienic fly removal without gross chemicals, sticky paper, strips or handling of dead flies. The trap eliminates fruit flies to prevent further breeding and multiplying. Perfect for kitchens, bars, and restaurants. Also easily portable, great for a fly free picnic.  No mess or waste, Chemical Free, Eco Friendly, Child and Pet safe, Patent Pending Design
features: Easily and safely eliminates fruit flies.
Simple attachment - Fits most glass wine, beer, soda, or water bottles (not included). Lures flies to enter through a small opening and prevents escape.
Attracts flies discreetly – A good-looking way to catch fruit flies and gnats in kitchens, bars, or restaurants. Compact size allows inconspicuous use and easy storage.
Reusable - Made with durable food grade silicon. Easy to wash. Disposal merely requires recycling the bottle.
Eco Friendly – Non-toxic and natural pest control. Add bait in a repurposed container of your choice.

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: UPDATE:  These traps are amazing.  The pics speak for themselves.
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: 1st UPDATE:  The weather is warming up and these traps are keeping those fruit flies occupied.  The fruit flies just keep coming in from the outside, but it hasn't been an issue, since I can just setup new traps.  Plus I already have everything I need to make these guys work, a wine bottle and a piece of banana.  See the pics I attached and you can see these actually work.I found that the fruitfly fly on in catchers really do work.  Little pieces of banana work the best.  I had some  buzzing around me across the room eating a banana and they all disappeared.  We found them inside the wine bottle.  I also like the idea of being able to just wash them, store them in a drawer and reuse them when the flies show up again.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on February 22, 2019

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I have a bad case of fruit flies in my kitchen around my drain, and it feels like they keep multiplying no matter what I do. I really hoped these would be helpful, but they did not help whatsoever! They seem to just attract a ton of them to the cans I have the product in, but the flies will not even climb down into the funnel. They just hover and climb all around it. This product is absolutely useless. I may as well just leave an open drink sitting out on the counter.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on August 25, 2019

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: final third part to our gnat extermination plan
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: We have had a gnat infestation almost all summer.  I have used all sorts of traps, attractants, etc with little to no improvement.  Sometimes I think they actually made it work.  This is the closest we've come to success -- we are using an additive that you put in your drains and yellow sticky sheets to catch flies.  We didn't have swarms, but our numbers seemed to hold steady.,  Adding this seems to be the missing link.  We simply put a little wine in one and a bit of banana in the other.  Initially we didn't have any results and I was disappointed, but within a couple hours there were 10 in the bottle with the wine and nearly that in the one with the banana.  Does it catch them all?  Of course not.  A large number will run circles around the rim and never venture in.  BUT I've never seen one come back out once in.  Reusing is simple -- we put it in the freezer and then took it out, sat it to thaw and then dumped and rinsed.  The next time we added water, waited for the swimmers to stop, then dumped and rinsed.  Easy and hopefully the end is in sight!
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on October 28, 2019

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: These work amazingly well!  The first round I used wine cooler type bottles.  I drank 2/3 of the beverage and then put the funnel caps on.  They both caught hundreds of fruit flies over a period of several weeks.  I cleaned them out - rinsing only needed.  Then I put apple cider vinegar in them, about 2 oz. per bottle.  This time they caught hundreds in just one day!  The vinegar definitely works better.They are designed for fruit flies, not house flies, knats or other insects.  If you buy them for anything other than fruit flies, you shouldn’t complain when they don’t work ...

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I set this up at work Friday morning. I shoved some banana in the bottom added a little water hoping the would fall in it and die. When I left my desk around 4 hours after installation I was slightly disappointed only 3 dead gnats.... but I left it and forgot about it. Forward to Monday morning I walked in and wanted to vomit... success the bottle had lots of victims... now most still alive, thinking of adding more water in to drown them before removing the plug. I can’t have them all fly away. Also lots like to hang out at the top. I think it helped over the weekend likely less food = attracting more but I could not believe how well it worked. I can’t wait to set up the other one I will try 2 different baits. Maybe the apple cider vinegar will attract them and actually kill them, however they do love the few day old banana. I am so pleased with my purchase I highly recommend it. I did really want the electric fly swatter but they were expensive and I feared what the guys in my office might do if they got ahold of it....
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on August 19, 2019

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: we’re good people - give to others, kind, didn’t spoil End Game - so naturally I was confused when last week we were hit with a plague of gnats.  I’m not talking 20. What we experienced was a massive seemingly never ending tsunami of tiny flying get into everything creatures. And, we have a cleaning service. No food on counters, no bag of rotting tomatoes, no bananas. But these buggers were even getting into the refrigerator. I was leaving traps everywhere, and these were like genetically modified supergnats that could sense my next move and avoid it. Until I put some smushed up bananas in several wine bottles and topped them off with these no escapy one way ticket to bliss then death gnat corks. Within 2 days, gnats eradicated. We did eventually locate the source - apparently we had left a trash bin on the lanai with the leavings from a dinner party, but we eliminated that a couple of days before buying the bottle corks.  They’re now washed and in a drawer should we get punished with a swarm of insects again.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on May 4, 2019

aPlusContent.title: Product description
aPlusContent.rawText:
Product description

15W UV Replacement Bulbs 
Bulb is replaceable, our insect trap with the special spectrum, UV lights will attract moths and mosquitoes to the bug zapper, Internal high-voltage grid releasing high-voltage current can effectively kill the pest in seconds 
Product Features: 
The internal grid is fenced with a ABS housing to prevent children and animals from touching the electrified grids inside the device. 
Electric mosquito killer made of fireproof and ABS material, non-irritating odor, non-toxic and harmless to humans, only the effect of current, which electrocutes mosquitoes upon contact. 
Powerful electronic 4200V grid.
Designed for indoor/outdoor use electric mosquito killer: living room, kitchen, bedroom, basement, restaurant, store, office etc.
Includes: 
1 x Bug Zapper · 1 x User Manual · 1 x Brush 
Electric mosquito killer uses 365 Nm wavelength ultraviolet light-emitting bulb that lures insects in and then immediately zaps them with a 4200V electric shock. 
Works best for catching gnats, fruit flies and mosquitoes. Set up the insect trapper as close to the insects as possible (e.g next to the fruit, plants, or trash bin). 
Do not place the device too close to humans because human presence may impair its performance 
Widely Used Mosquito Trap 
area: 80m²/1000 sq Ft. Per unit 
Suitable for bedroom, living room, toilet, kitchen, hotel, Camping, Enclosed Patios, Garage, Deck, etc. 
Powerful & Effective Ultraviolet Light 
Ultraviolet (UV) light that insects can’t resist, Attract mosquitoes in with a 15w UV light (can also add bait attractant), Then 4200V current is released through the internal high voltage grid, which effectively kills the pests in a few seconds 
The UV light bulb is replaceable 
Durable and Easy to Clean Mosquito Zapper 
The waterproof casing protects against everyday weather, rugged, weatherproof polycarbonate construction, will not rust and fade. 
The mosquito-killer lamp has a removable tray, dead insects collect at the bottom of the unit, which can be easily removed for quick, safe, and easy cleaning 
How to Use: 
Simply place it on a flat place, or use a ring to hang it on a tree or house wall bracket. Plug it in and wait. For better results, please put your fly traps in areas where flying insects are frequent activity. 
For Outdoor and Indoor Use!

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: Kills flying gnats ha ha ha YES!!!
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: What I like most is the clean snap that I can hear when a gnat, lured in by the black light tries to land on the bulb, but gets cooked by the spark passing through it to reach the second grid. That's one sweet sound, after trying cider vinegar and water with a drop of dish soap in it. The liquid works great for fruit flies, and these appear to be such at first glance, but they are not and are not attracted to the things fruit flies are. The gnats are tiny, like the light produced from a phone screen, follow the reflected phone light to the face of the person using the phone, then the warm air exiting from the nose, and the next breath in, sucks a gnat into your nose, and that's not pleasant at all. I have been bothered all winter by gnats. To tiny for fly swaters, and sprays, to quick for hands, I thought about using hanging flying insect killer strips until it said that it should be in a room that people occupy less than 4 hours a day. That's not what I consider safe for inside. I have had a 2 bulb, rectangular bug zapper that died after years of faithful service. Thinking that the bulbs would be the shortest life of the thing, I ordered a good supply of extras. I still have bulbs, but the zapper is no longer available sadly. I thought that this might serve as a replacement zapper, although it uses horse shoe shaped bulb of the same wattage, but only a single bulb instead of two.Well I got it unpacked and plugged in and in short order I was able to hear that snap of success,  as "Another One Bites The Dust"!! The unit has a cleaning brush to tidy the grids, but I expect to just use a small cordless vacuum cleaner to remove any debris that is found in the grid or bottom of the zapper. I will check for a source of bulb replacements after 30 days, since I will know by then if I will keep it. So far however I am thrilled that I am reducing the number of flying pests that I have been trying to remove since last season. Sometimes it makes two snaps in succession, a double whammy!!!! Like a home run with the bases loaded!!! Buy it, it works inside without any toxic chemicals, safe around pets and children too. Unless you have flies for pets, lol.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on August 30, 2024

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I've had this zapper for over a year now from Amazon so I feel I can comment a bit. Still working great! I do not have it plugged in constantly but will have it on for days at a time when for example there may be fungus gnats around due to overwatering plants. That said, I never really have a major flying insect problem, but I have heard it zap here and there when it does its thing. I have never used this outdoors so I have no idea how it would hold up exposed to the outdoors.I mostly agree with what I am reading in the AI summary (nicely implemented btw, kudos Amazon.)Of note:Ease of cleaning - definitely. I find it easy to unscrew the plastic bottom tray over a trash can and done. There has only ever been a little debris in there so in my experience it has not been gross. In addition to emptying the tray here and there I would recommend the occasional blast of air from a rubber rocket blower or whatever you prefer just to ensure no dust buildup.Noise level - it is completely silent except for the occasional zap. In my experience, this is like once or twice a day when plugged in. It can be a bit startling, but I do not think it is excessively loud, and I like that feedback that it is working.Brightness - it is on the bright side with the lights out but not with the lights on. I'd imagine it works better in a dark room. I wouldn't sleep in the same room with it unless you are tolerant to light and zaps or wear earplugs / sleep mask. My kill-a-watt P3 device tells methis is using 12W.Cord length – AI summary flagged this. My guess is this is intentional as tripping over the cable may damage the unit or you might fall. I've seen these short cables in other devices as a safety measure. I have never used an extension cord. I will position it near an outlet elevated on a chair or even the counter. I'd follow all manufacturer instructions regarding this and everything else about it.Lastly, while the zapping element appears to be just a bulb protected by a cage. I wouldn't go poking around it with it turned on. No where near water, children, pets etc. Just follow the manufacturer guidelines.Anyway, works great and I can def. recommend it. I do not love insects but hate to zap them. I only pull this out when they cross into the pest-zone and if you have ever had gnats, you know what I mean. Recommended!
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on October 24, 2024

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: Why don't these have a full five stars?
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: These arrived 2NOV25. At 22:30, I hung ten of them over my infested area,  which I inadvertently caused, in hopes to mitigate the infestation. Today, 3NOV25, I went to check on them at 14:00 to see if they were working. This is a time lapse of roughly 16 hours. As you can see in the photos, I have killed well over 1,000 gnats and flies living in the decomposing Timothy hay I'm using to prep my garden. Don't worry, I'm not going to add soil or even mix it after it's ready (for you garden trolls). I just completed hanging the other ten since they're are twenty in the box. Flies were landing on them as I was hanging them.For best results: things I've learned in the past sixteen hours.1. Do not hang at night. The rolls were cold because of the outside temperature, and this made the glue stiff and the rolls hard to extract and unwind. One of them even tore in half because it was cold and stiff to unwind, but as you can see in the pictures, it still works fine.2. Let the rolls sit in the sun for four or five hours before you open them. The glue loosens, allowing them to open and unwind easily and WITHOUT TEARING.3. Though they curl up when you first hang them, they unwind on their own during the day, allowing a greater killing area.4. The ones I hung closer to the source of the infestation, in my case, the ground, the higher quantity of kills the ribbons made.5. Anyone posting less than 5 stars for this very inexpensive solution to fly control for any reason at all, did not install them correctly, or is whining about the shipping, which is immaterial to the product and its use.6. DO NOT scratch off and scan the QR code on the card. It has nothing to do with using this, and it's only some poorly misspelled marketing garbage from the Chinese company that produced them.7. In the past hour since I hung the last ten lower to where they are, I just noticed a few hundred already stuck.8. Do not worry about them getting wet. When I installed them last night, I did my nightly watering of the hay to accelerate decomposition, which is both working well and causing the fly/gnats problem. They all got wet, but as you can see, it did not affect their potency or use.9. Order two boxes the first time so you don't have to wait two more days for more, like I'm doing now.I hope all this helps. At $9.00, this is the best, cost-effective way to delete these pests. This isn't a long-term solution, though. You will need to mitigate the cause of the fly infestation to totally solve your problem.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on November 3, 2025

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I've been using various adhesive fly strips throughout our Pomeranian Kennel for the past 12 years.  The company I was buying them from no longer offers them.  So, I decided to go with this company.  I bought one box of 20 to see how they performed.  Out of the box of 20, I had around 7 to tear, the string to hand them from the ceiling pulled out even before I could get the paper pulled out, or the paper pulled out of the cylinder they were put in.Well, I thought to myself that 7 of 20 isn't too bad.  Boy was I wrong.  I bought three boxes of 20.  THE FIRST 13 I TRIED TO INSTALL EITHER THE PAPER RIPPED IN THE MIDDLE, THE PAPER RIPPED AT THE BEGINNING, THE PAPER PULLED OUT OF THE CYLINDER, OR THE STRING PULLED OUT.  13 OF 20 FAILED.  Over the past 12 years, I have used hundreds of these.  I have a very sensitive and smooth way to pull the paper out with great success.  NOT WITH THESE.  My method failed in first 13 I tried to install.Don't get me wrong, these strips work great in controlling flies and gnats.  NOT THESE.  Hell, I couldn't even get the first 13 out of the cylinder without a total failure.  I even dropped on on a Pomeranian and it was a nightmare getting the sticky crap out of her fur.  I had to wash that area about four times to get the stick crap out of her fur.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on May 30, 2024

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: This arrived 72 hours ago, and since then we've killed at least 15 mosquitoes in the house. There are several things to like abut this model: the hanging loop, universal USB charging (it comes with a cable but not a wall adapter), the folding handle, and the satisfying ZAP. It was worked well for swatting bugs mid-flight and particularly well for trapping them against the wall while they're resting. It's been useless as a stationary bug zapper sitting on a table in constant ON mode: the purple light doesn't seem to attract mosquitoes, even when the room is dark, and the insect would have to purposely fly through the outer cage to get zapped. We've yet to kill a bug that way, and it quickly wastes battery. While standing by in manual mode, the battery life is adequate. Note that manual mode doesn't work exactly as described in the included instruction sheet. The tiny red light turns on when the button is held, but the purple backlight does not. At first I thought it was broken, but it works just fine that was and I'm more than satisfied. I think it's a mis-print in the manual.Can't believe I've survived 29 year in Florida without one of these handy things.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on September 7, 2025

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: Was not sure what to expect from an electric fly swatter, but this one exceeded all my expectations. It’s lightweight, easy to use, and incredibly effective. The powerful electric grid zaps flies, mosquitoes, and even those pesky gnats instantly with just a light touch. No more chasing bugs around the house or using harmful sprays!The rechargeable battery lasts a long time on a single charge, and I love the built-in LED light for spotting bugs in darker corners. It’s oddly satisfying to hear that “zap” and know the job is done.If you’re tired of flies ruining your indoor or outdoor space, this tool is a must-have. Highly recommend it for anyone looking for a clean, fast, and fun way to keep bugs at bay!
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on September 19, 2025

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: This fly zapper works really good. I like that it folds. Great auto/manual zap. Kills those gnats. Love that it doesn't use batteries.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on November 23, 2025

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: Here mosquito, mosquito, mosquito! I got something for ya!
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: Just got mine in today. GREAT product! It's lightweight and really does zap mosquitoes into oblivion! Someway somehow, at least one mosquito seems to get into my bedroom every night, and I was tired of trying to clap them and missing, and watching them do that disappearing trick where they fly into the distance and vanish. Decided to give this wand a try. I actually purchased this model and the older model that doesn't fold. Decided to use the non-folding model at home and take the folding model to work (I work outdoors). Works like a charm. It zaps loud and strong, with an obvious flash of light so you know the bug is history. Testing these, I became a mosquito racket Jedi, gracefully swinging my wand around as if dueling mosquitoes of the Empire, striking them down one by one. I liked this product so much that I decided to purchase two more for my relatives!There are only two improvements I can think of, on the off chance that the manufacturer reads reviews. One, a carrying bag would be nice. Just a slim little bag that it fits into for transport and protection would be great for the folding model, for traveling or camping and such. Two, it doesn't come with any cleaning brushes for getting rid of fried mosquito carcasses. I ended up using a fine paint brush and shaking them out, but a small bag and some sort of tool for cleaning would make this great product even better.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on May 31, 2025

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: This product solves the issue of standard swatters which is needing to strike quick with precise aim. This one, the surface are is so large, you simply have wave the wand in the general direction of the insect and it will practically fly right into the zapper on its own. Plus you never need to whack anything with force to ensure its demise.This bug zapper charges quickly within a few hours and can be used a very long time before needing to recharge again. It can be used in always on mode for a continuous bug zapping spree, or on-demand mode to zap only when the button is pressed down. It also can be propped up to stand on it's own for passive bug blasting.So far, I have am 4 for 4 in mosquito take-downs and I must say it's very satisfying. The zapping noise is very loud, so if that's not your thing, then maybe it might not be for you. My only issue is I want one for every room. It's quite large... about the size of a squash racket, so carrying it on your person around the house is a bit arduous.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on May 17, 2025

    This is a great buy for the price and they work as advertised. Besides killing flying inspects (mosquitoes) these swatters are fun to use. Not used like a swatter, best to just place over a landed insect or just wave towards them while they are in flight but the results are instant death. I have a 2pk but have had one I used fully charged for nearly 3 weeks to kill random mosquitoes and the batter is still 3/4 full as you only turn it on or activate upon use. I also have set it on my porch at night and just sat and watched mosquitoes land on the paddle, electrified to their death, great product and works as advertised. Need to be gentle with them as they don’t feel very sturdy but they were not advertised as heavy duty, they are light weight and as I shared, work as advertised.

title: 72 Pcs Classic Sticky Traps for Fungus Gnat (Plants), Flying Insect, Whitefly and Fly, Yellow Sticky Insect Catcher Traps for Indoor/Outdoor, Kitchen, and Plants, High-End Glue, Non-Toxic, 4 Shapes
breadCrumbs: Patio, Lawn & Garden > Pest Control > Traps
features: [Product Effect] Using the Top-Grade extremely sticky glue, to make sure to have up to 99% trapped rate while Insects touch the sticky trap. Two ways to use the trap, insert into the ground or hanging up can help it adapt and be more effective in different heights.

[Easy to Set Up] 1) Peel off the white films on both sides of the trap and then insert the trap into the soil using the little shovel provided. 2) Wrap the string through the hole on the trap and secure on the desired place to hang it, then peel off the films. Congratulations, DONE! Can be used indoors, outdoors, kitchen, garden, etc.
[Protect Plants & Stop the Bothering] GreenKeeper Sticky Traps can protect your plants from harmful insects, while also getting rid of those annoying flies that keep on bothering you.
[Non-Toxic and Disposable] Non-Toxic, safe for children and pets, and is easily disposable after use. It is also Waterproof and UV resistant.

[4 Shapes + Extra Little Shovel and Strips] Comes with 36 traps of 4 natural designs that are in the shapes of: birds x18 pcs, butterflies x18 pcs, sunflowers x18 pcs and roses x18 pcs. Also comes with an extra little shovel for digging inside pots to put the trap in. The strips can be used to go through the hole on the trap and tied to hang it on a branch or leaf. 24/7 US Customer Service, free return or refund.
