productPageReviews.reviewTitle: Not very impressed with these sticky traps
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I purchased the large rectangular sticky traps before, but since the problem was fungus gnats that I brought home in a plant from a big box store that then infested the soil of other plants, I decided to give these a try because they can be put right into the pot your plant or flowers are in. productPageReviews.reviewDescription: They work! I love plants and have them all over my house. However, with some plants I also had gnats. These are sticky and immediately started catching the little critters. My husband and I noticed a reduction in the number of gnats within a couple of day. It may not get every single gnat in your house but it will get plenty of them. They are pretty big so I cut them in half to use on some of my smaller plants.I don’t have one in every plant (I have almost 100!), but I placed them in the ones that seemed to have gnats. In addition to these, I also have small glass containers with Dawn dish detergent, apple cider vinegar, and water to catch the gnats as well. The two things together have worked wonders for us. Well worth the money to be able to have my plants. I highly recommend them.

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: These sticky traps are conveniently packaged and work very well.  The yellow stickies are just the right size.  The protective paper comes off easily.  The traps fit into the included holders, which have pointed ends to put into the soil.  The holders can also be stacked for use with tall plants.  The traps are good and sticky.  Im using mine against fungus gnats that have been attacking the Basil plants that I'm growing over the winter.  The picture shows how many gnats were caught in 3 days.  Couldn't ask for a better product.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on November 20, 2025

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I bought 17 new indoor hanging plants on line for my new home. I am sure each plant came with1,000 fungus gnats each. When you walked by them or disturbed them in any way you could see them crawling inside my white planters and then they start flying up your nose or in your eyes. I bought those yellow sticky flowers, birds etc. but they were huge, bulky and ugly. Saw these on the sticks&tried them, not as sticky,but how sticky do they have to be to trap those tiny pests?I love this product, It's just as effective, easier to use, and much less intrusive. They also don't stick out like a sore thumb & takes up much less room in the pots and can be hidden in the plant which is, after all why you bought the plants in the first place. I think this product is exactly what is needed to protect your beautiful plants.by the way if you have used those other HUGE stickers and then replaced them with these stake ones you'll probably see less bugs on them only because you have already caught some with the other larger ones first,again I love this product and won't go back to those tiger (kitty cat style) ones, sorry just an exaggeration about the difference in the size.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on August 20, 2024

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: These work well to catch the leaf miner flies in my veggie garden. The number of tunnels greatly reduced after a couple weeks of using these. I wish they were cut in an oval or leaf shape, to make them blend in more with the plants looks-wise (I ended up cutting them myself to look more leaf like). Also the little clips on the stakes that you slip the sticky traps into are a little tricky, it takes practice to learn to slide it in: I peel off just the tip of the trap and stick it into the slip, then carefully peel off the rest of the paper -- otherwise it sticks to fingers and pulls right out when you try to let go. It is a little too easy to knock them out of the clips too, so have to be careful when working around them. But still worth it for catching all those flies!
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on September 20, 2025

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I like this one. It can be used in small flowerpots and will not stick to plant leaves.

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: Some reviews said these do not work - too sticky to get plastic cover off, not catching gnats/flies like original brand, and hard to put into unit on wall.  I DO NOT FIND ANY of these issues.  These work GREAT.  No problem peeling off the plastic cover.  Put into my unit just fine, and in just one day there are already tons of gnats on it.  We of course, make sure it is in an area where the most gnats are and that any other lights (or air-filter or computer buttons) are turned off at night (hint: gnats attracted by the light so you don't want other - even tiny - lights drawing them).  I love the price over the original brand.  The ONLY difference I see with these is that they have a white background where the gnats stick vs. a clear one like the Zevo brand, but this has not hindered it's affectiiveness at all.

aPlusContent.title: Product description bright colors and cute patterns
aPlusContent.rawText:
Product description
bright colors and cute patterns 
Stingmon Yellow Sticky Trap 
Yellow sticky traps have bright colors, cute shapes, diverse styles. The yellow fruit fly trap is safe, and it uses high quality glue so that the mosquito and insects cannot escape. 
Stingmon Helps With Plant Bugs 
There are a lot of invisible insect eggs in the potted soil. 
After a period of time, the eggs of flying insects slowly grow up. 
As the larvae grow, they break through the soil, and the flying insects will annoy you. 
Our orange sticky bug trap will solve your flying insect problems. 
Stingmon Yellow Sticky Trap 
Stingmon real comment graph 
Our sticky trap is effective in use and can be used in a wide range of situations, such as indoor and outdoor occasions, and can also be used for indoor pot decoration. It's the best way to get rid of bugs.

Anywhere you want

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I have bought lots of these types of products in the past, so I may be a little extra critical.  These work just as well as any and come with all the extras I like (the green sticks to stick them in the dirt are my favorite).  I like that they don't have the triangle under the shape - that never works for sticking it in the dirt and look silly if you would rather hang them.  The little shovel is worthless and they should save the plastic.I bought this set because my previous favorites are no longer available and these seemed to be the closest thing.  What makes them not as good - the paper is MUCH harder to get off these!  I hate fighting with them and getting them stuck to my fingers.  The shapes aren't as cute as those I've had before.  The mushroom and bird are fine, the bug and butterfly are from a strange angle and look weird.OK, but I'll look for something else next time.

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: These fruit fly traps work amazingly well. I put a few around my plants and near the trash, and within a day they were already covered with gnats. The adhesive is strong and the bright color attracts the bugs quickly. They’re easy to peel and stick wherever you need them, and they don’t have a smell or make a mess. If you’re dealing with fungus gnats or fruit flies, these are a simple and effective solution. I’m glad I bought the big pack because they really work.

productPageReviewsFromOtherCountries.reviewTitle: Very very effective to reduce fungus gnats and flies
productPageReviewsFromOtherCountries.reviewDescription: I have purchased this product 3 times . It is extremely effective to reduce fungus gnats and any other flies that get attached it grow / hatch in the organic materials used in gardening. Just that the peel off can be made more easy as it does not always come off in one go.  Also an extn on top which is non sticky to hold it and push into soil will be great  . Holding the non sticky base does not always work as it gets inserted into the soil and sometimes the whole thing may get stuck to a finger or hand when trying to push the base and also avoid it coming in contact with fingers or back if palm and then it  removes / reduces the glue when trying to pull it off to re- insert again.  A little attention to these mfg details will take it notches higher.
Super sticky and does the job. I popped these in my house plants.

aPlusContent.title: Product description
aPlusContent.rawText:
Product description
Monterey: Your Home Gardening Resource! 
Monterey Lawn and Garden Products is a subsidiary of Brandt Consolidated and a distributor of plant protection and fertilizer products. We are based in Fresno, in the heart of California’s agricultural region where we first emerged in 1963 as Monterey Ag Resources. Twenty-five years later, we established Monterey Lawn and Garden with the goal of offering home gardeners the same professional grade technology available to the ag industry. Now, as a member of the Brandt family of companies, we are able to offer new products to all our gardening customers. This growing line of Monterey products provides economical and effective solutions to yard and garden problems, and are properly registered and labeled to be sold to and used by the home gardener. We invite you to explore our products — they offer solutions for all types of gardeners and all types of gardening challenges. Spring, summer, fall and winter…it’s time to get gardening! 
Monterey Lawn & Garden 
Providing Solutions for the home gardener since 1988. 
We offer traditional and organic gardening products to make your garden a paradise. 
Our ready-to-use organic pesticides make it easy to protect your houseplants from indoor pests. 
Monterey strives to create quality products that help plants to thrive, and help family grow together. 
Monterey - solutions for the home gardener since 1988.

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: Catches Olive 🫒 Fruit Flies 🪰, a bit expensive 💰
productPageReviews.reviewDescription:
The media could not be loaded.
                
            
                
            
            
        
    
    








  
  
    Works (5 stars). Very expensive (-1 star).I saw a few olives with signs of dark patches in June. Took me a while to find out what it was and then realized it’s Olive Fruit Flies. Found this product and put it up on the high branch of my 6 foot tree (late Sep). Within 4 hours, about 15 Olive Fruit Flies got trapped. So yes it is effective, just so expensive!Wish I had put this up in April if I knew. Most olives are ruined this year but hopefully the trap catches the flies enough that next year they are gone. I’ve removed all olives and garbaged them to avoid them falling off and overwintering of the larvae inside.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on September 22, 2025

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: Did not catch enough Fruit Flies
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I bought and hung one or more of these fruit-fly traps on each of my olive trees. The traps caught a lot of fruit flies as well as house flies, moths, yellow jackets, and even a baby bird. Unfortunately the Traps only caught a small fraction of the fruit flies as every one of the olives on each of my eleven olive trees are severely infected, and unusable for either curing or for oil. I did clean up all the dropped olives last year, but that was not good enough.

title: Fruit Fly Trap Refill Liquid Kit with 2 Bottles (0.68oz Each), Includes 2 Reusable Traps and 8 Sticky Pads, Non Toxic Indoor Fruit Fly Control Safe Near Food with Effective Lure for Home Kitchen Use
breadCrumbs: Patio, Lawn & Garden > Pest Control > Traps
description: 🍃 What is this fruit fly trap kit for?  This fruit fly trap kit with fruit fly refill liquid and fruit fly traps for indoors is designed to attract and capture fruit flies naturally. It helps keep your kitchen, pantry, and living areas free from buzzing flies without using harmful chemicals.  🍃 What's included in the kit?  The kit includes 2 bottles of fruit fly trap refill liquid (0.68oz each), 2 reusable fruit fly traps, and 8 sticky boards. You can use fruit fly trap liquid refill, fruit fly trap refill liquid, or gnat traps for indoors to set up a complete system wherever fruit flies appear.  🍃 How does it work?  The fruit fly trap refill liquid uses a natural, non-toxic formula to lure fruit flies into traps. Sticky boards capture them efficiently, making this approach safer and more effective than sprays, aerosols, or manual swatting.  🍃 Is it safe around family and pets?  Yes. Our fruit fly trap refill liquid and gnat traps are non-toxic and safe to place near food, children, and pets. You can use fruit fly trap liquid refill without worrying about chemicals while controlling fruit flies effectively.  🍃 How long does it last and how to use?  Simply pour the fruit fly refill liquid into a trap, insert a sticky board, and place it wherever fruit flies gather. Each bottle lasts up to 42 days, and the reusable fruit fly traps for indoors provide long-lasting, hassle free fly control

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: No Flies in Culture but give it time!
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: The delivery date kept changing but I was okay with that. However when culture arrived there were no flies in it. I did find some larva so hopeful that I will see some flies soon. Will update my review on what happens.8/28/23 Update: Do not throw your culture away if you do not see flies! As started above mine did not have live flies but was full of Larva. I placed it in the container i use for incubating my cultures for a few days and there was an explosion of flies! There were tons of flies and I am still having eggs laid, larva hatching and flies hatching. Very impressed now with it and will buy from seller again!
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on August 17, 2023

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: Disappointed in quality, culture full of mites.
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: Delivered to wrong address but found it. There are very few live fruit flies in culture and very few eggs/pupae. Poorly packed with no padding and no way to keep fruit flies warm on their trip cross country. No markings on box that say it’s live animals or fragile so the contents of culture rolled around in cup and crushed a lot of the fruit flies.Won’t be sourcing my fruit flies from here again.Quality not worth price or wait. Hoping I will be able to collect enough live fruit flies to start a new culture as media looks old and pretty dry, but there are so few I don’t think I will be able to. I really wanted this to work because it’s so easy to order through Amazon.Back a week later, it is beginning to produce, but is completely infested with grain mites, to the point where I’m probably just dumping it and the two cultures I tried to start with it.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on March 20, 2024

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: First time buying bugs online!!! Won’t b last
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: This is my first time buying live insects online. I am extremely happy with this culture. I followed instructions and waited a couple days for breeding, ect. The population exploded, they were all so mobile. And, so many more than I expected. Plus, no smell. I checked every time I opened it. Even after a couple weeks , no smell and they are still breeding. I am satisfied and will purchase again from this seller.

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: Well, this was a good product experience. This culture smells a little more funky than others I've used in the past, but my critters don't seem to mind so I guess I don't either. productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I received this container of fruit flies to feed my dart frogs. It came in a cardboard box with holes in it, and the plastic container that the flies were in was cracked so all the flies were gone. I did contact the owner and they did send another Container, but the quality was not as good as Josh’s frogs.  I guess you get what you pay for and I will never order from this company again.

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: There seem to be a large number of live flies and larvae, which is great. However, the cup was shoved into a box that was slightly too small, forcing the sides to buckle. productPageReviews.reviewTitle: Works Great and Super Easy to Use
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I’m very impressed with this insect trap. It was extremely easy to set up right out of the box, and the blue LED light looks great while effectively attracting flies and mosquitoes. The suction works well, and the sticky glue boards catch small bugs quickly.I really like that it doesn’t use any chemicals, which makes it safe to use around kids and pets. It runs quietly with no noticeable odor, so it’s perfect for using in the kitchen or at night. The design is modern and compact, and it blends in nicely with home décor.Cleaning and replacing the glue boards is simple, and with multiple boards included, it feels like a great value for long-term use. After using it for a short time, I already noticed a big reduction in insects indoors. Overall, this product does exactly what it promises, and I would definitely recommend it to anyone looking for an effective indoor bug solution.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on January 23, 2026

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: Works Great!  And Makes Me Want To Go Rewatch Stargate Movies!
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I have a greenhouse or what some people may call a sunroom attached to my house front door.  I grow lots of plants in this.  I have an electric fly zapper in the room that wipes out most bugs.  But some still make it inside my house.I purchased a "sticky fly" type of trap ages ago as some of these dang fungus gnats would find a way in my house.  I put it in the bathroom and it caught a lot of these things.  However, I was still seeing the pest issue in my TV room, computer room and the kitchen.I purchased a couple plug in things - not full-blown traps like this device.  They had high ratings on Amazon so I tried some.  They worked...but not great.  I don't know how they received such high ratings.  My big traps catch more.So, I bought 2 more legit large gnat traps.  This one that I put in the kitchen outperformed the other one I put in my TV room.  In the same amount of time it caught more gnats (and mosquitos which I didn't know I had) than the other new trap.  I thought this product would only catch Sci-Fi gnats...thinking they were heading towards some kind of wormhole.  But it did catch more than my "fancier" gnat catcher in the TV room.  The fancier version shows humidity, temperature, has adjustable light brightness and fan speed and has manual versus automatic operation based on how bright the room is.  It is super fancy - didn't catch as many gnats though.I also attached a picture of this trap next to my electrical outlet mounted trap.  Not a single gnat has been caught in the small wall mount since I put this other trap next to it.  This sci-fi wormhole trap has a timer too.  6 or 12 hour.  I just leave it on because I see the gnats all the time.Update:  Used the thing for a while.  It's my favorite gnat trap out of all I have tried - and cheapest out of all of them besides the plug in traps which don't perform as well.  How often does that happen?  I always say you get what you pay for but the cheaper version here works better than the other 2 more expensive gnat traps I have.  Main reason for my update is adding a 5th star - and really this is such a huge deal I'd say this is a 6 star product.  Reason?  After use these things collect dust and need to be cleaned.  This IS A DREAM TO CLEAN!  For my other versions (besides outlet plug in)  I have to get out a bunch of Q-tips, some rubbing alcohol and spend a lot more time than I want cleaning out the dust build-up so air flow can stay high.  This thing?  I noticed dust build-up and just took a Swifter pad to it, the gaps in the suction area are large enough I could get even my XXXL (not joking) hands and fingers in and clear out the dust.  Then I thought I have a Swifter dust mop thingy - used it and it worked even better!  Literally took seconds to clear and clean dust.  The other brands I tried take a lot of time and effort to clean up.  This alone is going to drive me to replace my other gnat traps with this type because I dread having to clean the other traps.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on June 16, 2025

features: Designed for Zevo Devices - The Zevo Flying Insect Trap Refill can fit in both our MAX or standard indoor fly traps. You can use either pest control plug in as a continuous bug catcher to effortlessly trap gnats, house flies and fruit flies.

Why Choose the Zevo Refill? - This product is the only Zevo approved refill and unlike other pest control traps, it is made with no added fragrance or dyes. Instead, the Zevo fly catcher uses blue and UV light to lure insects into the bug trap.

Mess-Free Disposal - The fly trap refill features a strong adhesive backing that you never have to touch. Flying insects will be trapped here, and you can quickly pop the cartridge out to throw it away once it's full.

Catch Insects 24/7 - Zevo fly traps can be used in any room, and will work night and day to catch pesky insects. Stock up on fly trap refills so you can keep a designated trap in your kitchen, bathroom, garage, screened porch, or any other living space.
Includes 6 Refill Cartridges - This kit includes six flying insect trap refills. Stock up to ensure you always have one on hand and can count on year round protection.

aPlusContent.title: Product description
aPlusContent.rawText:
Product description
What is the Zevo Flying Insect Trap? 
The Zevo Flying Insect Trap features a durable plug-in base that uses blue and UV light to attract insects to a disposable refill cartridge. Insects get stuck on an adhesive pad in the cartridge, which is easy to remove and dispose of. 
What type of flying insects does it catch? 
The Zevo Flying Insect Trap is designed to catch fruit flies, gnats, house flies, drain flies, fungus gnats, & non-biting midges. 
Can I use the Zevo Flying Insect Trap in spaces with kids, pets and/or near food? 
Yes! Zevo's U.S. device base meets third-party electrical safety standards. It is a durable, plug-in base requiring 120V/60Hz power and a standard North American plug. As with other electrical devices, keep away from children. 
How often should I change the cartridge? 
Replace the refill cartridge when it is full of insects. We recommend checking it every 2 weeks or as needed. 
Is there anywhere you should NOT keep your trap plugged in? 
Do not use the Zevo Flying Insect Trap outdoors where it might get wet. Avoid placing it near TV or computer screens, and keep it away from furniture, curtains, or corners that could block insects. If you can't see Zevo, insects can't see it either.

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: Game‑changer for fruit flies & gnats—easy install and works like a charm
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I wasn’t sure how well these Zevo refills would hold up, but they’ve genuinely exceeded my expectations. I already had the base unit plugged in near our kitchen fruit bowl (which had become a gnat‑hotspot). Swapping in one of these cartridges was so simple—just remove the old one, slide in a fresh refill, and plug it back in. According to Zevo, the “Trap + Lock” adhesive tech captures flying insects reliably without messes, odors or sprays.zevoinsect.com+1Within a few days I started seeing flies and gnats stuck on the adhesive sheet. It’s not totally invisible, you can see the bugs, but the unit blends well enough into the background that it’s not an eyesore. I’m especially pleased it’s safe to run 24/7, no harsh fragrances or zapping noises.AmazonThe four‑cartridge pack gives me peace of mind that I won’t need to worry again for a while. If there’s a small caveat: It works best when you place it near where the bugs are actually gathering (fruit bowl, sink area, door walls), so if it doesn’t seem to “catch” much at first, try repositioning.All in all, if you’re tired of fruit flies, gnats or small indoor flying insects and want a rather hands‑off solution, this is absolutely worth getting.

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: Refills That Actually Work (and Fit Perfectly)
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I actually thought this thing would never work, but I was proved wrong - I’ve been using the Zevo Flying Insect Trap for a while now, and these official refill cartridges are the real deal. They fit perfectly, install in seconds, and—most importantly—they work exactly as advertised. Within hours you can see flies and gnats stuck to the cartridge, and after a day or two it’s clear this thing is earning its keep.No chemicals, no smell, no mess. It just quietly does what it’s supposed to do, which I appreciate. I’ve tried off-brand refills before—won’t do that again. Poor fit, weak performance. Lesson learned: stick with the official Zevo refills.If you already own the Zevo trap, these are a no-brainer. Reliable, effective, and worth the money. Sometimes the old rule applies: buy the right part the first time.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on January 30, 2026

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: The tiny flying freeloaders never saw it coming
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I bought these Zevo refill cartridges because the gnats in my kitchen were starting to act like they paid rent. Spoiler: they did not. One night after installing the refills, I walked into the kitchen and it looked like a tiny bug crime scene—these things work fast. If the insects could leave Yelp reviews, they’d give this product zero stars and demand a manager.The best part is, I don’t have to chase anything with a rolled-up paper towel like a confused ninja. I just plug the thing in, let it glow like a bug nightclub, and boom—VIP list: denied.Pros:• Works ridiculously well—flying pests check in but definitely don’t check out• No smell, no mess, no “oops I sprayed that directly into my eye,” which is a plus• Easy to install and replace, even for people who avoid reading instructions (me)• The glowing blue light doubles as a nightlight if you enjoy ambiance with your bug justiceCons:• Makes you realize just how many unwelcome guests you actually had• You’ll start judging friends who don’t use these (“Oh… you still chase your fruit flies?”)• Watching the trap fill up is satisfying but also mildly concerning about your life choicesIf you want a peaceful, bug-free home without the drama, these are a must. The Zevo trap does the work while you sit back and enjoy your kitchen without feeling like you’re sharing it with a cloud of bugs on vacation. Highly recommend.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on December 11, 2025

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: We've been having a serious fruit fly/gnat infestation for years, and I'm almost positive they're breeding somewhere in our walls, floors or ceilings -- we've had leaks in the past, and I think we still do. So while it may take more than a trap to eradicate the gnat population here, this trap has brought us substantial relief, in just the span of 2-3 days.We can't call in the professionals at this point, for reasons I can't go into, but since the weather got warmer this spring, life in our small apartment was becoming unbearable. They were flying into our faces, landing on us, and landing in or on our food, even as we prepared it or ate it.I don't think it's been more than 48 hours, and there are A LOT of dead flies in the trap.We've caught substantial numbers with both homemade vinegar traps and the electronic Katchees, but this trap seems to summon the gnats, even from other rooms of the apartment (the trap is in the bedroom, but we also have a combination kitchen/LR, a bathroom and a foyer).Be warned, the trap does have a strict vinegar smell that wafts around, but the smell is NOTHING in comparison to the nuisance of the gnats.They do hang around the rim of the trap for a while, but then they definitely fly into it and die. We put it in an awkward place, and I don't want to take the chance of spilling it, so I can't get anything close to a count, but not only can I glimpse a large quantity of dead gnats, but I haven't seen ANY flying or landing anywhere else in our place, in many hours. This is truly amazing.I don't expect that it will eradicate them completely, because we don't know the breeding source, but one can always hope that all breeding adults will eventually fly around and find this thing. Until then, we may have to keep replacing the traps and finding ways to stretch out the liquid. Maybe we'll post an update, at some point.Meanwhile, I THANK GOD for the blessing of having this trap to control the terrible nuisance that was making our lives so unpleasant and difficult.Posting this update about 2-3 weeks later -- unfortunately in the warm weather (and we don't have air conditioning), these gnats are multiplying faster than the trap can catch them. Without it, we'd probably be swamped in gnats, fruit flies and whatever all these pests are. We'll have to put more traps around.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on May 22, 2023

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: Awesome if used with a sticky fruit fly trap
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: The product on its own gets mediocre results because the gnats/fruit flies need to dive into the liquid.It does attract them so you simply need to insert a sticky fruit fly trap in order to catch them.  I also position it in front of a monitor or other light source.See photo. Fruit fly caught within a couple of minutes.  Without the sticky trap, it sat for 30-minutes with no results.It works wonders with the sticky trap.Note:  I’ve tried just the sticky trap in a glass  in front of my monitor and results are not as good as combining the 2 products.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on October 14, 2024

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: First, the product is not that easy to use. The instructions say to shake well before opening, but it creates a layer of foamy bubbles that take about 15 minutes to dissolve. While the bubbles are present, the fruit flies don't go anywhere close to it. Then the whole product is a jar of some vinegar liquid that is just supposed to be left open on the counter 24 hours a day until all the flies are trapped. It's really easy to tip/spill every where and at 5.4 fl oz, it makes a mess.Second, the scent is awful. It smells like a jar of vinegar (which I could have poured without spending money on this product) and it permeates a 5' radius of where it's located. We kept trying to figure out what was leaking before we realized it was the fruit fly trap.Third, the fruit flies think it's a day spa! They land on the rim and dip down to the top of the liquid, but don't actually fly in the trap. They just chill out around it like we've created a resort for them instead of exterminating.We have other Stem products and love them, but this one was an epic fail!

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: It works well for fruit flies with plant-based ingredients which is safe for use around people and pets. productPageReviews.reviewTitle: Hasn’t gotten all the fruit flies but sure works at taking out most of them!
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: The most important thing about this is “does it catch fruit flies?” Yes, it most definitely does! Doesn’t get all of them but sure makes a dent on the rest of them. Nice, small size too. The sticky pad was a bit tricky figuring out how to make it stand up on its own like the images but I eventually got it. Would definitely recommend!
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on July 20, 2025

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: They work really good had a hard time getting the yellow piece in the jar.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on November 25, 2025

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: The sticky papers are very helpful
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I had purchased a different version (the little red apple one) of a fly trap before I purchased this one. The liquid of both catches flies pretty much equally. The difference is the sticky papers; they catch a lot of flies. If you change them them on a regular basis, sticking to the same time frame, you can see if your flies are increasing or decreasing in number. Once I think I've rid myself of the little buggers, I'll put out clean sticky papers. If they don't catch any flies for a week or so, I'll think I'm probably done with the flies. I'll refill the base with new liquid and sticky papers and leave that out for a while. I really appreciate that this company sells refills, unlike other ones I've seen.The reason I'm so hesitant to declare a victory is that I think the flies got into my drains and were feeding there as well. Someone suggested Bio-Clean for the drain problem, and I've been treating all my drains for about three weeks. It has made a big difference. That takes me about 20 minutes every night. Once I've declared victory, I'll start treating the drains once a week, and if successful, once a month.By the way, I've read in several places that you can refill the containers with apple cider vinegar. I did try that with the little apples and it didn't work for me. Then Chat GPT told me that you need apple cider vinegar, plus sugar, plus a few drops of dish soap. I haven't tried that, but it seems to make sense.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on July 25, 2025

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: These DO NOT work -- I have had them in my rooms for almost a week-- and not one fly in it or on the sticky.  I watched a fly land on the sticky--struggle for a second and then fly away. Please don't waste your money on these.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on January 9, 2026

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: Product works better than some of more expensive products I have tried.  They are not all gone but it's only been a few days.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on November 11, 2025

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: This works wonders.  But there are no refills for the sticky pads.  I don't want to have to purchase a whole kit.  Pretty much useless without additional pads.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on August 14, 2025

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: INITIAL REVIEW: Had my hopes up reading and looking at the reviews and pictures. I had left sitting on my counter and window for about 4 days and haven’t caught anything.UPDATED REVIEW: It seems it would require more patience. Some more days later and some flies have finally been caught. I have already used 1.5 bottles of the liquid stuff, but there are still more flying around. Hopefully they’ll all be gone before I’m all out of this stuff.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on December 27, 2025

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I always get lot of fruit flies in my apartment but I noticed a down tick of them as they getting stuck on the traps. The traps are pretty sticky so the flies are never able to come off after landing on the sticky trap. Best part is no smell, except the liquid does have a light scent, smells almost like Apple Cider. However, the smell is not strong, you have to be close to it to actually smell it.

aPlusContent.title: From the manufacturer
aPlusContent.rawText:
From the manufacturer
TERRO Fruit Fly Trap - 2 Pack 
How It Works 
A familiar problem for most homeowners, fruit flies are commonly spotted swarming around kitchen fruit bowls and near garbage storage areas. 
TERRO Fruit Fly Traps lure adult fruit flies into the trap using a non-toxic, food-based liquid lure. Once the flies enter the trap, they cannot escape to continue breeding and multiplying. And better yet, gone are the days of unsightly traps — this attractive, apple-shaped trap looks right at home in any kitchen. 
Don't let these annoying pests get the best of you — get rid of them with TERRO's ready-to-use, fast-acting Fruit Fly Trap. 
Trapping Fruit Flies with TERRO 
All stages of a fruit fly infestation depend on moist organic debris to complete the fruit fly life cycle. The non-toxic vinegar found in the TERRO Fruit Fly Trap lures the flies to the traps and eliminates the infestation. Within a few days of placing the trap near a breeding source, you should see a significant decrease in the number of fruit flies. 
Fruit Fly Breeding Areas 
The key to successful elimination of fruit flies is to locate their breeding source and place a TERRO Fruit Fly Trap nearby. Fruit flies are commonly spotted swarming around kitchen fruit bowls and near garbage storage areas. Place traps behind or under kitchen counters, sinks, dishwashers, near unrefrigerated fruits and vegetables, and any other location where fruit is stored. 
It''s as Simple as 1-2-3 
Activate the TERRO Fruit Fly Trap by pulling the leaf-like lid off the base of the trap and exposing the vapor chamber. 
Empty the liquid lure into the trap, keeping the lid open while trap is in use 
Place the trap in an area where flies have been seen and where it won't be disturbed or tipped over 
You're done. You won't find an easier trap to use. 
Frequently Asked Questions 
Q: The insects swarming around my house aren''t attracted to the TERRO Fruit Fly Trap I just purchased. Can you explain why? 
A: Often a gnat, drain fly or other type of nuisance fly invasion is mistaken for a fruit fly invasion. As their name suggests, fruit flies are attracted to fruit - they're small, winged insects typically found around ripe produce in your kitchen, near garbage cans and open bottles of wine. 
To determine what type of infestation you're dealing with, simply place a TERRO Fruit Fly trap near the area where the insects are swarming. If the flies aren't attracted to the lure, you're most likely not dealing with a fruit fly invasion. 
Q: How do the TERRO Fruit Fly Traps work to control fruit fly invasions? 
A: All stages of a fruit fly infestation depend on moist organic debris to complete the fruit fly life cycle. The non-toxic, food based liquid in the TERRO Fruit Fly Trap lures adult flies into the traps. Once the flies enter the trap, they cannot escape to continue breeding and multiplying. 
Q: How long does it take to get rid of a fruit fly invasion? 
A: The key to successfully controlling a fruit fly invasion is locating their breeding source and placing a TERRO Fruit Fly trap nearby. Within a few days of placing the trap near a breeding source, you will see a significant decrease in the number of fruit flies. 
It's always a good idea to take some preventive measures and eliminate common breeding sources, such as overripe fruit and trash, whenever possible.

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: This eliminated my fruit fly problem fairly cost effectively. I tried some blue night light sticky paper combo and it didnt catch a single fruit fly. This was one third the price and got the job done. The construction is a bit flimsy. The top doesnt screw on or off, you need to push in the lower half and twist the top to remove. Push the top down to secure. I would have gone with a proper thread mechanism to avoid spilling the bait and any dead fruit flies.Im used to apple cider vinegar, so it only smells when you get up close.The squeeze bottle with nozzle makes adding bait easy with out removing the top.Recommend for small infestations.

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I would buy more than one pack if you have a true fruit fly issue . It took a few days but it got rid of them !

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: Work well for fruit flies, not fungus gnats
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: These are a nice little option to help if you have a fruit fly problem. You can refill with apple cider vinegar and a drop of dish soap. I have seen them used in bars where the fruit flies are attracted to the sugars in the liquors.  Especially nice since they don’t look like they are hanging out due to a bug issue 😳.  Not so great for fungus gnats around your plants so if that’s your problem don’t bother with this.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on August 15, 2024

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: Although it does attract a few bugs, yes it works, it doesn’t get rid of most of them. This would be a good product for an occasional bug, more for prevention. However if you have 10+ bugs flying around your kitchen, recommend getting a bug zapper and plugging it in at night in your kitchen. If you are serious about getting rid of them, you may need to remove all food products like onions ,fruit, potatoes etc off your open counter shelves and just eat refrigerated fruit for awhile (grapes etc). Also make sure you cover your sink drain holes at night (day too if you can)  were they like to hang out. Also they like damp areas, make sure your bath mats are able to drive well. I put some cheap chemical toilet tablets in my toilet as well so they didn’t have access to water. I think I am starting to win the battle with these bugs. They seem to be flying in when I open my patio door. I may need to move my hanging plants with moist soil away from the door.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on June 22, 2025

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: First three days, I saw no flies trapped and was thinking of returning the item. Later on, I have seen the flies walking on the trap and finally got trapped inside.Con’s: It takes time for the flies to go inside as the entry is not wide. Slowly they get trapped. I still have lot of flies inside the kitchen. This item can trap few flies.

Which means you can rinse these out after use and refill them with cider vinegar and a little dish soap to help trap the flies.

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: Premixed mini bottles of fruit fly trap liquid
productPageReviews.reviewDescription:
The media could not be loaded.
                
            
                
            
            
        
    
    








  
  
    I have been battling soil gnats for months because I purchased a bag of potting soil online that was infested, and I didn’t realize it until it was too late so I have been battling nuts for quite some time. I know this is primarily meant for fruit flies and I have had fruit fly problems also so I am intending to use this for both but for now I’m basing this off of my use for gnats.This package contains 10 mini premixed bottles and in my past experience making a similar solution I don’t know why but I can’t get the ratios right so I wasn’t able to trap fruit flies. This mixture is actually working even with just the soil gnats it’s only been a couple of days and I’m combining it with the yellow sticky strips.I made my own little containers while I wait for the one I purchased to arrive. For now, I just have them in a small plastic container and I’ve cut the top out of as you can see, and even the soil gnats are attracted to this solution. I have no doubt at all. Fruit flies will flock to this, and for that reason, I am saving the rest of the solution for my next fruit fly invasion.It’s really easy to use, I love that it is nontoxic since I do have a curious cat. It’s also safe to have near food and it is plant based.  The scent is very mild. It’s definitely not as strong as when I’ve tried to make it myself at home. I think this is a really good value but the convenience of having pre-mix liquid is a game changer for me. Highly recommend this premade, nontoxic, gnat catcher liquid.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on February 3, 2026

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: Just a waste of money. I tried it for over a week, maybe like 2 or 3 fruit flies got trapped (I had like hundreds of them in my kitchen). This liquid doesn’t attach them at all, they are more interested in anything else in my kitchen then than this “fruit fly trap”Even just a splash of water on the counter attract more fluid flies
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on February 8, 2026

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I have a lot of houseplants so tend to have a handful of resident gnats always buzzing around. The bug trap I use works pretty well but I have to add a piece of fruit (gross to peel off when it dries out) or apple cider vinegar (hard to pour into the tiny trap reservoir and I spill sometimes). So, I was very excited to try these little bottles instead! Super easy to fill the trap without spillage. And I’m happy to report that they are working as a great attractant to lure my house gnats to their sticky pad deaths. I do top it off with a few new drops for extra scent and have better results that way, so it doesn’t last super long. I do also wish they just came in slightly bigger bottles, having so many tiny bottles in one pack feels wasteful.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on February 4, 2026

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: Not apple cider vinegar. Convenient dropper tip.
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I thought this would be apple cider vinegar with some kind of soap/detergent.The detergent part is Sodium Lauryl Sulfate. If you shake it, then it forms lots of tiny bubbles. They mark it as the active ingredient.The rest is Vinegar, Water, Glycerin, Apple Concentrate, and Potassium Sorbate.  It smells similar to apple cider vinegar, but doesn't seem to be quite the same.The bottles are soft plastic, and they have a very small dropper tip. They are marked 20ml / 0.68 fl oz.I'm using it with the apple style trap with lots of holes in the top, and a viewing window below. Each bottle seems like enough for two uses.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on February 3, 2026

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: The tiny bar that fruit flies just can't quit.
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: It started with a forgotten banana on the counter. Then came the tiny, infuriating cloud. My store-bought traps were empty, and I was waving a rolled-up magazine like a maniac. These little bottles of refill liquid showed up as my last hope. They're small, unassuming bottles of a clear, slightly sweet-smelling liquid.You pour it into your existing trap (the kind with the funnel top they fly into but can't escape), and the real magic begins. Within hours, it was like a fruit fly happy hour in there. They zoomed right past my actual fruit bowl to get to this potion. It doesn't have a strong chemical smell, which was a relief in the kitchen.· The sweet victory: This stuff works disturbingly well. It attracts flies incredibly quickly. A little goes a long way in a trap. It's non-toxic, so I didn't worry about using it near food prep areas.· The sour note: The liquid is thin and can be a bit messy to pour into the small opening of some traps. Once the flies are in there, it's... a sight. You have to be okay with disposing of a little insect graveyard.It turned the tide in the Great Kitchen Fly War almost overnight. I'm really hoping the remaining bottles stay sealed and potent for the next seasonal invasion, because I am never running out of this stuff again. A simple, highly effective solution to a very annoying problem.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on January 29, 2026

aPlusContent.rawText:
Product description
Package Includes: Fruit fly trap refill liquid *3; Specifications: Target Pests: Fruit Flies Net Content: 0.68 oz (20 ml) per bottle
Features:
1. Uses a natural, physical trapping method, no harmful sprays or swatters needed. 
2. Safe for Kids & Pets: Non toxic formula. Can be placed on walls, counters, or trash bins without spill risks. 
3. Easy Setup & Disposal: Just open, place near fruit fly hotspots (kitchens, trash), and discard after use. 
4. Cost Effective Solution: Works as both refill liquid and standalone trap, eliminating the need for extra containers. 
Tips for Better Use:
1. For best results, place the liquid near fruit or other fly attracting items. 
2. Store out of reach of children and pets when not in use. 
3. The sticker on the back is suitable for glass, marble, wood, plastic, metal, brick, acrylic and other flat surfaces, not suitable for painted walls, wallpaper and other wet, uneven, oily surfaces.

features: FAST ACTING - Eco Defense Fruit Fly Trap uses a powerful yet natural blend of ingredients to lure annoying fruit flies, drain fly, gnats, and more away from your food and into the solution for fast removal. Powerful as a gnat killer for indoor treatment as well.

EASY TO USE - Simply pour the fruit fly trap solution into a small dish or ramekin, place bowl where fruit flies gather - when it's full of fruit flies pour it down the drain. Simple yet incredibly effective.

SAFE AROUND FOOD : Stop using chemical laden products around your food and family. Unlike other products on the market, our Fruit Fly Killer uses natural ingredients that are safe to use around your home, kitchen, restaurants, or wherever fruit flies have become a nuisance to your property.
LONG LASTING : Depending on the level of fruit fly infestation our product can last for months to help keep fruit flies out while providing an extremely economical solution.

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: Works well, not the cheapest but on par with the DIY naterials, but easier and more efficient
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: Works as advertised.  Not sure how some would report it doesn’t work while other pics show countless dead flies, generally that should tell you you’re doing something wrong.For one, it’s an attractant, but is not necessarily more attractive than actual rotting fruit or other less expected choice foods (dairy products in particular seem popular with some fruit flies, likely due to sugar content).  Things like vodka can attract them but aren’t as strong, though wine certainly attracts them as well (And just because it smells like vinegar does not make it overpriced vinegar - if you aren’t convinced, go ahead and see if regular vinegar has any decent results - spoiler: it won’t).  The “vinegar” smell is simply the result of the chemical reactions that occur when fruits and sugars break down and rot, entering the fermentation process.  Little wonder that wine also attracts them, or that it smells like vinegar, as even most white wines should smell a bit like vinegar if you have a sensitive nose.  Since vinegar itself is not actually all that useful, though, it most definitely is not just that; If anything it would be apple cider vinegar based as that’s the go-to DIY lure - apple cider vinegar in a cup with a pump of unscented dish soap to break surface tension, mixed in with a light swirl, and covered with plastic wrap secured with a rubber band on top.  Poke some fruit fly size-ish holes in there, and voila.  I’d say the average DIY trap actually does not work as well as this alone though.Part could be that instructions do not ask for a covering.  Some additive, like the dish soap, is obviously reducing surface tension because flies do not land on the liquid and fly off as they may with apple cider vinegar sans dish soap, they simply immediately become half submerged and will eventually sink, or do so rather quickly if you swirl the container a bit.  Perhaps the apple cider vinegar method would work equally well with no lid of any type, though instructions always call for one online.  As I said this does not though, nor does it require soap, it’s ready to go.  I found it useful to fill up shot glasses and spread them around / top them off for evaporation, though a big bowl like in some pics makes sense if you identify a source of recently hatched flies, as it may be the first stop and decimate the entire population.As always, there can’t be additional sources for them to eat and lay eggs or at best these will minimize the flies you see, to actually get rid of them entirely any food source should also go.  That can be irritating for a restaurant but it’s is necessary and frankly gross not to.  That includes fridges as they don’t necessarily die in them, their metabolism will slow down but they can still get at food. Factory sealed air tight containers like a bag of chips, canned food. anything that a bug would not be able to get into is fine to keep, but most else should go.Finally, use hot water in drains or get the drain fly eliminator type bottle here, it coats the drain and does kill anything there, idk about the claims of multi-week action though.  Boiling water works okay too in drains but you should avoid this if you are in a newer construction with pvc pipes like mindell instead of metal ones l, which can handle boiling hot water just fine.  I have no idea if sub-boiling point water kills anything, but if you’re unsure of the source it’s worth a shot.  Outdoors can often be the source of flies if your windows are open often or don’t seal, or your front door leads straight outside into a densely infested area etc.  More commonly a handful of flies might get in and live off what they find to eat and multiply though, hence why clearing out everything is key.  If nothing works just clean clean clean and keep a lot of traps out, they’ll eventually go.Source: Studied drosophila spp. (fruit flies), they’re dumb creatures, had to deal with labs where they were selectively bred and examined (often waking up and flying off).  If we had had any food sources it would have been awful, used various traps.  I use this in summer at home and the Terrero apple lures / trals w/ clear side windows as well.  Works as refill liquid for the traps also.Idk who could use these and get no flies yet have an infestation. unless you don’t have fruit flies but gnats or something else.  For a true fruit fly it works very well.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on September 27, 2019

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: Effective Product for Big Fruit Fly Problem
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: Eco Defense works very well in capturing most fruit flies but like all products won't capture everyone. It works well enough that I reordered a second bottle. to battle a lingering problem. When you enjoy fresh fruits and vegetables, fruit flies are inevitable. You may think you are rid of them but they seem to return and if ignored multiply fast and travel everywhere especially bathrooms.The directions call for placing fluid in a small dish which does work well but uses an excessive amount of the fluid. I found a nice compromise using the smaller cover of a storage jar about 2-3 inches in diameter which works well.  Will it work in the small covered trap? It works great in that trap or any other slightly closed trap but it takes much longer to capture the flies. To compensate, set out numerous traps throughout the house. If you have a big problem with fruit flies, Eco Defense can help. It's also important to do all the recommended things to discourage fast fruit fly breeding by cleaning drains and careful storage of fruits and vegetables and removal.of baskets where fruit flies can breed.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on December 21, 2016

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: My goto brand to fight flies
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: Though this is not cheap it is pretty effective when it comes to eliminate fruit flies fast. I tried other brands too but those do not come anywhere this one in terms of effectiveness. I am sticking to this brand.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on January 6, 2024

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: SAVE YOUR MONEY!!!  Unless you need some overpriced vinegar for your salad...
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I bought this product based on all the "over the top" reviews stating that the fruit flies were willingly jumping in to die one after another.  There were claims of thousands of dead insets within hours.  I am here to say that I spent the $15, naively believing that my fruit fly problems would be over.  I am here to say that I put two bowls of this stuff out a week ago in my kitchen and one in the office, and NOT ONE fruit fly ended up in the mixture.  They all just continue to annoy our family.  I had a glass of wine yesterday evening and had to continually swat 3 or 4 of them away, while the Eco Defense remained untouched.  I actually saw one land on the bowl and then fly away.  The other thing I should point out is that immediately after opening the bottle, I noticed a familiar smell.  I swear this stuff is extremely overpriced vinegar!!!  If you're thinking of buying, do yourself a favor.  Take 15 dollars and burn it.  The brief moment of heat and smoke may actually kill a fruit fly...  UGH!
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on June 9, 2018

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: One SINGLE DAY and ALL GONE!  I poured a bit of it into the bottom of green, blue, and red apple-sized fruit fly traps the evening I received this Eco Defense product.  We had quite the infestation going on!  I got home from work the next day and asked my wife if it was working?  She said "I don't know, but the fruit flies are gone.  The green trap had 1 fruit fly in the bottom, the blue trap has 6, the red trap had the rest.  I can't say for certain if it was the color, because the red trap was placed in the bowl where the fruit had been (it's important to remove the fruit).  The other two traps (apple green & sky blue) were placed 4 ft away to either side.  Anyway, as a Ph.D. Wildlife Biologist, I once took a class on insect behavior and learned how "super stimuli" can be used to trap insects.  It definitely worked!  The added benefit is that this is a natural product; the last thing one wants in the kitchen is a toxic pesticide, which is why I gave this product a try.  See attached photos of the red trap in question that the Eco Defense Fruit Fly bait was used in.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on June 26, 2016

title: TERRO Drain Insect Killer - T590 Drain Fly Killer Spray - Foam Treatment for Fruit Flies, House Flies, and Cockroaches
breadCrumbs: Health & Household > Household Supplies > Indoor Insect & Pest Control > Sprays
description: TERRO Drain Insect Killer is a powerful solution designed with a dry foam formulation that contains three Insect Growth Regulators (IGRs). This unique formulation reduces the chances of missed areas and provides full-contact, 360-degree coverage, making it highly effective for stopping and preventing infestations. The IGRs in TERRO Drain Insect Killer disrupt the normal development of immature drain flies, fruit flies, and roaches. Additionally, it disrupts the normal life cycle of drain flies and fruit flies, effectively eliminating them during their pupal stage, thereby breaking the cycle of infestation. To effectively use TERRO Drain Insect Killer, apply the foam into and around the downspout and catch basin to cover the entire area. When treating For a double-basin sink, be sure to apply it in both drains. Use the convenient fold-up extension tube for precise application into the pipes. Thoroughly coating these areas is crucial as fly larvae tend to develop in the scum buildup on the sides of drainpipes. The visible foam formulation ensures that no areas are missed during application. This product can be used on all types of pipes. Depending on the severity of the infestation, it's recommended to repeat applications every 2 to 4 weeks to kill fruit flies and other drain insects effectively.
features: Leave the fruit fly drain treatment in the drain until the foam fully dissipates
Extension tube allows for precise application of TERRO Drain Insect Killer Foam
BREAKS THE LIFE CYCLE of drain flies, fruit flies, house flies, and roaches with drain flies killer
ELIMINATES INFESTATIONS AND PREVENTS NEW ONES – Use as drain flies eliminator to stop active insects and block future invasions
This fly spray can be used on all types of pipes

aPlusContent.title: From the manufacturer
aPlusContent.rawText:
From the manufacturer
TERRO Drain Insect Killer 
TERRO Drain Insect Killer is a powerful solution that targets insects in your drain, including drain flies, fruit flies, house flies, and roaches. It works by using three Insect Growth Regulators to break the insect lifecycle, stopping the infestation in its tracks. The dry foam formulation reduces the chances of missed areas and provides complete drain coverage for effective insect control. 
Targets Drain Insects 
TERRO Drain Insect Killer contains three Insect Growth Regulators (IGRs), which disrupt the normal development of immature drain flies, fruit flies, and roaches. Additionally, it disrupts the normal life cycle of drain flies and fruit flies, effectively eliminating them during their pupal stage, thereby breaking the cycle of infestation. 
Dry Foam Formulation 
TERRO Drain Insect Killer features a unique, dry foam formulation that expands to provide full-contact, 360-degree drain coverage. This allows you to reach areas that liquid drain products can’t, making it highly effective in tackling and preventing insect infestations. The visible foam also ensures that no areas are missed during application. 
Easy to Use 
Apply TERRO Drain Insect Killer into and around the downspout and catch basin. It's important to thoroughly coat these areas, as fly larvae develop in the scum buildup on the sides of drainpipes. When treating a double-basin sink, apply it in both drains. The fold-up extension tube allows for precise application into the pipes. Reapply every 2 to 4 weeks depending on the severity of the infestation. 
See More

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: Works but may need a few applications
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: This stuff works and I am pleased with the out come but it did take three applications. I live in an old house, about 100 years old and the gnat's seem to appear every summer. I also live by a lake so I don't know if that has anything to do with it. I would say it was an infestation, I couldn't eat without them buzzing around me. I am one of those people that is a bug lands in my food, it's now the dogs food so these things were maddening. (And yes, I know it's irrational to stop eating your food just because a tiny bug lands on it but gross's me out and that's the way it is.) Forget about drinking red wine because they love it and land in it immediately.  In any event, the gnats literally drive me crazy. I hate them! I have tried everything to get rid of them. The vinegar, soap, water traps. The lights with the sticky cards. Anything I could find. These traps catch the gnats but they don't get rid of the problem. I saw this and thought I would give it a try. I ordered two cans to start with because I have three full bathrooms, a utility sink, plus the kitchen sink. The first time, I performed the application at night on July 7th and then went to bed. I used both full cans, which was mainly because I was figuring out how to use it properly and I put almost a full can in the garbage disposal. I would say in the next couple of days you could see a significant reduction in the number of gnats and I found several in my bathrooms dead. I figured it would take a couple of applications due to the other reviews that I read. Within about four days, I saw them beginning to increase again.  I ordered two more cans and applied about 1.5 of them the night of July 12th. That is 5 days following the first application. Following that application, there was another significant reduction in the gnats and I was still finding dead gnats on my bathroom floors. After the first two applications there was a significant improvement in the quality of my life. Just chipping away at killing these gnats and I was/am determined to rid my house of these pests! Ordered two more cans. I was leaving for the weekend so I thought a perfect time for another application and then it would be able to sit for a couple of days.  Applied another ~1.5 cans on Friday afternoon around 3:00pm on July 18th. Returned Sunday July 20th early evening. To my shear delight, I did not see any gnats! Monday, I saw one gnat. Tuesday, I saw one gnat. Wednesday I did not see any gnats. It is now Thursday morning and I have not seen any gnats. I am hoping that is the last of them but I still have enough product for another application if it is needed. So, in summary, it has taken me close to four cans to fully eliminate them over the course of 16 days. I ordered a total of 6 cans at a price tag of $95.82+tax so over $100 in total, so not cheap. Also, pretty sure the stuff is toxic because it says something about hazmat on the label for shipping. Hope this helps.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on July 24, 2025

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I have already recommended this to others. I prefer natural insect control so spraying even a “natural” product means I was at the end of that rope. The little flies that are inevitably present sometimes as I bring in harvests and garden critters were unusually bad. Vinegar and baking soda, and boiled water was just not ending the infestation (ok, it was one sink). What I realized is the bugs were going into a tiny opening into an enclosed space I couldn’t get to. This spray got into the space and the problem is solved. I sprayed the drain at the same time and I saw it expand quickly. Nothing offensive about the spray. It rinsed away easily. I’m keeping this on hand.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on January 15, 2026

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: Good in combo with other products to rid of drain fly infestation
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: Nice foam and good to use to fill drain to help rid drain of drain flies but if you have a real serious infestation you will need this plus Green Gobbler for drain flies and Gentrol Complete EC3. This product weekly and the other 2 daily until 2 weeks after drain flies are gone. Zevo lights help too.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on February 13, 2026

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: No More Bugs, life is so much better now.
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: This product is what I've needed for years! It not only ended the infestation, but it identified where the heck they were breeding and coming from. As soon as I started to spray this in the sink overflow hole, they made a quick exit. For years those little buggers were nipping at my ankles and calves. It's been months now and no more pesky bugs. Thank you, TERRO!
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on January 31, 2026

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: Does the job, but the spray angle is frustrating
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: The foam itself actually works once it gets where it needs to go. After a couple of days, there were noticeably fewer flies hovering around the sink, so it’s clearly doing something down there. The problem is getting it applied in the first place.Using it felt oddly finicky. If the can isn’t held almost perfectly upright, it mostly just blasts out air instead of foam, which is awkward when you’re trying to angle it into a drain or around a disposal. I had to stop and readjust several times, and even then it felt like I was wasting product just trying to get a consistent stream. The extension tube helps a bit, but it doesn’t completely solve the issue when the can is tilted even slightly.Once I managed to coat the drain walls, it did seem to help, but the experience wasn’t smooth or intuitive. Between the awkward spraying and the sense that some of the product never actually made it into the pipe, it felt harder to use than it should be for something meant to be a quick treatment.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on December 28, 2025

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: The gnats don’t stand a chance
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I’ve been dealing with a serious gnat problem for the past few months. I mean a serious- gnat problem. We used this in our kitchen drain and by the next morning I didn’t see a single gnat. Absolutely phenomenal.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on February 3, 2026

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I was having an issue with nat’s out of nowhere in my kitchen sink. I tried several different things (bleach&water, flying insect spray) and the plastic red apple bug trap to no avail. We clean as we cook at my house so our kitchen stays clean so the nat’s are a mystery that seems to come every two to three years. I read the can and applied the spray just as laid out on the can per the manufacturer. It foams up into a rather thick foam that reacts kinda slowly. The next day the nat’s were no more. To this day they are no more. So for us this spray foam was the eviction notice we’d been looking for. Worked just as advertised.!.!.!.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on June 14, 2025

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: Easy to use, yea!

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: These work great.  This purchase is about my 4th of this product.  Over the Christmas period I made the mistake of leaving a bowl of apples in our dining room. productPageReviews.reviewTitle: The Best Natural, Pet-Safe & Effective Fruit Fly Trap
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I’ve been getting fruit flies in my bathroom that seem to come up from the drain for awhile now and I’ve made my own diy fruit fly traps and consistently poured white vinegar down the shower and sink drains, and it would get rid of a lot but not all of them and then eventually there would be a lot all over again. I started looking for a natural cat-safe and effective fruit fly trap and I decided on this one, and I’m glad I did. It’s been about a week and finally I have zero fruit flies! This is very effective and it’s very affordable too and lasts for about 30 days. I love how it has a lid, so that when I’m cleaning the bathroom cleaner, I can close the lid and put it on the floor while I clean. I even accidentally knocked it over while cleaning with the lid on, and nothing leaked out at all. It doesn’t have a strong vinegar smell after the first few days, and even before then it isn’t so strong, it just smells like red wine vinegar. It’s very easy to use, you just open the lid and remove the seal and place it where you want it, and that’s it. Will definitely be repurchasing to prevent any future fruit flies, $7 for 30 days is a really good deal.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on October 15, 2025

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: So disappointed! No longer recommend. Seller never responded.
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: FRUIT FLY SEASON IS RAPIDLY APPROACHING AND I'M DETERMINED TO PREVENT LAST SUMMER'S INFESTATION. Update : I contacted the company multiple ways and multiple times.Amazon and the company totally ignored my legitimate complaint.  Obviously they don't care about their customers dissatisfaction. It's not about the money - it's about blatant misrepresentation and unethical behavior displayed by both companies. I couldn't even get Amazon to include the "frequently returned" box on the item's page.I bought  three bottles of auntie's product on Amazon 6 days ago to replace my DIY liquid attractant of appl cider vinegar +brown sugar +dish detergent.It's been 5 days and can confirm the other negative reviews that the product CAN'T contain the same liquid attractant - the fruit flies feed on the liquid and are not trapped - they can fly away!!!I unintentionally performed a side by side experiment using this WORTHLESS product against my attractant. Both liquids were placed next to separate hanging cylinders of bright yellow sticky fly paper.I actually watched the flies fly from the  auntie's product into my liquid. Unlike the auntie product, any flies that were not trapped in my DIY liquid were trapped in the fly paper.After 5 days I had to replace the fly paper adjacent to DIY liquid. in contrast, the 3 bottles of Auntie containing who knows what had A TOTAL OF LESS THAN 20 IN THE BOTTLES AND ABOUT 10 TO 20 TRAPPED BY THE FLY PAPER.I am so angry about wasting money on products from Amazon with good reviews. This product has overall rating of 3.8. But that rating is CUMULATIVE - The much older reviews are generally positive compared to more current negative reviews. I conclude this is due to a change in formulation that neither amazon or the seller made clear.I was so pleased that Good Housekeeping had recommended the best way to eliminates fruit fly infestations in a recent article.  The GH panel incorrectly included this product as the only commercially effective liquid attractant since it contained malic acid as part of the formulation. TheFRUIT FLY SEASON article made me remember that I had used Aunties successfully a few summers ago and loved their esthetics and effectiveness.I already emailed a request for them to provide evidence of its effectiveness using a recent bottle.I am requesting a refund from this seller based on my results and will update the status with both responses.Meanwhile if you are battling with fruit flies, infestations made worse by the higher temperature (I live in Connecticut) in the Northeast, read below.First understand that you must use the following steps throughout the fruit fly lifecycle (over several weeks so that the original fruit flies are trapped as well as the their progeny hatched from the hundreds of larvae deposited on whatever they fed on. {I am a biochemist and patent attorney so I needed to know what was going on}.Keep in mind for DIY solutions or other traps that you must attract and rid them from escaping. Otherwise you'll have dead flies near and flying around the attractant.A single fruit fly will deposit hundreds of larvae on fruit and vegetables and existing food sources.Follow these steps :1. Clean all surfaces daily with your typical all purpose cleaner (I use Fantastic with bleach) wherever you find these annoying bugs. For us this is primarily the kitchen in addition to the bathroom and TV room.REMEMBER THEY ARE ATTRACTED TO LIGHT AND 1 FLY WILL DEPOSIT EGGS THAT MATURE IN about 5 to7 DAYS.2. Unfortunately surface cleaning every 1-2 days is necessary to eliminate flying flies you see and larva that will hatch that you can't.3. Immediately thoroughly wash all fruit and vegetables (existing or new) and refrigerate.4. Eliminate All food debris from kitchen counters, microwave, garbage cans, toaster oven,  pet water and food bowls, etc. and keep everything covered or washed like used knives, glassware and plates.5. If you think this is drastic like I did, then you will see an infestation that feels as if those little things won...6. in the article, Good housekeeping recommended this product as using malic acid which is found in apple cider vinegar.  It was part of discussion on using a DIY solution see ingredients above. Any solution must also contain a detergent which causes the flies eating on the attractant  liguid to sink in the solution. They can't fly away and begin a new cycle of depositing eggs. At the beginning of the infestation I used the ingredients above along with standing/hanging yellow sticky fly paper I also bought from Amazon.I am very frustrated that I didn't do the research and work upfront instead of wasting money trying to find the non-existent magic bullet all summer!! As much as I love tasty vine ripened tomatoes and other summer fruit and vegetables, it's just not worth it. I think the high temperatures in July throughout the North East as well as nationaly/globally contributed to the problem experienced by me and almost everyone I know.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on August 9, 2023

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: This product works!  I placed it on my counter and within hours many of these annoying fruit flies disappeared.  I will agree with other reviewers that the vinegar smell is strong.  But keep in mind, that is what many other fruit fly traps use.  The one thing that I don't like about this product is that once opened, the top is wide open so it must be carefully placed to avoid knocking it over.  I imagine the wide opening is what attracts more flies.  Other products I have used did not capture as many flies as quickly.  Highly recommend this product to eliminate fruit flies.

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: Works so well, I keep ordering more. I leave fruit in my kitchen every day which attracts a lot of fruit flies, but this trap gets them all. I no longer have fruit flies flying around, they are all inside the bottle.

Definitely use this product you won't be disappointed

productPageReviewsFromOtherCountries.reviewTitle: Useless vinegar in a bottle trap.
productPageReviewsFromOtherCountries.reviewDescription: This is effectively vinegar in a plastic container. You could make your own for around £1 and it would be more effective . The container is not even shaped to trap them. They fly around it but not in it. This seems to attract flies but it doesn’t trap or kill any….not a single one. Using  your own trap is better. Please don’t waste your money … this is just an expensive rip off and doesn’t work.

productPageReviewsFromOtherCountries.reviewDescription: I loved this! I had so many of these horrid tiny flies flying around my fruit. Decided to get this. It isn’t cheap but it sure well does the job. I’d say in 48 hours I caught 20 plus in there. Thanks Aunt Fannie

title: Aunt Fannie's FlyPunch Fruit Fly Trap for Indoor and Kitchen Use, Ready to Use, Made with Plant Based Ingredients, Safe Near Food, Each Bottle Lasts Up to 30 Days, Pack of 1

items.title: Ingredients
items.text: Active Ingredients: Sodium Lauryl Sulfate Cas# 151-21-3 3.00% Malic Acid Cas# 6915-15-7 0.05% Other Ingredients;* 96.95% Total: 100.00% (*water Cas# 7732-18-5, Vinegar Cas# 8028-52-2, Ethyl Lactate Cas# 97-64-3, Citrus Pulp Cas# 68514-76-1)

features: Fast Acting & Long Lasting: Our formula is designed to bait & rid your home of fruit fly infestations fast; the powerful attractant lures fruit flies in as soon as the top is open; Lasts up to 30 days
No Mixing, No Mess: Just open the flip top on the safe, natural, indoor fruit fly trap & set where flies are either seen or feared to tackle the fruit fly population quickly; Not formulated for gnats
Fresh Fruits & Vegetables are Healthy, but Inevitably Attract Fruit Flies: FlyPunch! is perfect for fans of farmer's markets & CSAs, gardeners, & home brewers; Try near your compost or recycling bin
Safe Near Food, Pets, & Kids: Our fruit fly trap is made with plant based ingredients, free from harsh chemicals & is safe to be put out anywhere in the home, including the kitchen & food prep areas
Refills & Multi-packs Available: Replace as needed; Solve your fruit fly problem that was created by beer, wine, vegetables, fermentation, syrups, jams, spilled drinks, dirty sponges, & even pet food
