The functionality and effectiveness receive mixed reviews - while some say it works immediately and catches many flies, others report it doesn't work at all or catch any. Moreover, the product receives mixed feedback about its vinegar content, with some appreciating the apple cider vinegar and dish soap mixture while others consider it overpriced. Additionally, the smell and value for money are negative aspects, with customers describing the smell as terrible and considering it a waste of money.

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: Gets rid of fruit flies fast!
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: These work great.  This purchase is about my 4th of this product.  Over the Christmas period I made the mistake of leaving a bowl of apples in our dining room.  January 2nd I went back to trying to eat healthy and realized I had made a fruit fly infestation.  We were out over the Holiday's so I hadn't noticed them.  I tried a homemade fruit fly trap which didn't work.  Ordered these that I'd used before and after about two days all the flies had gone.  They do have a strong smell of apple cider vinegar but it's not unpleasant.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on January 14, 2026

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: The Best Natural, Pet-Safe & Effective Fruit Fly Trap
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I’ve been getting fruit flies in my bathroom that seem to come up from the drain for awhile now and I’ve made my own diy fruit fly traps and consistently poured white vinegar down the shower and sink drains, and it would get rid of a lot but not all of them and then eventually there would be a lot all over again. I started looking for a natural cat-safe and effective fruit fly trap and I decided on this one, and I’m glad I did. It’s been about a week and finally I have zero fruit flies! This is very effective and it’s very affordable too and lasts for about 30 days. I love how it has a lid, so that when I’m cleaning the bathroom cleaner, I can close the lid and put it on the floor while I clean. I even accidentally knocked it over while cleaning with the lid on, and nothing leaked out at all. It doesn’t have a strong vinegar smell after the first few days, and even before then it isn’t so strong, it just smells like red wine vinegar. It’s very easy to use, you just open the lid and remove the seal and place it where you want it, and that’s it. Will definitely be repurchasing to prevent any future fruit flies, $7 for 30 days is a really good deal.

title: Raid Reusable Fly Trap Jar, 1 Count, Convenient, Hanging Flying Insect Jar with Food-Based Lure, No-Spray Fly Traps Outdoor Suitable Defense for Yards, Patios & Campsites
breadCrumbs: Patio, Lawn & Garden > Pest Control > Traps

items.title: Safety Information
items.text: Keep out of reach of children and pets

description: Raid Reusable Fly Trap Jar is equipped with a tested and proven food-based lure that attracts once water is added. The Custom designed entry entices flies to enter the trap. Once they enter, they are unable to escape and then drown. The trap is a sanitary, easy-to-use solution that works week after week. The Raid Reusable and Recyclable Fly Jar Trap is an easy and efficient way of controlling flies outdoors. To use simply remove the seal around the blue lid. Unscrew the blue lid and set it aside. Remove the lure packet from inside the trap. Open the lure packet and pour the contents into the trap. Pour 2 1/2 cups of water into the trap and replace the blue lid. Place the trap on a flat surface or attach a string to the top of the funnel to hang the trap. Ensure that the trap is used in an outdoor area where flies are a nuisance away from outdoor living spaces.
features: Attract, Trap & Kill: Raid Reusable Fly Trap Jar attracts and captures common flying insects with a food-based lure. It’s a reliable solution versus foggers and backyard sprays to help prevent flies and insects from gathering around patios or gardens

One-Way Entry Funnel Prevents Escape: Once lured inside our flying insect trap, insects are funneled through a specially designed top-entry system that makes it easy to get in, but impossible to get out of this hanging fly catcher

Simple Setup: Just add lure, water & hang or place. No complicated instructions or tools required. Just pour in the included attractant, add water and hang the trap using the built-in hook. Suitable for outdoor use in trees, bushes, or shepherd hooks

Reusable & Sanitary: When the fly trap jar is full, empty the contents, rinse, and refill. This heavy-duty trap is built for multiple uses, offering long-term protection without the mess. Just be sure to hang at least 10–20 feet from activity zones

Strong on Bugs, Easy on You: For over 65 years, Raid has been protecting homes from bugs. Backed by science, our easy-to-use sprays, baits, and traps are designed to give families peace of mind by keeping everyday life free from insect interruptions

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: This fruit fly trap works really well and blends in nicely indoors. The design is discreet and doesn’t look like a typical bug trap, which I really appreciate. The sticky panels are very effective and catch gnats and fruit flies quickly.The UV light is gentle—not overly bright—so it doesn’t disturb the room or become annoying at night. I also like that it comes with multiple devices and plenty of refill panels, which makes it a good value.Overall, it’s effective, subtle, and perfect for indoor use. I’d definitely recommend it if you’re dealing with gnats or fruit flies.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on December 28, 2025

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: This is very simple but effective. I was literally surprised at how many little fruit flies this trapped, besides I didn’t realize we had that many flying around. I’m very pleased with this. It’s perfect size, appearance, durability is just what we were looking for. There’s no smell, just a sticky paper placed in a box that has a purple light and the nasty, irritating insects get drawn in and are stuck thus die. The price point is perfect for a budget friendly house. In the photo I just put this up 1 day ago because the other one was full of fruit flies.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on November 26, 2025

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I’ve been struggling to get rid of plant/dirt gnats for months. Unfortunately, I bought a bag of contaminated, indoor potting soil and didn’t realize until it was too late that it was infested with gnats and their eggs. I have been battling to get rid of them for months now. They don’t respond to the sugar vinegar solutions since they’re not fruit flies I got this hoping the black light effect would attract more of them quickly to help get rid of them fast faster. It is odorless. It doesn’t make any noise. It’s super small and emits less light than a nightlight. The removable replaceable clear, sticky sheets are sticky on both sides. If you have a curious pet or small child you may want to put it up where they won’t get to it because it will definitely stick to their face or hands. I would hate to see this get stuck to an animals whiskers so bear that in mind if you have small curious, cats or dogs.The picture I attached is one of the sticky sheets that I removed when I was replacing it so it’s not in the device with the light but as you can see it captured so many gnats. It works great at night in a dark room so that that light can truly attract the gnats. This one was in the trap for about 2 1/2 weeks. As you can see, it’s working wonderfully.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on January 28, 2026

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: It works great for catching small gnats. No issues there. For flies, it seems to work best when the room is completely dark, but it still gets the job done. I was surprised that it even caught a few mosquitoes! The sticky refills are extremely adhesive, and swapping them out is quick and easy. The light is gentle and not at all bothersome when it’s on. Overall, very effective and easy to use.

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I got this Fly Trap delivered this morning and immediately plugged it in... within 30 minutes, there were 9 fruit flies that had gotten caught!  I have a lot of houseplants and I think the flies get in via the soil.  This seems to work better than an electric one that I have.  For the price, it's easy to set up and use, and really seems to be effective!

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: These LFSYS fruit fly traps work amazingly well! I noticed a big difference within just a couple of days. The plug-in UV light attracts gnats and fruit flies effectively, and the trap does the rest quietly and efficiently.I love that it’s plug-and-play — no messy liquids or strong odors. productPageReviewsFromOtherCountries.reviewTitle: No ayudo a resolver plaga de moscas de la fruta :/
productPageReviewsFromOtherCountries.reviewDescription: Lo compré para lidiar con una plaga de moscas de la fruta. Seguí todas las instrucciones al pie de la letra y le dí hasta semanas de oportunidad para ver si servía de algo, ya que las instrucciones decían que podía "tardar hasta una semana en funcionar" o algo por el estilo, pero jamás atrapó una sola mosca. A pesar de que tenía esperanzas de que funcionara, me dejó muy decepcionado. Ví que el producto tenía varias reseñas positivas, no sé si a mí me tocó un dispositivo defectuoso o qué. En fin, tendremos que buscar otras opciones para lidiar con la plaga. Personalmente, si te interesa comprarlo específicamente para moscas de la fruta, no lo recomiendo. Quizá para otras especies de mosca e insectos voladores sí funciona, pero no me consta.

productPageReviewsFromOtherCountries.reviewDescription: Unreal product. Only thing that keeps the fruit flies away

productPageReviewsFromOtherCountries.reviewTitle: Do Not Purchase Please - Thank You
productPageReviewsFromOtherCountries.reviewDescription: Green Strike - STRIKES OUT!!! This product does not do what it promised in the product description. It doesn't deliver the way that is says it will. NOT AT ALL! The so called "120 days supply" of the attractant that comes along with (see pic - the thimble sized contain with the red lid) it only lasts a few days - maybe, just maybe, a week (and that is only if you do not put as much attractant into the neon green traps as required. I don't know how they can say that it will last you for 120 days when you only receive literally two to three table spoons of attractant with each 13$ purchase. It didn't take long before all the fruit flies were hanging out on top of the trap like it was a summer get away. Yes there were 30 dead flies inside, but it sure didn't last as long or even 5% as long as promised. DON'T PLEASE DON'T WASTE YOUR MONEY on this one, trust me, it is a flimsy "quick fix" that has a nice price point but it really doesn't put much of a dent in the overall population. If it came with 120 days of attractant that might be a different story. I just used two ex-large garbage bags and draped them over my garbage bin, it stops the little buggers from getting in the bin and out of the bin. Yes it is a pain, as you cannot just push the pedal and have the lid swing open, instead you have to life the two layers and usually I accidentally grab the actual garbage bag inside the bin - but al least when I do this extra step (which is a pain) I don't see 20 or 30 fruit flies exiting my bin.

productPageReviewsFromOtherCountries.reviewTitle: It's exactly what it looks like, it does the trick if you understand the principle.
productPageReviewsFromOtherCountries.reviewDescription: My most satisfying fly trap. As for the 120 days of solution? :Shrug: I barely remember if it did work. But the way to catch flies is generally easy. You take advantage of their inelegant flying skill. Fly wobble around until they get close to the food they want and wobble down to get it. They're not laser focused. Which mean you can catch them like fish. Give them an  opening they can easily get into but only narrowly come out of and they'll be trapped.That's what I wanted and this delivered. For years now I've had these and I like it for a few reasons. It's got a simple design that does what I want. It's reusable. I can put in custom bait. And generally it gets the flies.For my money Apple Cider vinegar works 80% of the time. You put in a 1-1.5cm layer and as I said previously the flies go in but can't easily find their way out. They might make it eventually if not the the ACV. They drink it they drown in it and voila death of a fly. Until recently I haven't had an issue with these devices. I load up 'em in the fly season and forget out them out of season. The ad says "spill proof" and I don't know why. That's not something I'm especially looking for in a fly trap and more importantly it isn't. As another review noted there are multiple gaps in the top of the lid so there's multiple venues for flies to enter and equally multiple venues for fluid to escape when tipped. That said it's shape is pretty sturdy and it doesn't fall over unless I leave it on a folder and pick up the folder.My current issue with flies has to do with complex landlord things but my issue with the trap is that I need four of them for the two more rooms that have persistent gnats. The two rooms with the traps keep numbers down but the gnats keep coming and there's 4-6 gnats that escaped the bathroom whose pipes keep birthing them. And if I move the traps to those rooms it takes longer to kill them and  in the mean time the main rooms start to get more gnats. It's a weirdly never ending war. Love you landlord /s.It's a very efficient design but if you're money conscious it's something that you can easily replicate with a small deli cup (4-8oz). Just stab some holes in the lid. Put some apple cider vinegar in the bottom. ANd it does the same thing just not as pretty. But just as effective. Unfortunately I needed something with spouse appeal and these do the trick.

productPageReviewsFromOtherCountries.reviewTitle: Works well for my small kitchen
productPageReviewsFromOtherCountries.reviewDescription: I'm not sure what others are doing that they say this doesn't work.I was in the hospital for 2 weeks unexpectedly. The fruit I bought left on the kitchen counter as I thought I'd just be in emergency for a few hours, not 2 weeks.Needless to say the counter was a fruit fly covered mess once I got home.I ordered a couple of different products (this, sticky sheets & plug in gizmos). This worked the fastest. After 2 days 90% of the fruit flies are gone. Gizmos did little. The sheets caught some. This caught twice as much as the others combined.I'm not sure about the entire "works for 120 days" thing. After 2 days I emptied the containers and refilled with apple cider vinegar. I'm now waiting to see if it catches the rest.My only issue is that the extra attractant cost. Thus -1 star on the review.

productPageReviewsFromOtherCountries.reviewTitle: It caught fruit flies, but took it time and not all went in
productPageReviewsFromOtherCountries.reviewDescription: I left it in a place with many small fruit flies and put the liquid inside up to the mark as instructed.Not a single fly goes in, had to put a second UV light with electric zapper nearby.I would not recommend to buy this, given it does not work as advertised.Update 10/7/25 :Finally after some time do see dead fruit flies inside, although do not expect this to work instantly or even in the first couple of days.It does take its time and the liquid included is just enough for the two packs, the attractant still has some left, but not enough to fill any one of the packs.So unless you plan to keep the same liquid for 120 days with all the dead fruit flies, you will need to purchase a replacement liquid, that is why I took some stars out, given I do not think this will last for that long and remain effective.

title: Hot Shot Flying Insect Trap, Discreet, Hassle-Free Trap Attracts, Captures House and Fruit Flies, 24/7 Continuous Attraction, No Plug-in Or Electricity Needed, 6 Count

description: Show bugs who’s boss with Hot Shot® Flying Insect Trap. This innovative solution provides 24/7 continuous action to attract and capture fruit flies and house flies. productPageReviews.reviewDescription: Absolutely useless. The little tubs that hold the liquid are made of the thinnest of plastic, and every single tub was warped, causing it to sit lopsided in the hard case. I spilled some liquid on my hands, and honestly, it just smells like pure vinegar. I mean, maybe there’s some sort of pheromone or chemical in there but it’s not effective. Every summer, the flies are so bad in my area. I’ve had these tubs sitting on my windowsills for three days now. I placed them there because that’s where a lot of the flies gravitate to, but it hasn’t caught a single one. Save your money, and invest in a good flyswatter. I suggest a metal one. Oh, and stay away from those little zapping paddles because I tried that too. All it did was knock the fly out for literally five seconds. Hardly enough time to finish the job before they regain their senses and fly away.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on June 8, 2025

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: Flys were gone within 3 days.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on February 6, 2026

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I bought these traps to put in my kitchen and bathrooms.  In our kitchen, we tend to get fruit flies around our coffee machine.  In the bathroom, we get them around the sinks, as they come out of the overflow drains and like to hang around my potted plants.These come in a 6-pack, which includes 6 small plastic cases and 6 single-use solution tubs.To setup, you take on plastic case, and one solution tub.  Peel the sealed cover off of the solution tub, drop it into the case, and put the lid on the case.  From there, you just place it in the area where you notice flying insects.The solution looks unremarkable, visually, but smells slightly of vinegar.  After these had dried up, it looked somewhat goopy, but still smelled of vinegar.  Putting a bit of water into the solution tub quickly reconstitutes the solution.  Given this, I believe the solution is nothing more than vinegar, water, and soap, which is the recipe for a DIY fruit fly trap, but in a MUCH smaller scale.The first night I put these out, I caught a single fruit fly in the bathroom, which made me very hopeful that these were to solution to my problem.  Unfortunately, these seem to be absolutely ineffective, as that first fruit fly I caught has been THE ONLY fruit fly I’ve caught, even though you can see them flying around these things.  I’m not sure if the solution is too weak, too small amount, or just not an effective solution in the first place.Given that I’ve had these in place for months at this point, and have only caught one single fly, which I believe was a complete fluke, I have to come to the conclusion that these are a complete sham that do absolutely nothing, which the company probably knows, but still decides to cash in on people needing a real solution.Overall, these are a major disappointment, and I would not recommend these to anyone.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on August 9, 2025

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: Not at all effective. We have had an issue with tiny fruit flies. Needed an alternative to the plug-in for a length of time I was going away. After 2 weeks, undisturbed, not one insect collected. I had more in a small water cup-catch located in yet a different area, but zero in the trap. I returned the plug in, and  overnight had at least 10 on the sticky paper catch.  Don’t bother with this product.

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: There is always that one fruit fly...
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: Monty Python has a cautionary tale about Basil, that one fruit fly who reproduces before he dies. I've been dealing with Basil and his kin for months. After researching my options, I've tried a country recipe, the plug-ins with the sticky cards, and the red containers with holes on top that hold these liquid refills. Each has strengths and weaknesses in the fight against this mighty foe.I put the container in my sink near the sink drain since that's where the flies tend to be. These refills attract the flies with a low amount of odor. Even with a clean drain, I still have flies. The trap didn't catch as many flies when I tried to lure them away from the sink. Cleaning out the bug filled solution is gross, but it's manageable. This six-bottle pack is convenient. Half of a bottle about once a week is all that's needed.I've had a real infestation over the past several months, but with patience and realistic expectations, I'm pleased with the progress so far. Based on how they reproduce, it seems hard to kill them all. But with the help of these refills, Python would be proud, I'm down to a few stray Basils. If I stay the course, I do have hope that I can use brains over brawn and win the war. These refills seem to be a good step in the process. I recommend them for anyone who is looking for a, effective low cost-per-use alternative to name brands. Given that I can get twelve doses from a box, these are a great value for the money. After watching the solution collect flies for a few days, it exceeded my expectations in how may flies it has caught; I had not realized there were still that many. Exceeding my expectations earns these Fruit Fly Trap Refill Replacement Six Packs 5 Stars in my book.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on January 18, 2025

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: The Fruit Fly Trap Refill has been working very good so far. I've only been using the liquid for about to weeks and so far have caught more that a dozen fruit flies. So far I have been very happy with the results and the price.

aPlusContent.title: Product description Effective Capture
aPlusContent.rawText:
Product description
The video showcases the product in use.The video guides you through product setup.The video compares multiple products.The video shows the product being unpacked.
FLYING INSECT TRAP
Merchant Video
Effective Capture 
Fly cather for moths, flies, fruit flies, mosquitoes, gnats and other flying insects 
1.Uncover Refill Release Paper 
2.Insert Refill into Cartridge 
3.Insert Cartridge into Device 
4.Plug in Device (110V-220V) 
What is the OSVDO Flying Insect Trap? 
The OSVDO Flying Insect Trap is a device designed to attract, trap, and eliminate flying insects such as mosquitoes, flies, moths, and gnats. It typically uses a combination of UV light (to attract insects) and sticky glue board (to capture them) 
What type of flying insects does it catch? 
The OSVDO Flying Insect Trap is designed to catch fruit flies, gnats, house flies, drain flies, fungus gnats, & non-biting midges. 
Can I use the OSVDO Flying Insect Trap in spaces with kids, pets and/or near food? 
Yes! OSVDO device base meets third-party electrical safety standards. It is a durable, plug-in base requiring 120V/60Hz power and a standard North American plug. As with other electrical devices, keep away from children. 
How often should I change the cartridge? 
Replace the refill cartridge when it is full of insects. We recommend checking it every 2 weeks or as needed. 
Is there anywhere you should NOT keep your trap plugged in? 
Keep OSVDO trap away from furniture, curtains, or corners that could block insects. If you can't see OSVDO, insects can't see it either.

The product receives positive feedback for its quality, with customers noting it's better than alternatives like Zevo, and they like that it comes with extra sticky sheets. However, opinions are mixed regarding its effectiveness: while some say it catches a lot of bugs, others report it catches nothing. Moreover, customers disagree on the light level, with some finding it bright enough while others say it lacks brightness. The value for money is also debated, with some finding it good for the price while others consider it not worth the cost.

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: Not an instant solution like sprays but far safer for pets and they do work given a couple days.
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: We found one of my kids had left a food bag with some scraps that had basically rotted and now we had a huge infestation of those little gnats, fruit flies, or similar little bugs around. we have 2 cats and dog and I'm hesitant to use sprays, even those that say they're "pet friendly".  I decided to give the a shot and although the results haven't been instantaneous, after a couple of days, there has been a marked decline in the number of flying bugs.I went with this particular product because it was a 2 pack with a bunch of refill sticky pads. I set one up in two different rooms where the infestation was the worst.  They could not be any easier to use. you peel the plastic covering off the sticky pads and insert them in devices and plug them in.  They have blue light that attracts the bugs which then get caught on the sticky pads.They do seem to work best when it gets dark and there is no sunlight through the windows and lights in the room are off to compete with the devices light for the bugs attention.I would definitely purchase these again.

The brightness receives mixed reactions, with some appreciating it as a night light while others find it annoying.

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: Great product at a lower price.
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: Picture is 8 days of a new trap, before I treated exterior of the house.Works great at a much lower price. I was skeptical but wanted to try it out. Seems many people have a gnat/fruit fly invasion this summer so I have several traps. Zevo works but so expensive so wanted to try this lower priced brand. Works great, first night catching 30+ and within a few days having to change it out. Finally treated exterior of the house and most are now gone, but still catching stragglers.I did notice after about a 10-14 days, dust particles seem to weaken the glue, allowing larger insects to slowly crawl to the edge and fly away...but this doesn't happen at the beginning of a new trap.

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: Living in Georgia is a retiree's dream but with that said the downside are mosquitos and other pesky insects that enter the home and become a nuisance.  Insect intrusion was the reason we purchased the INRAGEO Flying Insect Trap with 5 Cartridges. We thought this insect trap would eliminate the problem and it did to a certain  point but it is not 100% effective. The design of the unit is excellent, there is no noise, the cartridges are easy to replace and plugging it into a wall outlet makes it easy to activate. However, not all insects are attracted to the device as our "test for effectivity" showed; based on the number of insects captured during the first week and those who flew by gave us a figure of approximately, 75%.  We recommend the device but with the caveat that it will capture most of the insects but not 100%.

    I’m so happy with these fruit flies! I’m going on vacation on the 23rd, and I needed live flies for my salamander that arrived quick. Luckily, I found these! They had 2.6 stars, but they were my last hope, so I bought them. And, boy, was I surprised! Two containers of fruit flies came, a bigger species and a smaller species. In addition to this, there was a heat pack in the box to keep the flies warm and alive! Both of these were very thoughtful, and the box came in perfect condition, despite past reviews. I thought I was done for, but these fruit flies are amazing! They are also one of the only LIVE feeders I could find! To sum it up, these fruit flies came in two different varieties, came in perfect condition with a heat pack, arrive quick, arrive alive, and are easy to feed to your pets! I am definitely going to buy them again!

aPlusContent.title: From the manufacturer
aPlusContent.rawText:
From the manufacturer
PIC Fly Ribbon Traps, 10 Pack 
The PIC Fly Ribbon presents an effective remedy for capturing pesky flying insects. This Fly Ribbon efficiently traps and holds flies and other flying pests, making it suitable for both indoor and outdoor use. This trap is formulated without any harmful chemicals and features a fragrance-free design. Its ease of use is remarkable: simply warm the tube in cold conditions for effortless ribbon removal. After removing the provided thumbtack, grasp the red loop and twirl down the tube to reveal the ribbon. Hang it using the thumbtack in areas plagued by flies, such as kitchens, barns, stables, patios, and decks, or near trash bins. 
Hanging Fly Solution 
Traps Come With 10 Fly Ribbon Traps 
Efficiently Traps And Holds Flies And Other Flying Pests 
Suitable For Both Indoor And Outdoor Environments

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: Absolutely thrilled with this fly paper! It does exactly what it promises—quietly, efficiently, and without any chemicals or hassle. Once it’s up, it gets to work right away, trapping flies and keeping the space noticeably calmer and cleaner. I love how low-maintenance it is—no batteries, no sprays, no mess. It’s simple, effective, and surprisingly satisfying to see it do its thing. If you’re dealing with flies and want an easy, no-nonsense solution, this fly paper is a total win.

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: Be careful not to let the fully-extended sticky ribbon touch anything, including your hands, or else you'll need to scrub the mess off with a good paper towel and water.  It is SUPER STICKY and serves the purpose of attracting and catching flies...once they land on the strip, they are stuck and soon die.  Not overly malodorous, so, no problem there, but some flies take longer than others to land on it.  Nine hanging strips hung throughout my apartment -- far out of reach of family pets -- successfully got rid of my early summer fly infestation, though, it took several weeks (after new flies hatched).
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on September 19, 2023

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: Please fly strips are heaven sent as far as I'm concerned. I live out in the country. there are certain times when these flys are so crazy out here .of course you can't stop them.But there are annoying and bothersome ,they're always around when you have food and you don't want that you don't want them landed on your food ,all that stuff going on. It's just not healthy.I know they might look unattractive hanging up all over the place but at least when the flies land on them you don't have to worry about the flies anymore I mean just take it after it's been up there about a month or maybe 3 months and dispose it in the trash and keep your doors closed you don't have to worry about them being all over the place in your face. People in the city don't have to worry about all that so much but people out in the country it's different.I was raised up in the city and I don't remember ever having a problem with flies when I was growing up in my house with my mom and dad but out here in the country it happens but I love living in the country great open and free and I don't have to worry about having my doors locked or anything like that and sometimes or after cooking flies come or when you got fruit in the house or when the kids leave the door and door open and they don't know how to shut it. It's crazy things like that that make you go stir crazy I can't stand flies please bugs nothing I don't like them but this is one of my best friends right here this fly strip it's coming and it works it may not be pretty but it does the job. There is no smell to it, it's versatile you can hang them up anywhere you want to your bedroom the bathroom the kitchen the living room. And as far as the size goes they just hang up in the ceiling and drape down you have to uncoil it and make sure it's all the way to the end it's probably maybe about maybe 2 ft long. they last as long as you want to keep them up there you can hang them up there a month you can hang them up there 6 months it doesn't matter. And there is no size they are all are the same length so you're good to go. And they got some kind of adhesive on there that makes them really sticky so the stickiness is really good and it doesn't matter if it's cold or it's hot outside or inside that still works those flies land on there and they do not come off.I have to buy a big pack of them every year and I replace them about three times a year. And I will continue to keep coming back for more thank you all so much for reading my review because I'm telling you the honest truth they are awesome
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on July 1, 2024

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: They attract the flies. But they don't trap the flies!Came into the kitchen this morning, only to discover an infestation of fruit flies. Quicklt tied up the trash bag, and went to go grab the vacuum. Then remembered we had these traps on hand. Ripped a couple of these bad boys open. And almost immediately watched about a dozen flies land on on the sticky ribbon. Perfection! Left the room thinking I'd just saved the day. Only to retuen 15 minutes later, and find all but three of the flies had gone back over to the garbage can. What! Took a closer look, and watched as a couple of flies landed on the goopy, sticky paper. Walk around, then casually take back off.Went and got the vacuum...

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: It Works, but There Are Much Better Fly Paper/Fly Ribbon Products Available Here on Amazon! I Write This from Experience!
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: 3 Stars. It works, but it is not the best fly paper/ fly strip / fly ribbon, nor is it the worst.I bought three sets of these from Amazon over the past 18 months, and finally, a few months ago, I got tired of their rather lack-luster performance, and sat down and did some research on Amazon, and ultimately located a different brand of fly paper product that had a much higher star rating in the reviews (researching this is a tricky matter, because many of these Chinese products on Amazon have a lot of fake reviews as well, and it is important to first filter out the fake reviews prior to looking at the star rating, and prior to reading any reviews...).Anyway, as a result of my research here on Amazon, I finally found a different fly paper product, named "Sticky Fly Ribbons,Fly Catcher Ribbon,Fly Paper Ribbon,Fly Paper Strips,Super Value 16 Packs by JCHope", and that product works about twice as well as this product, because the adhesive is stronger and more uniformly applied, and because the adhesive has an odor that attracts house flies more quickly. So, I have, in the end, switched to the JCHope fly paper product.

aPlusContent.title: Product description
aPlusContent.rawText:
Product description
Whether indoors or outdoors, keep bugs at bay by adding a lure to the bottom cup, and let our trap do the rest! Once the fly, mosquito, gnat, wasp, or any other insect falls for the bait, the super sticky fly ribbon locks them in for their inevitable demise. 
HIGH QUALITY While our traps aren’t kind to insects, they are on your team against flies! As a small, Veteran-owned company, we specialize in creating the best flying insect traps possible. Our fly stick traps are constructed with recycled paper with an ultra-sticky cylindrical trap. With a sleek design, flat bottom, and a built-in hook for hanging, the traps have been thoughtfully designed for convenient portability and placement anywhere you go where bugs might be a nuisance! 
JUST ADD BAIT Our traps require no assembly and arrive ready to get down to business. Simply add an attractant, such as honey or syrup, to the bottom cup, and watch pests take the bait for their eventual extermination. Available in a 2 pack, 4 pack or jumbo pack of 8. 
PROTECT YOUR HOUSE 
Whether it’s biting mosquitoes, bacteria-ridden flies, or other buzzing bugs, irritating insects are one of the last things you want in your home. Place our trap in problem areas such as by the garbage container, near windows, or around houseplants to ward off unwanted houseguests. 
THE GREAT OUTDOORS 
Much of life is spent outside, unfortunately, these times are often shared with pesky bugs. Our fly stick traps are ideal for placing on the porch or patio when enjoying company, around picnics and campsites, or inside barns, kennels, and stables. Bring the fly stick trap with you on the go so you can quit zapping and bug-slapping for a more peaceful and enjoyable experience

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: Excellent!! I have had problems with fruit flies no matter how clean I kept my Kitchen sink.  Even bleach did not deter them . aPlusContent.rawText:
Product description
EFFECTIVE & EASY 
At last! A plant-powered solution to get rid of fruit flies** is here! This targeted, self-contained product is your easy tool to keep these annoying bugs away from the fun. 
Simply place the trap near the source of fruit flies - such as bowls of fruit, trash cans, kitchen sinks - twist off lid, remove seal, replace cap, press to open, and let the trap do the rest! 
**Will not work with other types of flies, including phorid flies and fungus gnats. 
PLANT-POWERED 
Since this fruit fly catcher is powered by plants, it's safe* to place around food, pets, and kids and throughout the house, yet powerful enough to fully eliminate fruit flies and provide lasting defense of your household. 
Lasts up to 21 days. 
*Safe when used as directed. 
Ingredients: Active Ingredient: Sodium lauryl sulfate (derived from plants) 
Inert ingredients: Water, vinegar, syrups, hydrolyzed starch, hydrogenated, malic acid, yeast, ethyl lactate. 
360 PROTECTION 
Fruit Fly Trap Home + Kitchen is part of the Wondercide 360° Protection System and helps families stay bug-free all year long. 
Use Wondercide for your 360° pest protection inside and outside of your home. Use our Outdoor Pest Control spray to kill and repel insects at the source, throughout your yard and outdoor areas. Use the Indoor Pest Control for barrier protection and lasting repellency throughout the home. Use our new Ant & Roach aerosol spray for an additional line of defense!

items.text:
When you love someone, you do everything in your power to protect them. 
Stephanie thought she was keeping her dog Luna safe, but then Luna became ill. Her vet suspected conventional flea and tick medication and monthly pest control services. 
She knew there had to be a better way, so she founded Wondercide to protect pets, families, and homes with plant-powered solutions that actually work.

Today, Wondercide is trusted by over 20 million families. 
Wondercide gives you peace of mind in the backyard and beyond. 
Starting with one woman’s mission to save her dog, we exist to empower you to Protect Your Pack.

You shouldn't have to choose between effective protection and protection you feel good about. 
Lab proven and love driven, Wondercide means you don’t have to settle when it comes to protecting your pack. We focus on plant powered solutions that work.

We proudly manufacture Wondercide products in the USA. 
From our pack to yours, Wondercide is proudly made with love in Austin, TX—manufactured with pride, purpose, and plenty of tail wags.

aiReviewsSummary.text: Customers find the fruit fly trap straightforward to use and easy to install. However, the functionality receives mixed feedback - while some say it works wonders, others report it doesn't work at all. Similarly, the effectiveness against fruit flies is mixed, with some customers reporting no more flies while others say it doesn't attract them. Moreover, the product quality, smell, and value for money receive negative feedback, with customers describing it as terrible, stinky, and not worth the money. Additionally, customers express concerns about the lid safety, noting that flies don't enter the holes, and the vinegar content, which is just apple cider vinegar.

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: Takes a few days, but then 100% super effective!
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: Takes a little patience and understanding, but wonderful. At first it seems anti-helpful. Since it attracts fruit flies to be trapped, for a while you will see MORE fruit flies. I'd say essentially no flies after maybe three days, or just an occasional fly. Take the top off and look inside, you see nothing, but look inside with a FLASHLIGHT and you see thousands of dead ones on the bottom. My guess is that it takes a few days to fully interrupt the fly breeding cycle.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on December 9, 2025

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: This thing doesn’t trap or kill the flies. It just attracts them to hang out around it from time to time. Not a single fly went inside to drown and you can make a better one yourself with apple cider vinegar and dish soap. See the pictures for proof. The flies just happily chill and enjoy themselves around this trap without actually flying in.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on January 15, 2026

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: Update: Not working for us - The product DOES work!!!
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: Update:  I guess I was premature in my review.Today I woke up to NO flies. All were inside the Wondercide bottles. I am SO Happy!  Changing from two stars to five stars!This product has captured two flies in 5 days. They land on the product but they dont go in.I guess I have professional flies. We still have about a thousand more to go.  I am having more success with a bowl of dawn, ACV, and a piece of apple. I gave two stars for two flies.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on November 30, 2024

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: Inside product didn’t work for us at all. Had 2 placed around house. Used the sticky yellow traps and those caught more gnats than anything but non were attracted to this. Maybe outside on patio would be different.

items.text: TERRO T518 Fly Magnet Sticky Fly Paper Fly Trap, 8 Count (Pack of 1)

description: Keep your home and outdoor spaces fly-free with the TERRO Fly Ribbon 8 Pack, ideal for fly control. These fly traps indoor for home offer a simple, mess-free solution to annoying flying insects. Each ribbon is designed to attract and trap flies without the need for bait, odor, or harmful chemicals, making them safer home indoor fly trap options around children and pets. The ribbon design is effortless to set up—just pull it out and hang it where flies are a problem. Once the ribbon is full, simply dispose of it—no mess, no fuss. This convenient pack includes 8 fly paper strips, providing lasting protection across multiple areas like kitchens, porches, barns, and more. Say goodbye to flies the easy way with TERRO’s trusted fly-catching solution. Use as fly tape indoors and as a fly catcher indoors to keep spaces fly-free. Experience the TERRO fly magnet action with the best indoor fly trap, a reliable house fly trap solution.
features: INCLUDES 8 FLY PAPERS - These sticky fly traps for indoors can be spread around your home
CONVENIENTLY DISPOSABLE - Indoor fly trap for home

FLY MAGNET STICKY FLY PAPER - No baiting, no odor, no mess

aPlusContent.title: From the manufacturer
aPlusContent.rawText:
From the manufacturer
TERRO Fly Magnet Sticky Fly Paper Trap 
TERRO Fly Magnet fly paper traps use a sticky ribbon to attract and trap flies and other airborne insects. Fly Magnets come in cardboard tubes that, with a twisting pull, expose a sticky surface to trap flies. Each tube includes a hanging ribbon and thumbtack for easy installation inside barns, at campsites, on porches, in kennels and at vacation homes. This fly paper requires no baiting, has no stinky odor and won’t create a mess. When the trap is full, drop it into a bag and throw it in the trash. 
How to Pull Out Fly Paper 
To activate your Fly Magnet, grasp the red ribbon in one hand and the tube in the other. Then pull on it while twisting the base. Only activate the Fly Magnet when it is at room temperature. 
How to Hang Fly Paper 
Hang your fly paper with the included tack or by looping the red ribbon around a nail. Avoid hanging in a windy area. For best results, hang in the corner of a room.

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: Cheap and effective, if you have a place to hang it.
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: It's a strip of glue. If you've never used such a thing before, then this seems like an ineffective way to catch flies, but if you leave it alone for any period of time, you'll be surprised by what you find stuck to it when you come back. I don't know if it's "sufficiently" sticky, but I have multiple traps practically encrusted with flies so whatever it is, it is sticky enough. The big problem is that there are places I have an infestation but no place to attach it to; light fixtures seem best since flies like the light and will happily fly right into the trap but if you have no such place then you'll need alternate solutions.I'd take a half star away from the review if I could for that particular issue, as well as the fact that, well, it makes no bones about the fact it's basically a length of packing tape flapping in the wind and you'll be greeted with a strip of fly corpses whenever you enter a room which might be a little off-putting to say the least.  I'd probably also not use this for anything save a plague of flies, the stray fly that might find in an otherwise bug-free home probably should be dealt with in a more manual method.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on August 2, 2025

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: The Sticky Fly Trap Indoor Hanging is a simple and reliable way to reduce annoying flies and small flying insects inside your home. Just hang it where you see activity — near windows, doors, or kitchens — and the sticky surface does the rest. No sprays, poisons, or complicated setup required.It catches flies quickly and silently, and the hanging design keeps it out of the way yet visible where it’s needed most. The traps are lightweight and easy to replace when full.Pros: Easy to use, chemical‑free, effective on common house fliesCons: Needs replacement over time, works best as part of regular pest controlBottom Line: A convenient and affordable indoor fly trap that helps keep flying insects under control without fuss — ideal for kitchens, patios, and living spaces.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on February 13, 2026

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: Long story short, this solved our gnat problem but I have no idea how. I don't know if anything got stuck to the tape but the bugs are gone. Since it worked, I won't question it.So we had these annoying gnats/fruit fly like bugs that were only in our bedroom for the most part. There was only like 1 or 2 per day but they were those ones that drives you nuts by flying right in your face and you can never kill them, so we bought these.They opened fairly easily without much force or the string or anything breaking randomly. I took the time to unravel it and tried pulling it tight and holding it like that for a bit, but as soon as I let go then it just wound right back up. I figured it would unravel on its own after hanging it up, so it wasn't a big deal. (The pic with more light shows how it was out of the box the first night)We put it into our ceiling above our computer where those things managed to evoke the most rage in us and went to bed. The very next morning we didn't see any flies. I didn't notice any stuck on the tape either though.Its now been 4 days since putting the fly paper up and we have not seen a single one of those gnats anywhere in my home. I *think* one may be stuck to the paper, but not completely sure, it could be dust too. I really don't understand it. We had these flies for 3 weeks and no severe change in weather or anything else, so I know it was these papers. They're the only change we made.Also side note, it ended up unraveling a little bit after we turned on our humidifier last night, but otherwise it still hasnt really straightened out yet.(You can see in the 2nd pic, the darker one where more of the window is in the shot) Its only been up for 4 days and I just measured it to be about 2 feet, even with it still curled a bit. I saw some other reviewers not like that it didnt unravel completely. I don't mind since it's not like we need the extra tape space and I'd probably manage to hit my head off it somehow if it was longer, which I'm definitely okay with never doing (again).Ultimately we bought this to get rid of our gnat problem, which it did. Even if it wasn't in the expected way. So I'm competely happy with the purchase. And it does seem sticky enough that if they land on it they would get stuck to it. Also getting 8 of them for the price is worth it as well.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on April 8, 2023

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: The only one's you'll ever need!
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: These really do work! And I mean nothing works better! The others are just sticky traps! That's it! These have a science behind them, that really attracts the bugs! It's going to shock you how many bugs this will catch so quickly! Others takes months! The quality is also unmatched. I've tried many many different ones before!Edited: After reading other reviews after writing this one, I realized one thing! It probably works very well if it's hung up right up under a ceiling light! Because that's exactly where all of mine are hanging! Bugs are attracted to the light! Hang right up under your ceiling light! Use the screws already on the ceiling light if you can't use the tack! Just hang it via the screw part hanging out of the light cover! Or remove the cover, and hang from the screws inside!Idk, but the trick has worked well in every single room!!! And someone said these aren't baited, yes they are! If you have used their ant baits, you know!!!

productPageReviewsFromOtherCountries.reviewDescription: This fly paper is a simple and effective way to get rid of annoying flies. It has no smell, no bait, and no mess. It comes in a cardboard tube that you can easily pull and hang anywhere. The sticky surface catches flies and other insects quickly and securely. It is a great product for indoor and outdoor use.
productPageReviewsFromOtherCountries.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United Arab Emirates on February 6, 2024

productPageReviewsFromOtherCountries.reviewDescription: I have a few of these in my large garden courtyard, which we have fenced off for 2 puppies to play (until they can be trusted in the yard not to hurt themselves or destroy the reticulation and the garden).  These hang out of reach over their 2 potty areas and do a great job.Just remember to be very careful not to let your pups get at them. We had a strong wind and one blew down. While they’re not toxic they are a strip of gunk covered fabric. Luckily our dog finally vomitted it up so we discovered what had happened to it and it didn’t get stuck in his intestines.The other day it was extremely windy and one must have blown off and was eaten by one of the puppies. It appeared in vomit a day later and in diarrhoea over a couple of days. Looked it up and thank goodness no insecticides are used and the substance isn’t toxic. I now take that one down when it’s very windy!

items.title: Safety Information
items.text: PRECAUTIONARY STATEMENTS AND INSTRUCTIONS PERTAINING TO THE RISK OF FIRE, ELECTRIC SHOCK, OR INJURY TO PERSONS: When using electrical appliances, basic precautions should always be followed, including the following: Please read before using and save these instructions. To reduce the risk of injury, close supervision is necessary when product is used near children. Product must be handled/applied by an adult. Do not put fingers or hands into the product. For safe use, plug only into properly functioning 120-volt wall outlets where device is ventilated. If using a GFCI outlet, test on a regular basis. Do not use with extension cords, multi-plugs, or 3-prong adapters. Do not immerse in water. Do not use outdoors. Use only the product recommended glue traps in this device. For indoor use only. KEEP OUT OF REACH OF CHILDREN AND PETS.

description: Control ﬂying insects the easy, mess-free way with STEM Flying Insect Fan and Light Trap. This portable fan trap attracts and traps ﬂying insects like house ﬂies, fruit ﬂies and mosquitoes. Hide away unsightly ﬂy swatters and let this device handle pests for you – no touching bugs or sticky sprays. The fan operates quietly in the background and works with the UV light to lure and capture pests. Use the customizable touchscreen display to select your preferred fan speed and run timer – 3, 8, or 12 hours or 24/7. Designed to be people and pet-friendly when used as directed, STEM Fan Trap is perfect for use in high-traﬃc spaces like entryways, kitchens, living rooms and more. An included six-foot cord helps extend the portability of the device so you can use it where you need pest control the most. Stock up on fan trap reﬁlls to have mess-free pest control on hand year-round. When the glue trap is full, simply throw it away and replace with a fresh reﬁll pad.
features: Dual Attraction Technology: The fan and blue light work together in this portable bug trap to attract and trap mosquitoes, fruit ﬂies and house ﬂies
Powerful Fly & Mosquito Trap: Perfect for areas of your home where insects are present, this bug catcher quietly controls pests so you can enjoy a peaceful space
Customizable Settings: Use the touch display to select your ideal fan speed and set a timer for 3, 8, or 12 hours, or opt for continuous 24/7 operation
Indoor Pest Control: Includes 5 mess-free glue traps and a 6-foot cord, oﬀering portability for easy setup in your home or enclosed patio
How It Works: Flying insects are attracted by the discreet UV light, while the quiet fan pulls them into the device where pests are captured on the sticky glue trap; Replace glue card when full
People & Pet Friendly: This odorless ﬂy trap is crafted without chemical insecticides, making it family- and pet-friendly when used as directed

aPlusContent.title: From the manufacturer
aPlusContent.rawText:
From the manufacturer
See How STEM for Bugs Protects Your Home from Flying Insects, Quietly and Effectively 
Invisible to you, attractive to bugs. Our insect trap’s discreet UV light attracts flying insects, helping keep bugs out of your space without disrupting your ambiance. 
No more loud zapping sounds! This plug-in fly trap device features a quiet fan that gently pulls insects into the trap, providing effective pest control without disrupting your calm. 
Capture pests without chemical insecticides. Our sticky traps effectively trap and hold insects, providing a chemical-free solution. 
Effortless, Uninterrupted Protection 
How far can the bug trap reach? 
The plug in bug catcher has a generous 6ft cord that provides ultimate flexibility, allowing you to easily move the device from room to room as needed. 
Does its fan create noticeable noise? 
Find the perfect setting with our adjustable, whisper-quiet fan. Choose from two speeds on the bug trap for the needed space. 
How long does the fan trap run? 
Customize your indoor bug trap control with flexible timer options. Choose from 3, 8, or 12 hours, or set it to 24/7 for continuous protection. 
How do I clean STEM fan trap? 
STEM Fan Trap contains sticky pad that trap bugs. When it’s full, toss it in the trash and insert a new fan trap refill pad. It’s easy, and, best of all, mess-free.

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: Works well at catching fungus gnats
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I like this product for catching fungus gnats that hitched a ride inside after I brought my outdoor plants inside for the winter.It seems to do well attracting the adults, while I work on stopping the source they're originating from.It's super quiet. I have to put my hand over it to feel if it's working sometimes. There's an indication light, but I don't always trust stuff and like to check anyway.I have it plugged in and running 24-7, but there is an option for a timer. It's a simple touch screen with lights and pictures as buttons.It seems to catch bugs easily. I also have the plug in type that works well. I think they do about the same job attracting and trapping critters.It came with a refill pack of six discs of the sticky cardboard you place inside, that catches the bugs. It's nice not having to immediately buy replacement parts.It's powered by a USB style plug in cord which is included.It looks decent in a room. It's nice that you can't see the bugs on the sticky pad inside.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on November 30, 2025

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: Pros:- Works really well for light-focused bugs- Much better than other UV fly traps I've tried that don't have a fan. Worth the extra costCons:- Not going to work well for bugs that are more scent-focused (like fruit flies or large house flies)- Safety hazard if it is placed where children can look at the light- The slow-speed mode for the fan causes the device to emit a high-pitched coil whine noise. Not a big deal since the fan isn't very loud in high-speed mode- Doesn't automatically turn back on after losing/regaining power
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on September 5, 2025

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: I'd give it 5 stars if stinkbugs didn't get stuck in funnel
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: Works awesome! Catches a lot of bugs. Including stinkbugs. But those big stupid idiots get stuck in the bottom of the funnel and block it so you have to poke them through or shake them out.I'm very glad it catches them. I might just try to Dremel the hole larger.It makes a noticeable difference in how many bugs are bumbling around the house.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on December 3, 2025

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: Didn't catch anything. Maybe they bugs were scared off by the noise?
productPageReviews.reviewDescription:
I was ordering some
  
B0DSQSMG1W STEM Flying Insect Light Trap Refill Cartridges







  
  
    , which were great BTW, when I noticed this fan trap. I figured their glue trap cartridges worked so well that this must be good, too.Firstly, this thing is very noisy. Not brass band noisy, but noisy enough to be heard from the other side of a large room. It's really disturbing.Even after we decided to tolerate the noise by running it while we weren't home, it didn't catch anything.The glue sheets caught a dozen gnats each (my wife likes house plants, and these bugs seem to be unavoidable), while this thing didn't catch a thing.My recommendation is to start with a Raid Essentials Flying Insect Light Trap Starter Kit and, when you need them, get the STEM refills that I mentioned.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on February 11, 2026

productPageReviews.reviewDescription: These fan assisted flying insect traps may have saved my life this past summer!  These traps are only a few inches in diameter and can fit on the smallest side table.The summer of '25 was the worst summer for mosquitoes in decades.  I was eaten alive in June, I (personally) used four entire spray cans of non-DEET mosquito repellent in July and wore fishing hats with mosquito netting, long sleeve shirts and long pants in our antique house, and was still bitten all over, every day!I bought these insect trap in early August.  These traps don't just work, they work perfectly-- and it has ZERO ODOR!!!  I was only bitten a handful times all month!It did my heart good to look inside after only a week and see more than a dozen little insects stuck to the bottom!  Those little dead monsters saved me from being bitten more than 60 times!  I threw the little sticky disk out after two weeks of constant use.  Info about where to buy more sticky pads would be nice.One little bone to pick is that the instructions were unclear about how to turn the unit on 24/7.  All one needs to do is plug it in and tap the power button.  Don't set a timer and it will just keep on keepin' on!  But DO push the fan button because it seems to suck the little insects in and keep them in better.  The UV light is eye-catching at first, but it's easy to get used to ignore once one is used to seeing it in the background.All things considered, this is my single best anti-mosquito purchase of this decade!  I cannot recommend this any more highly.  It's perfect!
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on October 9, 2025

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: Works, but was not as effective as Zevo
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: PROS:  The fan is relatively quiet, easy to set up and use, and straightforward adhesive disk replacement. It works reasonably well in trapping little critters.CONS:  I bought this along with a Zevo flying insect trap, installed both in the same room a few feet apart for comparison.  While they both work, the Zevo unit is trapping far more insects, at least twice as many.  And that’s without having any moving parts.  I also find the Zevo unit to be easier for my old, arthritic hands to change out the trap refills.
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on July 28, 2025

productPageReviews.reviewTitle: It does work super well regardless of some ratings
productPageReviews.reviewDescription: I read the reviews of this product and viewed the photos posted. One stated after days they haven’t captured a single gnat or fruit fly. As soon as I received it I activated it by placing a sticky disc as instructed. You have to peel off an invisible film protecting the sticky side. The unit, with no exaggeration became populated with approximately 300 gnats…that makes me think she didn’t remove that membrane…granted no instructions come with it, but each disc has a “peel here” written small along a side of the edge. Great product if you have the ability to figure out these things, instructions or not.I love it and no longer have a houseful of irritation flying up my nose…
productPageReviews.reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on December 7, 2025

Easy-to-Use Indoor Gnat Trap:Simply uncap the bait bottle, pour the liquid into your fruit fly trap, and position it in fly-prone areas. Fruit flies enter through the top opening, lured by the scent.
